headline: French Broad Chocolate Recalls Bette's Bake Sale Bonbon Collection for Undeclared Tree Nuts

image.caption: French Broad Chocolates PBC Recalls Bette's Bake Sale Bonbons Due to Undeclared Walnuts

description: French Broad Chocolate is recalling select Bette's Bake Sale Bonbon Collection boxes due to undeclared walnuts, a tree nut allergen. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
French Broad Chocolate Factory has issued a recall for Bette's Bake Sale Bonbon Collection in 6pc (2.5oz), 12pc (5oz), and 24pc (10oz) boxes due to undeclared walnuts, a tree nut allergen. According to the FDA notice, the recall involves batch numbers 260414 and 260417, distributed between April 14 and April 20, 2026, through retail stores in Asheville, NC, and shipped online to 41 states.

The recall was initiated after a labeling error on the tasting notes insert, which failed to declare walnuts in the Walnut Fudge bonbons. This poses a risk of serious allergic reactions for individuals with walnut allergies. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the recall date.

headline: Recall: Frederik's by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix Due to Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens

image.caption: Ferris Coffee & Nut Co. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat and Soy in Frederik's By Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix

description: Ferris Coffee & Nut Co. recalled Frederik's by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix (9 oz) for undeclared wheat and soy. No illnesses reported. FDA notice April 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Ferris Coffee & Nut Co. of Grand Rapids, Michigan is recalling one lot of Frederik's by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix (9 oz) due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens. Individuals with allergies or sensitivities to wheat or soy risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The recall affects product sold in Meijer stores in Michigan, Indiana, Ohio, Illinois, Wisconsin, and Kentucky. The issue was caused by a production error in which chocolate-covered pretzel balls containing wheat and soy were included without proper allergen labeling, replacing the intended bourbon-flavored caramels.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA notice. Production has been suspended while the packaging issue is addressed. Consumers should review the lot and expiration details below.

BrandProductSizeLot NumberExpiration DateDistribution Area
Frederik's by MeijerVanilla Bourbon Trail Mix9 oz6069-112/10/26MI, IN, OH, IL, WI, KY

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Frederik's by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix for the affected lot and avoid consuming it if they have wheat or soy allergies.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-877-363-4537

headline: Aligned Medical Solutions Recalls AMS6908E and AMS6908F Angio Packs for Syringe Adapter Risk

image.caption: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions Issues Nationwide Recall of Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter 10CC Control Syringe Placed into the Following Kits; AMS6908E Angio Pack, and AMS6908F Angio Pack

description: Aligned Medical Solutions recalled AMS6908E and AMS6908F Angio Packs after reports of syringe adapter disconnection, posing biohazard and safety risks.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Windstone Medical Packaging, doing business as Aligned Medical Solutions, has issued a nationwide recall of two Angio Pack kits (AMS6908E and AMS6908F) containing the Medline Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter (RA) 10CC Syringe. The recall was initiated due to the risk that the syringe rotating adapter may unwind during use, potentially resulting in a loose or disconnected connection between the syringes and manifold. This defect can lead to biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

The affected kits were manufactured between October 18, 2024, and November 13, 2025, and distributed from October 28, 2024, through November 28, 2025. According to the FDA notice, there have been three incidents of inadvertent air injection and one biohazard exposure reported, with no deaths associated with this issue. All distributed units may be affected by this failure mode.

This recall applies only to the Medline 10CC Control Syringe within these kits; other kit components are not included. The information above is based on the official FDA recall notice and company announcement.

Kit NameProduct CodeDI NumberLot NumbersManufacture Dates
AMS6908E Angio PackAMS6908EB098AMS6908E021487510/18/2024 – 11/13/2025
AMS6908F Angio PackAMS6908FB098AMS6908F0215436, 225001, 226706, 23408810/18/2024 – 11/13/2025

facts.reason: Risk of the syringe rotating adapter unwinding during use, which may result in a loose connection and/or full disconnection between the syringes and manifold, potentially causing biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Aligned Medical Angio Packs are from lot numbers AMS6908E or AMS6908F and stop using them due to risk of syringe adapter disconnection, which may cause biohazard exposure or other serious harm.

headline: Breeder's Edge and Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacers Recalled for Variable Vitamin D Levels

image.caption: Revival Animal Health, LLC Voluntarily Recalls Canine Milk Replacers Due to Elevated Levels of Vitamin D

description: Revival Animal Health has recalled select Breeder's Edge and Shelter's Choice canine milk replacers due to variable Vitamin D levels, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Revival Animal Health of Orange City, IA is voluntarily recalling specific lots of Breeder's Edge® Foster Care® and Shelter's Choice® Canine Milk Replacers due to variable levels of Vitamin D. Some products may contain either low or elevated Vitamin D, which can cause health issues in dogs. Elevated Vitamin D intake may result in symptoms such as vomiting, loss of appetite, increased thirst and urination, excessive drooling, and weight loss, with severe cases potentially leading to renal dysfunction.

The recall includes multiple package sizes and best-by dates of canine milk replacers distributed nationwide online and in pet stores. The FDA notice states that two complaints of rickets have been reported, and the recall follows confirmation of inconsistent Vitamin D levels. This action is being taken in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No other products are affected by this recall.

headline: Loard's Ice Cream Recalled Due to Undeclared Allergens and Additives

image.caption: Silver Moon LP DBA Loard's Ice Cream Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk, Eggs, Tree Nuts, Peanuts, Soy, Wheat, Sulfites, and Food Color Additives in Ice Cream Products

description: Silver Moon LP is recalling Loard's Ice Cream sold in Northern California due to undeclared milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, sulfites, and colors. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Silver Moon LP, doing business as Loard's Ice Cream, of San Leandro, CA, has voluntarily recalled all retail-sized Loard's Ice Cream products. The recall is due to undeclared allergens, sulfites, and food color additives on the packaging. These products lack required ingredient labeling, which may pose a risk of serious allergic reactions for individuals sensitive to milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, or wheat.

The affected ice cream was distributed in Loard's Ice Cream parlors in Northern California in 32oz blue paper cups and 56oz plastic cups. The FDA notice states that no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. The recall was initiated following FDA inspection findings of missing allergen and ingredient information on retail packaging.

Consumers are advised to review the list of affected flavors and undeclared ingredients. Detailed product information is provided below.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Silver Moon LP, doing business as Loard's Ice Cream, of San Leandro, CA, has voluntarily recalled all retail-sized Loard's Ice Cream products. The recall is due to undeclared allergens, sulfites, and food color additives on the packaging. These products lack required ingredient labeling, which may pose a risk of serious allergic reactions for individuals sensitive to milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, or wheat.

The affected ice cream was distributed in Loard's Ice Cream parlors in Northern California in 32oz blue paper cups and 56oz plastic cups. The FDA notice states that no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. The recall was initiated following FDA inspection findings of missing allergen and ingredient information on retail packaging.

Consumers are advised to review the list of affected flavors and undeclared ingredients. Detailed product information is provided below.

headline: Liquid Blenz Corp Recalls Good Brain Tonic Due to Botulism Hazard

image.caption: Liquid Blenz Corp. Recalls Product Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Liquid Blenz Corp recalled all Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz bottles nationwide due to a potential risk of botulism. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Liquid Blenz Corp of Rockville Center, NY, is recalling all lots of Good Brain Tonic due to a potential risk of botulism, a serious foodborne illness. Botulism can cause symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide through retail stores and online. This recall includes Good Brain Tonic bottled in 16 oz and 32 oz amber bottles with plastic caps. The botulism risk was identified through testing by the Cornell Food Venture Center and New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets.

No illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date, as stated in the FDA notice.

ProductSizeUPC Code
Good Brain Tonic16 oz Amber bottle860010984468
Good Brain Tonic32 oz Amber bottle860010984475

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Carbonated Soft Drinks Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz bottles and follow the FDA recall notice instructions for safe handling or disposal.

headline: Morovan Recalls Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) Due to Methylene Chloride and Chloroform
description: Morovan is recalling its Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) after FDA found prohibited methylene chloride and chloroform. No injuries or illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Morovan has recalled its Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) due to the presence of methylene chloride and chloroform, which are prohibited in cosmetic products. Methylene chloride is not permitted in cosmetics at any concentration under FDA regulations and has been linked to cancer in animal studies.

No injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported. The affected product was sold online at Amazon.com between August 2025 and January 2026.

Product NameSizePackagingProduction DateExpiration DateUnits AffectedRetailerManufactured In

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using Morovan Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) immediately if they have it, and contact Morovan at customer-service@morovan.com for further instructions as directed in the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: customer-service@morovan.com

headline: Blueroot Health Recalls Vital Nutrients Aller-C Due to Undeclared Allergens
description: Blueroot Health has expanded its recall of Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements because of undeclared egg, hazelnut, and soy. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 10, 2026, Blueroot Health of Middletown, Connecticut, expanded its voluntary recall of Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements due to undeclared egg, hazelnut, and soy allergens. The recall now includes all products from lot 25E04, in addition to previously recalled lots 25E04-A and 25E04-B.

Individuals with allergies or sensitivities to these ingredients risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume the affected supplements. The recalled products were distributed nationwide through VitalNutrients.co and other online retailers.

The supplements are packaged in white bottles of 100 or 200 capsules, with an expiration date of 05/27. Distribution occurred between September 2025 and March 2026. According to the FDA notice, no adverse events or illnesses have been reported to date.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do

headline: Blaine Labs Recalls Revitaderm and Tridergel Wound Care Gels Due to Lysinibacillus fusiformis

image.caption: Blaine Labs, Inc Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Wound Care Gel Products Due to Microbial Contamination

description: Blaine Labs, Inc. recalled select Revitaderm and Tridergel wound care gels after Lysinibacillus fusiformis contamination was found. No injuries have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blaine Labs, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of specific lots of Revitaderm® and Tridergel™ antiseptic wound care gels. The recall covers 1 oz. and 3 oz. bottles and tubes containing 0.1% Benzalkonium Chloride, due to the detection of Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental bacterium.

Lysinibacillus fusiformis is generally considered to have low pathogenicity in healthy individuals. However, people with open wounds, compromised skin, or weakened immune systems may be at increased risk for infection or delayed wound healing. No adverse events or injuries related to this recall have been reported to Blaine Labs as of the FDA notice.

BrandProduct DescriptionLot NumberExpiration DatePackaging Details
Revitaderm®1 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic GelBL360807/01/28Yellow ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, shorter twist cap
Revitaderm®1 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic GelBL343511/06/26Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, long pointy cap
Revitaderm®3 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic GelBL343511/06/26Imprinted on tube crimp
Revitaderm®1 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic GelBL352508/07/27Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, long pointy cap
Revitaderm®3 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic GelBL352508/07/27Imprinted on tube crimp
Tridergel™1 oz. Wound Care GelBL343511/06/26Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, long pointy cap

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No illnesses or injuries have been reported as of the recall date.

headline: DTF Sexual Chocolate Recalled by Nalpac Nationwide for Hidden Erectile Dysfunction Drugs

image.caption: Nalpac Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of DTF Sexual Chocolate Due to the Presence of the Pharmaceutical Ingredient's Sildenafil and Tadalafil

description: Nalpac has recalled DTF Sexual Chocolate due to undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, which may pose health risks. No adverse events have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Nalpac is voluntarily recalling DTF Sexual Chocolate because it contains undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients sildenafil and tadalafil. These substances are the active ingredients in prescription medications for erectile dysfunction and may interact dangerously with certain heart medications, such as nitrates. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product, purchased and resold by Nalpac, was not properly labeled to disclose these ingredients.

The FDA notice states that no adverse events or illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. The affected product can be identified by UPC 757817783069 and was distributed through adult retail stores and online at shopsexology.com and royalsins.com.

ProductUPCPackaging
DTF Sexual Chocolate757817783069Cases containing 20 retail units

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using DTF Sexual Chocolate and check if their product matches the recalled UPC. Do not consume the product if affected.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Nalpac is voluntarily recalling DTF Sexual Chocolate because it contains undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients sildenafil and tadalafil. These substances are the active ingredients in prescription medications for erectile dysfunction and may interact dangerously with certain heart medications, such as nitrates. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product, purchased and resold by Nalpac, was not properly labeled to disclose these ingredients.

The FDA notice states that no adverse events or illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. The affected product can be identified by UPC 757817783069 and was distributed through adult retail stores and online at shopsexology.com and royalsins.com.

ProductUPCPackaging
DTF Sexual Chocolate757817783069Cases containing 20 retail units

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using DTF Sexual Chocolate and check if their product matches the recalled UPC. Do not consume the product if affected.

headline: Wawa Recalls 16 oz Iced Tea Lemon, Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch Due to Undeclared Milk

image.caption: Wawa Issues Product Recall on 16-oz. Wawa Brand Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade and Fruit Punch Due to Possible Undeclared Milk Allergen

description: Wawa has recalled certain 16 oz bottles of Iced Tea Lemon, Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch due to undeclared milk. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wawa has issued a recall for select 16 oz bottles of its Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch beverages. The recall affects products sold at specific Wawa stores in Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, and Virginia. The action was taken due to the potential presence of an undeclared milk allergen, which could cause serious allergic reactions in those with milk allergies.

According to the official FDA notice, all affected products have been removed from sale at the impacted locations. No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the recall date.

The recall was initiated after Wawa identified a temporary equipment issue that may have resulted in the presence of undeclared milk in the affected beverages. The company has since corrected the issue.

headline: Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups Recalled by Karns Foods Due to Undeclared Peanuts
description: Karns Foods is recalling Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups sold in Pennsylvania due to undeclared peanuts. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Karns Foods has voluntarily recalled its Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups, sold in approximately 8-ounce packages, due to the potential presence of undeclared peanuts. The product was distributed exclusively at Karns Foods locations in Pennsylvania. People with peanut allergies may risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The affected product is marked with a pack date of September 18, 2025. Approximately 26 to 42 packages are involved. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that a product containing peanuts was mistakenly packaged without the allergen being declared on the label, due to a temporary packaging process issue.

The Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups were removed from store shelves starting January 30, 2026. Karns Foods notified registered rewards program shoppers who purchased the product. No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice.

ProductPackage SizePack DateDistribution AreaApproximate Quantity Affected
Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups8 ouncesSeptember 18, 2025Karns Foods locations in Pennsylvania26-42 packs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups with the specified pack date and return any affected product to Karns Foods. Contact Karns Foods at 717-766-6477 for further information.

headline: RAW FARM Recalls Raw Milk Cheddar Cheese Due to E. coli Risk
description: RAW FARM is recalling select lots of raw milk cheddar cheese, both shredded and block, after FDA concerns about potential E. coli contamination. Injuries have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 2, 2026, Raw Farm voluntarily recalled specific lots of RAW FARM-brand raw milk cheddar cheese, both shredded and block, due to potential contamination with E. coli. According to the FDA notice, no pathogens were detected in FDA or state Health Department samples. Raw Farm states it disagrees with the FDA's epidemiological evidence but is recalling the products at the FDA's request. The recall is limited to the lots listed below and any batches produced prior to those dates.

The FDA advisory is linked to an outbreak investigation of E. coli O157:H7 associated with raw cheddar cheese. The FDA notice confirms that injuries or adverse events have been reported in connection with this issue.

ItemItem DescriptionBatchExpiration DateBarcode (UPC)
10508 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Block20251027-28/23/2026835204001177
106080 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Block20251015-48/11/2026835204001160
107516 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Block20251027-48/23/2026835204000156
107680 oz Bag of Original Cheddar Shred202602055/6/2026835204000194
107816 oz Jalapeno Cheddar Block20251128-1J9/24/2026835204000354
10808 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Shred202602125/13/2026835204001184
10908 oz Jalapeno Cheddar Block20251128-2J9/24/2026835204000330
  • Do not consume RAW FARM cheddar cheese from the listed batches or any produced earlier.
  • Return recalled products to the original place of purchase for a full refund.
  • See the FDA advisory for more details: Outbreak Investigation of E. coli O157:H7: Raw Cheddar Cheese (March 2026) | FDA.
  • Contact Raw Farm for questions via their website: Find — RAW FARM usa.

headline: Tops Recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Due to Botulism Hazard

image.caption: Tops Issues Recall of Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic & Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Tops Friendly Markets has recalled Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic over potential Clostridium botulinum contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Tops Friendly Markets of Williamsville, NY, has recalled all lots of Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic and Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic due to possible contamination with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a rare but serious illness. The recall was initiated after it was determined that improper storage temperatures may have allowed the bacteria to grow.

The affected products are 6-ounce plastic bags of Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic and Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic, distributed at Tops Markets in New York, Pennsylvania, and Vermont. Consumers are cautioned that botulism can occur even if the garlic appears normal, as symptoms may develop without visible spoilage.

Symptoms of botulism may include weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. As of the FDA announcement, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

BrandProductPackage SizeUPCProduct Code Dates
Christopher RanchPeeled Garlic6 oz. plastic bags74574-10852All product code dates
GarlandFresh Peeled Garlic6 oz. plastic bags71894-00000All product code dates
GarlandFresh Peeled Garlic6 oz. plastic bags68826-75340All product code dates

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their packaging for Christopher Ranch or Garland Peeled Garlic in 6 oz. bags and discontinue use of affected products. For further information, contact Tops Friendly Markets as directed in the official FDA notice.

headline: Kian Pee Wan Dietary Supplement Capsules Recalled Nationwide for Unapproved Drug Content

image.caption: Aphreseller (Buy-herbal.com) Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Kian Pee Wan Capsules Due the Presence of Undeclared Dexamethasone and Cyproheptadine

description: Aphreseller is recalling all Kian Pee Wan dietary supplement capsules due to undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine, per FDA notice. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Aphreseller (Buy-herbal.com) has voluntarily recalled all lots of Kian Pee Wan dietary supplement capsules nationwide. FDA testing found the capsules contain undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine, which are not permitted in dietary supplements and render the product an unapproved drug.

Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that may cause serious side effects such as immune suppression, high blood sugar, muscle injury, psychiatric effects, cardiovascular events, and adrenal gland suppression. Cyproheptadine is a prescription antihistamine that can cause sedation, dizziness, fatigue, dry mouth, and other effects when not disclosed on the label.

The recalled Kian Pee Wan capsules are packaged in white bottles with beige labels (30 pills per bottle) and were distributed nationwide via eBay.com. As of the FDA recall notice, no adverse events or injuries related to this product have been reported.

Product NameFormPackagingDistributionRecall Reason
Kian Pee Wan capsulesDietary supplement capsulesWhite bottle with beige label, 30 pillsNationwide online via eBayContains undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine (unapproved drug ingredients)

Consumers with questions about the recall may contact the company by phone or email as listed in the FDA notice. Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA MedWatch program.

facts.product: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for treatment for gaining weight and stimulating appetite.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Kian Pee Wan capsules from Aphreseller should discontinue use and check the FDA recall notice for further instructions. Contact the company if you have questions or concerns.

FDA testing found the capsules contain undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine, which are not permitted in dietary supplements and render the product an unapproved drug.

Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that may cause serious side effects such as immune suppression, high blood sugar, muscle injury, psychiatric effects, cardiovascular events, and adrenal gland suppression. Cyproheptadine is a prescription antihistamine that can cause sedation, dizziness, fatigue, dry mouth, and other effects when not disclosed on the label.

The recalled Kian Pee Wan capsules are packaged in white bottles with beige labels (30 pills per bottle) and were distributed nationwide via eBay.com. As of the FDA recall notice, no adverse events or injuries related to this product have been reported.

Product NameFormPackagingDistributionRecall Reason
Kian Pee Wan capsulesDietary supplement capsulesWhite bottle with beige label, 30 pillsNationwide online via eBayContains undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine (unapproved drug ingredients)

Consumers with questions about the recall may contact the company by phone or email as listed in the FDA notice. Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA MedWatch program.

title: Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Cream Cheese Recalled for Undeclared Almonds
headline: Recall: Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Cream Cheese Due to Undeclared Almonds
description: Schreiber Foods recalled Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Cream Cheese after undeclared almonds were found. No illnesses reported. FDA notice issued March 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Schreiber Foods, Inc. of Green Bay, WI, has voluntarily recalled 144 cases of Einstein Bros. Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread due to undeclared almonds. This recall is important for people with almond allergies, as consuming the product could cause a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction. The affected product was distributed to Einstein Bros. Bagels locations in Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, and Wyoming.

The recalled product is a 6-ounce plastic cup with a Honey Almond lid and a Plain cup, labeled with lot code "Best If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LO" on the bottom. The lid correctly states Honey Almond, but the cup is labeled Plain and does not list almonds as an ingredient.

The recall was initiated after a packaging mismatch was discovered. Schreiber Foods has corrected the issue. According to the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date.

ProductSizeLot Code / Expiration DateDistribution Area
Einstein Bros. Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread (with mismatched Plain cup)6-ounce plastic cupBest If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LOColorado, Utah, New Mexico, Wyoming

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with almond allergies should not eat the recalled Honey Cream Cheese. Check for the lot code "Best If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LO" and mismatched packaging. If affected, return the product to the place of purchase for a refund.

title: Gear Isle Recalls Gold Lion and ilum Chocolate for Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: Gold Lion and ilum Chocolate Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Sildenafil, Tadalafil
description: Gear Isle is recalling Gold Lion and ilum chocolate products nationwide due to undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, per FDA notice. No adverse events reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Gear Isle has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of Gold Lion Aphrodisiac Chocolate Sachet and ilum Sex Chocolate due to the presence of undeclared active pharmaceutical ingredients, sildenafil and tadalafil. These substances are not listed on the product labels and may pose health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates for heart conditions.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide via internet sales and are marketed as dietary supplements. No reports of adverse events or illnesses related to these products have been received as of the FDA notice.

Undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil can cause significant drops in blood pressure, which may be dangerous for certain consumers. The FDA advises that consumers who experience health issues after using these products should consult a healthcare provider.

Product NamePackaging SizeUPCLot NumberExpiration Date
Gold Lion Aphrodisiac Chocolate Sachet (Male Enhancement)1-count795847916279No lot number06/2027
ilum Sex Chocolate (Male Sexual Enhancement Booster)1-count1002448578911No lot number12/25/2027

Consumers and healthcare professionals are encouraged to report any adverse reactions to the FDA's MedWatch program.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Gold Lion or ilum chocolate products from Gear Isle should check the product details for possible inclusion of sildenafil and tadalafil. If you have these products, contact Gear Isle for further information and support.

title: Falcon Trading Company Recalls Organic Black Bean Products for Pesticide Residue
headline: Recall: Falcon Trading Company Organic Black Beans, Chili Bean Blend, and Sunset Soup Mix Due to Pesticide Residue
description: Falcon Trading Company recalled select organic black bean products after pesticide residue was detected. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.
productDescription: Black Beans Organic | Chili Bean Blend Organic | Sunset Soup Mix Organic

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Falcon Trading Company, Inc. of Royal Oaks, California, has recalled specific organic black bean products due to the presence of pesticide residue. The recall covers all lot numbers of Black Beans Organic, Chili Bean Blend Organic, and Sunset Soup Mix Organic, as these items are sold in bulk and lot numbers may be mixed within sales bins.

The issue was identified during routine testing of organic beans sourced from a certified organic handler. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with these products as of the recall date.

title: Amneal Recalls Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP 4g/100mL IV Bags Due to Product Mix-Up
headline: Amneal Recalls Magnesium Sulfate IV Bags After Tranexamic Acid Found in Pouch
description: Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC recalled one lot of Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL IV bags after a pouch was found to contain Tranexamic Acid. No adverse events have been reported.
productDescription: Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL, IV bag
reason: A Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection pouch was found to contain an IV bag of Tranexamic Acid in 0.7% Sodium Chloride Injection, 10 mg/mL.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL IV bags (NDC 70121-1720-3) after a pouch labeled as Magnesium Sulfate was found to contain an IV bag of Tranexamic Acid in 0.7% Sodium Chloride Injection, 10 mg/mL, according to the FDA notice.

This mix-up poses significant risks, especially for pregnant individuals with preeclampsia or eclampsia who require magnesium therapy to prevent seizures. Delays in receiving the correct medication could result in serious or life-threatening complications for both mother and preterm neonates. The FDA notice states that Amneal has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall. Accidental administration of Tranexamic Acid instead of magnesium sulfate may cause adverse effects such as blood clotting, seizures, hypersensitivity reactions, visual disturbances, and dizziness.

The affected product, lot AH250162, was distributed nationwide to wholesalers and distributors between December 22, 2025, and February 27, 2026. The product is supplied in cartons containing twelve 100mL pouches. Amneal reports that 74% of the remaining recalled pouches have been inspected with no additional mix-ups found.

ProductLot NumberNDCPackagingDistribution Dates
Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL IV bagAH25016270121-1720-312 x 100mL pouches per carton12/22/2025 to 2/27/2026

For product returns or inquiries, Amneal provides a recall coordinator and contact information. Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA MedWatch program as outlined in the official notice.

title: Webcol Large Alcohol Prep Pads Recalled by Cardinal Health for Microbial Contamination
headline: Cardinal Health Recalls Webcol Large Alcohol Prep Pads Due to Microbial Contamination Risk
description: Cardinal Health recalled select lots of Webcol Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) after microbial contamination was identified. See FDA notice for details.
productDescription: Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Cardinal Health has voluntarily recalled select lots of Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) due to microbial contamination with Paenibacillus phoenicis. The recall covers products distributed in the United States, Puerto Rico, and Japan from September 2025 to February 2026. The contamination may cause local or systemic infections, especially in individuals with weakened immune systems, such as those undergoing chemotherapy or with poorly controlled diabetes. These infections could be severe or life-threatening, including bacteremia or central nervous system infections.

The affected Webcol™ Alcohol Prep Pads are intended for external use and commonly used for skin cleansing. Cardinal Health notified customers of the recall by overnight mail on March 2, 2026, and provided guidance to consumers.

No specific injuries or adverse events have been reported to date, according to the FDA notice. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

ProductLot NumberDistribution Period
Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)Various (select lots)September 2025 – February 2026

Consumers and healthcare professionals can report adverse events or quality problems related to this recall to the FDA MedWatch program by:

  • Submitting an online report at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Downloading a reporting form from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm and submitting it by mail or fax
  • Calling 1-800-332-1088 to request a form
  • Faxing the completed form to 1-800-FDA-0178

facts.product: Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Webcol Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) should check their product for possible inclusion in the recall. If you suspect your product is affected, contact Cardinal Health at 800-292-9332 or GMB-FieldCorrectiveAction@cardinalhealth.com for guidance and next steps.

title: Mama Grande Recalls Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar for Undeclared Wheat and Soy
headline: Undeclared Wheat and Soy Prompts Recall of Mama Grande Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar
description: Mama Grande Tortilla Factory recalled Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens. No illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Mama Grande Tortilla Factory of Mission, Texas, has recalled Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar because they contain undeclared wheat and soy allergens. The absence of this information on the product labels poses a risk of serious allergic reactions for people with wheat or soy allergies or sensitivities.

The recalled products were distributed in Texas to retail and wholesale customers between February 2, 2026, and March 5, 2026. Products are packaged in plastic bags with the product name and UPC code on the front.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA recall date. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product NameUPCExpiration Dates
Gorditas de Azucar860010238134All Expiration Dates
Doraditas de Azucar5901234123457All Expiration Dates

content.text: Consumers who purchased Mama Grande Gorditas de Azucar or Doraditas de Azucar should not eat these products if they have wheat or soy allergies. Check packaging for the affected UPCs and return or dispose of the products as advised in the official recall notice.

title: Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull Honey Dietary Supplements Recalled for Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC Recalls Honey Dietary Supplements Due to Undeclared Prescription Ingredients
description: Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull honey dietary supplements have been recalled nationwide after FDA found undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil in the products.
keywords: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC
brandName: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull
brandNames: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull
companyName: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC of Tampa, FL has voluntarily recalled certain honey dietary supplement products nationwide due to undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients. The affected products, sold under the Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull brands, were found to contain sildenafil and tadalafil, which are active ingredients in prescription medications for erectile dysfunction. These substances are not disclosed on the product labels and may pose health risks.

Sildenafil and tadalafil can interact with nitrates found in some prescription drugs, potentially causing dangerously low blood pressure. The recall applies to single-serving plastic tubes identified by the following details:

Product NameLot NumberExpiration DateUPC Code
Boner Bears HoneyBBH012801/28788362191603
Red Bull ExtremeORB52405/20299554100205595
Blue Bull ExtremeN/A02/2029707443349917

As of the FDA notice, no injuries or adverse events have been reported. Consumers are encouraged to report any adverse reactions to the FDA's MedWatch program.

facts.company: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC
facts.brand: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Boner Bear, Red Bull, or Blue Bull honey dietary supplements from Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC and avoid using affected products. For questions or support, contact the company at 1-800-362-4772 or email sales@purevitaminsusa.com.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-362-4772 | sales@purevitaminsusa.com

title: Insulet Recalls Certain Omnipod 5 Insulin Pumps for Potential Insulin Delivery Issue
headline: Recall: Omnipod 5 Insulin Infusion Pumps May Leak Insulin Due to Tubing Tear
description: Insulet Corporation is recalling select Omnipod 5 insulin infusion pumps after reports of internal tubing tears that may cause insulin leakage and under-delivery.
productDescription: Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump
reason: Pods may have a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin. This may leak inside the Pod instead of being fully infused into the body as intended.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Insulet Corporation has initiated a voluntary Medical Device Correction for certain lots of Omnipod 5 Pods in the United States. The recall is due to a manufacturing issue where a small tear in the internal tubing may cause insulin to leak inside the Pod, resulting in under-delivery of insulin to the user.

This defect may lead to high blood glucose levels and, in severe cases, diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). The FDA reports 18 serious adverse events, including hospitalizations and DKA, but no deaths. Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems are not affected by this issue.

Consumers can check if their Omnipod 5 Pods are affected by visiting omnipod.com/check-pods. Insulet has updated manufacturing processes and notified the FDA of these corrective actions.

Affected Omnipod 5 Pod Lot Numbers
Lot numbers can be verified at omnipod.com/check-pods.

facts.product: Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Omnipod 5 Pod lot numbers at omnipod.com/check-pods to determine if their device is affected. If so, discontinue use and follow the instructions provided on the site for replacement.

title: Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs Nougat Candy Recalled for Undeclared Hazelnuts by Lidl US
headline: Lidl US Recalls Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs Nougat Candy Due to Undeclared Hazelnut Allergen
description: Lidl US recalled all lots of Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat Candy due to undeclared hazelnuts. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Lidl US has recalled all lots of Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat Candy because the product contains undeclared hazelnuts. The absence of this allergen information on the label poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with hazelnut allergies.

The affected product was distributed between January 28, 2026, and March 11, 2026, at Lidl stores in Delaware, District of Columbia, Georgia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, and Virginia. As of the recall date, no illnesses related to this product have been reported.

ProductSizeUPCLot NumbersDistribution DatesStates
Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat3.52 oz box20304492All lots01/28/2026 – 03/11/2026DE, DC, GA, MD, NJ, NY, NC, PA, SC, VA

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date, according to the official recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with hazelnut allergies should not eat the recalled Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat Candy. Check packaging for affected lots and return the product to any Lidl US store for a full refund. For questions, contact Lidl US at (844) 747-5435.

title: Primal Herbs Volume Dietary Supplement Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Sildenafil
headline: Nationwide Recall: Primal Herbs Volume Dietary Supplement Contains Undeclared Sildenafil
description: Primal Supplements Group LLC recalled Primal Herbs Volume after FDA found undeclared sildenafil. The recall affects products sold online between July and September 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Primal Supplements Group LLC has voluntarily recalled Primal Herbs Volume dietary supplement nationwide due to undeclared sildenafil. FDA laboratory analysis confirmed the presence of sildenafil, the active ingredient in Viagra, which is not declared on the product label.

Sildenafil is a prescription medication used for erectile dysfunction and can pose serious health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. The interaction may result in dangerously low blood pressure.

The recall affects all Primal Herbs Volume orders placed between July 2, 2025, and September 19, 2025, purchased from primalherbs.com. The product is packaged in 8.5 oz round containers with green labels. As of the FDA notice, no injuries or adverse events had been reported.

Product NameOrder DatesPackagingWebsite Purchased From
Primal Herbs VolumeJuly 2, 2025 – September 19, 20258.5 oz round container with green labelprimalherbs.com
  • Consumers and healthcare professionals can report adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA's MedWatch program:
    • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
    • Mail or Fax: Download the form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088
    • Submit forms by mail to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Primal Herbs Volume dietary supplement if purchased between July 2 and September 19, 2025, and check the FDA recall notice for details. Contact Primal Supplements Group LLC for support if needed.

title: Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Frito-Lay Recalls Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Frito-Lay recalled select Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips after undeclared milk was found. No allergic reactions have been reported as of the FDA's 2026 notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on March 4, 2026, Frito-Lay has voluntarily recalled select 8 oz. bags of Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips due to undeclared milk allergens. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that some bags may contain jalapeño-flavored potato chips, which include milk as an ingredient. People with a milk allergy or severe sensitivity risk a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction if they consume these products.

The affected chips were distributed starting January 15, 2026, through grocery, convenience, and drug stores, as well as digital retailers in Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, New Mexico, Oklahoma, and Texas. No other Miss Vickie's products or flavors are included in this recall.

The issue was identified following a consumer report. As of the recall date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported in connection with this product, per the official FDA notice.

ProductSizeUPC"Guaranteed Fresh" DateManufacturing Codes
Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips8 oz.0 28400 76177 221 APR 202638U301414, 48U101514

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with a milk allergy or sensitivity should not eat the recalled Miss Vickie's Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips. Check the product's UPC and date codes listed in the recall notice and dispose of affected bags if present.

title: RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X Chocolate Recalled by USA LESS Co. for Undeclared Tadalafil
headline: USA LESS Co. Recalls RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X Chocolate Due to Hidden Tadalafil
description: USA LESS Co. recalled RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X chocolate after FDA found undeclared tadalafil, posing health risks. No injuries reported as of the FDA's 2026 notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, USA LESS Co. has recalled RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X chocolate because it contains undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug used to treat erectile dysfunction. The product is sold in 10 gram, 12-piece boxes with a black cardboard outer box and UPC Code 724087947668.

Tadalafil is not listed on the product label. Its presence may cause serious health risks for individuals taking prescription nitrates, such as nitroglycerin, due to the potential for dangerous drops in blood pressure. This risk is heightened for people with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease who may use nitrate medications.

The recalled chocolate was distributed through retail stores and online platforms, including the company's website. The product has been removed from sale online. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or injuries related to this recall have been reported.

ProductPackage SizeQuantityUPC CodeExpiration Date
RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X10 grams12 pieces72408794766810/2027

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X chocolate should stop using the product and check for the UPC code 724087947668. Contact USA LESS Co. by phone or email for further instructions or to arrange a return.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-872-5377 | 409749@email4pr.com

title: Savannah Bee Company Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard Recalled for Undeclared Wheat and Soy
headline: Recall: Savannah Bee Company Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard Due to Undeclared Allergens
description: Savannah Bee Company is recalling Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZ, due to undeclared wheat and soy. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on February 27, 2026, Savannah Bee Company has recalled its Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZ, lot number B1L1360525, due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens. The product has a best before date of 05/16/27 and UPC 8 50033 93758 9. Individuals with allergies or sensitivities to wheat or soy may face a risk of serious allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled BBQ sauce was distributed nationwide to distribution centers, retail stores, and consumers between July 30, 2025, and February 26, 2026. The product is packaged in clear glass bottles with an orange Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard label. The lot number and best before date are etched on the neck of the bottle.

The recall was initiated after a customer reported that Honey BBQ Sauce-Sweet was mislabeled with a Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard label, resulting in the omission of wheat and soy allergens from the ingredient statement. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date, as stated in the official notice.

ProductLot NumberBest Before DateUPC
Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZB1L136052505/16/278 50033 93758 9

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Savannah Bee Company Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZ, lot B1L1360525. If so, do not consume it due to undeclared wheat and soy; contact Savannah Bee Company for further guidance.

title: Go Raw LLC Recalls Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen for Low Thiamine Levels
headline: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen Recalled Nationwide for Potential Thiamine Deficiency Risk
description: Go Raw LLC has recalled select lots of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen due to potentially low thiamine (Vitamin B1) levels, per FDA notice.
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
reason: May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Go Raw LLC has expanded its voluntary recall of Quest Diet Cat Food products due to the potential for low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1). The recall now includes two lots of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen, in addition to a previously recalled freeze-dried product. The company has also enacted a stop sale of all Quest products at retailers until the thiamine issue is resolved.

Thiamine is essential for cats, and deficiency may cause decreased appetite, salivation, vomiting, weight loss, and, in advanced cases, neurological symptoms. The FDA states that prompt veterinary care can typically reverse these effects.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide through retail stores in multiple states. Retailers have been instructed to remove affected products from shelves, and Go Raw LLC is replacing inventory with newly formulated products.

title: Great Value Cottage Cheese Recalled by Saputo Cheese USA for Incomplete Pasteurization
headline: Saputo Cheese USA Recalls Great Value Cottage Cheese for Pasteurization Issue
description: Saputo Cheese USA is recalling select Great Value cottage cheese due to incomplete pasteurization, per FDA notice. Distributed in 24 states in February 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Saputo Cheese USA Inc. is voluntarily recalling select Great Value cottage cheese products due to the possible use of liquid dairy ingredients that were not fully pasteurized according to state regulatory standards. Consuming these products may pose a significant health risk, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

The issue was identified during pasteurizer troubleshooting in collaboration with the California Department of Food and Agriculture. The affected pasteurizer has since been restored to normal function, verified, and sealed by the Department. No other products from the facility are impacted by this recall.

Affected products were distributed to Walmart stores and distribution centers across multiple states between February 17 and February 20, 2026. This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product NamePackaging Size & TypeUPCExpiration DatesPlant Code
Great Value Fat Free Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 0% Milkfat24 oz. container078742373393BEST IF USED BY APR-01-26, APR-03-2606-71
Great Value Lowfat Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 2% Milkfat24 oz. container078742116730BEST IF USED BY APR-01-26, APR-02-26, APR-03-2606-71
Great Value Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 4% Milkfat minimum16 oz. container078742372358BEST IF USED BY APR-02-2606-71
Great Value Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 4% Milkfat minimum24 oz. container078742372365BEST IF USED BY APR-02-26, APR-03-2606-71
Great Value Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 4% Milkfat minimum3 lb. container078742147970BEST IF USED BY APR-01-26, APR-02-2606-71

The recalled products were sold in white plastic tubs with white lids. The "Best If Used By" date and plant code (PLT# 06-71) are printed on the side of the container lid. The recall only applies to the states listed below where these products were distributed:

  • Alaska
  • Alabama
  • Arkansas
  • Arizona
  • California
  • Colorado
  • Georgia
  • Iowa
  • Idaho
  • Illinois
  • Kansas
  • Kentucky
  • Louisiana
  • Missouri
  • Mississippi
  • Montana
  • New Mexico
  • Nevada
  • Oregon
  • Texas
  • Tennessee
  • Utah
  • Washington
  • Wyoming

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot codes and expiration dates on their Great Value cottage cheese containers. If your product matches the recalled information, do not consume it.

title: Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Sildenafil
headline: Lockout Supplements Recalls Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup Over Undeclared Sildenafil Risk
description: Lockout Supplements recalled all Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup due to undeclared sildenafil, a prescription drug ingredient. No adverse events reported, per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lockout Supplements has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup due to undeclared sildenafil, according to the FDA notice. Sildenafil is the active ingredient in certain prescription medications and was not disclosed on the product label.

The presence of undeclared sildenafil may pose serious health risks, especially for individuals taking medications containing nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. Use of sildenafil with nitrates can cause a significant drop in blood pressure. As of the FDA recall notice dated February 25, 2026, no injuries or adverse events have been reported.

The recall covers all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup distributed nationwide from January 1, 2025, through February 13, 2026. The product was sold online through lockoutsupplements.com. Lockout Supplements is notifying customers and offering refunds for returned products.

Product NameUPCLot Dates
Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup00085668357001/01/25 - 02/13/26

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup and check the product label and lot dates. For questions or returns, contact Lockout Supplements. Discontinue use until further notice.

title: Elite Treats Recalls Chicken Dog Treats for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Elite Treats Issues Recall of Chicken Chips for Dogs Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Elite Treats, LLC. recalled a single lot of Chicken Chips for Dogs due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Elite Treats, LLC., based in Boca Raton, FL, has recalled a single lot of "Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs" 6-ounce bags due to possible Salmonella contamination. The affected lot is number 24045 with an expiration date of 04/2027. Salmonella in pet food can pose health risks to both pets and humans.

Pets consuming contaminated treats may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea (possibly bloody), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Some pets may carry Salmonella without showing symptoms and can spread it to other animals or humans.

Humans may be exposed to Salmonella by handling the product, contact with pets that have consumed it, or contaminated surfaces. Symptoms in people can include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fever. Severe cases may lead to more serious health complications. The FDA states there have been no reported illnesses associated with this recall as of the announcement date.

The recalled product was distributed to feed stores in Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina through Florida Hardware, LLC.

ProductPackage SizeLot NumberExpiration Date
Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs6-ounce bag2404504/2027

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs for lot number 24045 and expiration date 04/2027. If you have the recalled product, follow the FDA's guidance to stop use and dispose of it safely.

title: Made Fresh Salads, Inc. Recalls Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread for Listeria Risk
headline: Recall: Made Fresh Salad Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread Due to Listeria monocytogenes
description: Made Fresh Salads, Inc. is recalling select cream cheese and tofu spread products due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Various Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Made Fresh Salads, Inc. of Bay Shore, NY, is recalling multiple flavors of its cream cheese and tofu spread products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This organism can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Pregnant women may experience miscarriage or stillbirth, while healthy individuals could have symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The recalled products were distributed in Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx, and other New York City areas through retail stores and distributors. Affected items are packaged in 5-pound white plastic tubs labeled with Made Fresh Salads branding, with expiration dates through February 27, 2026, printed on the bottom left corner of the label.

The recall was initiated after routine company sampling detected Listeria monocytogenes in a production mixer. The company has removed the affected equipment from service. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

title: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets Recalled for Low Thiamine, One Illness Reported
headline: Go Raw LLC Recalls Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets Due to Low Thiamine Levels
description: Go Raw LLC recalled one lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag, after testing found low thiamine and one illness was reported.
productDescription: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
reason: May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Go Raw LLC has issued a voluntary recall of a single lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10 oz bag, due to potentially low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1). According to the FDA notice, this recall follows one confirmed illness report associated with the product, and testing showed thiamine levels below requirements for cats.

Thiamine is essential for feline health. Deficiency may cause symptoms such as decreased appetite, salivation, vomiting, failure to grow, and weight loss. Advanced deficiency can result in neurological symptoms like ventroflexion (neck bending), mental dullness, vision changes, unsteady walking, circling, falling, and seizures. Early veterinary care is important, as thiamine deficiency is typically reversible with prompt treatment.

ProductSizeLot CodeUPCBest Buy DateDistribution States
Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets10 oz bagC252886-91730-18103-110/15/2027CO, UT, WA, OR, PA, RI, MI, CA, TX, IL

The recalled product is packaged in a beige zip-lock bag with a purple stripe, with the lot code and best buy date on the front. The recall was announced on February 17, 2026, and applies to retail stores in the listed states.

title: Advanced Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein Recalled for Excess 7-Hydroxymitragynine
headline: Shaman Botanicals Recalls Advanced Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein for Undeclared 7-OH Levels
description: Shaman Botanicals, LLC recalled one lot of Advanced Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein due to excess 7-Hydroxymitragynine. No adverse events have been reported.
reason: Product contains 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) in an amount more than the declared value of 7.5 mg/tablet.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Shaman Botanicals, LLC has voluntarily recalled one lot of its Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein dietary supplement due to an undeclared concentration of 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) exceeding the labeled amount of 7.5 mg per tablet. This recall affects products distributed nationwide to wholesalers, retailers, and consumers through online sales.

Consumers using the affected product may ingest a higher dose of 7-Hydroxymitragynine than intended, which could potentially lead to adverse health effects. To date, Shaman Botanicals, LLC has not received any reports of adverse events related to the recalled lot.

The recall applies to Lot B# AAW.501.3 of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein, regardless of expiration date. The product is sold in various packaging formats identified by their UPC codes and lot number printed on the package or bottle bottom.

Product NameLot NumberUPC Code
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (2-count bag)Lot B# AAW.501.3810057763724
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (20-count bag)Lot B# AAW.501.3810057763830
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (30-count bottle)Lot B# AAW.501.3810057763779

For questions or to arrange returns, contact Shaman Botanicals, LLC during business hours (Monday through Friday, 9am to 5pm CST) at:

Vince Sanders, Quality Department
1501 Iron Street
North Kansas City, MO 64116
Phone: 855-427-7386
Email: Quality@CBDAmericanShaman.com
Recall information and registration: https://recall.cbdamericanshaman.com/

Consumers who experience any adverse health issues related to this product should consult their healthcare provider. Adverse reactions or quality problems may also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program using one of the following methods:

  • Submit online at: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular mail or fax: Download the reporting form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form, then mail to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.text: Consumers should check if they have Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein, Lot B# AAW.501.3, and follow the recall instructions provided by Shaman Botanicals, LLC.

The recall applies to Lot B# AAW.501.3 of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein, regardless of expiration date. The product is sold in various packaging formats identified by their UPC codes and lot number printed on the package or bottle bottom.

Product NameLot NumberUPC Code

Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (20-count bag)Lot B# AAW.501.3810057763830
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (30-count bottle)Lot B# AAW.501.3810057763779

For questions or to arrange returns, contact Shaman Botanicals, LLC during business hours (Monday through Friday, 9am to 5pm CST) at:

Vince Sanders, Quality Department
1501 Iron Street
North Kansas City, MO 64116
Phone: 855-427-7386
Email: Quality@CBDAmericanShaman.com
Recall information and registration: https://recall.cbdamericanshaman.com/

Consumers who experience any adverse health issues related to this product should consult their healthcare provider. Adverse reactions or quality problems may also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program using one of the following methods:

  • Submit online at: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular mail or fax: Download the reporting form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form, then mail to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Rosabella Moringa Capsules Recalled for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Ambrosia Brands Recalls Rosabella Moringa Capsules Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Ambrosia Brands, LLC is recalling specific lots of Rosabella Moringa Capsules after possible Salmonella contamination. FDA issued the recall in February 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Ambrosia Brands, LLC of New York, NY, is voluntarily recalling certain lots of Rosabella Moringa Capsules due to potential Salmonella contamination. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Severe cases can result in more serious health complications.

The recall applies to Rosabella Moringa Capsules in 60-count bottles, distributed nationwide through the company's website and TikTok Shop since February 2025. The FDA notes concern about unauthorized sales on third-party platforms such as eBay and Shein. Consumers who purchased from Amazon.com are advised to check lot numbers, although affected lots were not sold there.

As of the FDA notice, seven illnesses and three hospitalizations in the U.S. have been reported in connection with this recall. Ambrosia Brands has stopped sourcing the implicated moringa leaf powder and is cooperating with the FDA and CDC in the ongoing investigation.

title: Green Lumber Dietary Supplement Recall for Undeclared Tadalafil
headline: Green Lumber Recalls Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared Prescription Drug Tadalafil
description: Green Lumber Holding, LLC recalled certain dietary supplements after FDA found undeclared tadalafil. Counterfeit products may pose health risks, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Green Lumber Holding, LLC has issued an updated consumer alert regarding a recall of certain dietary supplements falsely marketed as Green Lumber products. The recall, originally announced on August 28, 2025, follows the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) detection of tadalafil—an undeclared prescription drug—in some products labeled as Green Lumber. Tadalafil is not an ingredient in authentic Green Lumber products, and its presence can pose significant health risks.

The FDA's February 9, 2026 Enforcement Report referenced this recall. Green Lumber's investigation determined that a former employee misused genuine packaging and customer information to distribute counterfeit or adulterated products. Only products with lot number LOT308EXP03/28 are identified as counterfeit and subject to recall.

Authentic Green Lumber products have specific lot numbers not affected by this recall. Consumers are advised to verify lot numbers to confirm authenticity. Use of counterfeit products containing undeclared tadalafil may result in adverse health effects, particularly for individuals taking medications for heart disease or blood pressure. The FDA notice references adverse events associated with these products.

Lot NumberExpiration Date
LOTGL0130151EXP092609/26
GL01EXP09/2709/27
GL110725EXP11072711/07/27

The following lot number is the only one subject to recall due to being identified as counterfeit and containing undeclared tadalafil:

Lot NumberExpiration Date
LOT308EXP03/2803/28

Consumers are encouraged to report suspected counterfeit products to Green Lumber and the FDA's MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program. Green Lumber has taken steps to strengthen safeguards and is cooperating with the FDA and law enforcement.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately check their Green Lumber dietary supplements for lot number LOT308EXP03/28. If found, discontinue use and discard the product as directed in the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Green Lumber Holding, LLC | 949.426.8622 | consumer-safety@greenlumber.com

title: Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana Baby Food Recalled for Elevated Patulin Levels
headline: IF Copack, LLC Recalls Tippy Toes Baby Food Due to Elevated Patulin
description: IF Copack, LLC is recalling one lot of Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana baby food after FDA testing found elevated patulin levels. No illnesses reported.
companyName: IF Copack, LLC. d.b.a. Initiative Foods

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
IF Copack, LLC d.b.a. Initiative Foods has issued a recall for one lot of Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana Fruit puree baby food due to elevated levels of patulin, a mycotoxin produced by mold that can grow on fruit. According to the FDA, long-term exposure to patulin may cause immune suppression, nerve damage, headache, fever, and nausea. No illnesses or injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

The affected product was distributed nationwide in the United States, except Alaska, and may have reached Guam and Puerto Rico. The recall was initiated after the FDA's Total Diet Study detected patulin levels above the expected threshold. Initiative Foods is collaborating with the FDA to remove the affected lot from the market.

Product NamePackaging FormatUPCLot Number / Expiration DatePackage Code Identifier
Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana2-pack plastic tubs, with product information sleeve036800 265783Lot # 07174 / Best By Date: BB 07/17/2026INIA0120

content.text: Consumers should check their Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana baby food for lot #07174 with Best By 07/17/2026 and stop using it if matched. Dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase as directed by the recall notice.

title: Junebar Snack Bars Recalled for Undeclared Milk and Soy Allergens
headline: Junebar Snack Bars Recalled Due to Undeclared Milk and Soy, FDA Notice 2026
description: Juniper Granola, LLC. recalled select Junebar Snack Bars after undeclared milk and soy allergens were found. FDA published the recall in February 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on February 12, 2026, Juniper Granola, LLC. of Rochester, NY, has recalled certain Junebar snack bars due to undeclared milk and soy allergens. The recall affects Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Junebars with batch labels L1300, L1300A, L1300B, or L1301A. People with allergies or severe sensitivities to milk or soy risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume these products.

The affected snack bars were distributed in New York State in January 2026 through grocery stores and direct website orders. The presence of undeclared allergens was discovered after a customer reported an allergic reaction, leading to an internal investigation. The issue was traced to a sourcing error involving chocolate chips containing milk and soy.

No other Junebar products are affected by this recall. At the time of the FDA notice, the company reported the recall was initiated after one allergic reaction was reported.

ProductBatch LabelsDistribution AreaDistribution Date
Chocolate Cherry JunebarL1300, L1300A, L1300B, L1301ANew York StateJanuary 2026
Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip JunebarL1300, L1300A, L1300B, L1301ANew York StateJanuary 2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the batch label on the back of their Junebar Snack Bars. If the batch is L1300, L1300A, L1300B, or L1301A, do not eat the product if you have milk or soy allergies. Return affected bars to the place of purchase for a refund, as directed in the FDA recall notice.

content.text: Consumer support: (315) 226-3339 | contact@junebars.com

title: Wellsley Farms Salmon Recalled for Listeria Risk at BJ's Wholesale Club (2026)
headline: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. Recalls Wellsley Farms Salmon Due to Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. recalled one lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon sold at BJ's Wholesale Club due to possible Listeria monocytogenes. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. has recalled one lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected salmon was sold at BJ's Wholesale Club stores in Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia between January 31 and February 7, 2026.

The recalled product is a 2-pound bag labeled "Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon" with UPC code 888670025963 and lot number 3896. The recall was initiated after a random FDA sample tested positive for Listeria monocytogenes.

Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Listeria infection can be more serious for pregnant women, potentially causing miscarriages or stillbirths. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.
