title: TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems Labeling Correction Recall by Trividia Health, Inc.
headline: Labeling Correction Recall for TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems Due to E-5 Error Code Instructions
description: Trividia Health, Inc. is recalling TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems to update labeling instructions for E-5 error codes after reports of serious injuries and one fatality.
reason: As currently written, the Owner's Booklets/System Instructions for Use fails to emphasize that users must seek medical attention immediately if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high glucose.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a labeling correction for all TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems, including TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, and TRUE METRIX PRO models. This update affects devices distributed in the United States, United Kingdom, Mexico, Australia, and the Caribbean. The correction addresses Owner's Booklets/System Instructions for Use that do not sufficiently instruct users to seek immediate medical attention if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high blood glucose.

The E-5 error code may indicate a very high blood glucose reading (greater than 600 mg/dL) or a test strip error. According to the FDA notice, since the product launch in August 2014, there have been 114 reported serious injuries and one fatality associated with this error code. Without clear instructions, users may delay necessary medical treatment, which can result in serious health consequences or death.

Trividia Health has updated the instructions to advise users to retest with a new test strip and seek medical help immediately if symptoms such as fatigue, excessive urination, thirst, or blurry vision persist with an E-5 error. Notices are being distributed to pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies. The devices themselves do not require return or replacement, and users may continue normal use. Adverse events may be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

ProductDescriptionRegions AffectedCustomer ContactSupport Phone
TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, TRUE METRIX PROBlood Glucose Monitoring Systems requiring updated Owner's Booklets/System Instructions for UseUnited States, United Kingdom, Mexico, Australia, Caribbeanwww.trividiahealth.com/E-5productnotice1-888-835-2723 (Mon-Fri, 8AM-8PM EST)
1-800-803-6025 (Mon-Fri, 8AM-8PM EST for E-5 assistance)

Updated Instructions for E-5 Error Code for TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, and TRUE METRIX PRO:

DisplayReasonRecommended Action
E-5 Error CodeVery high blood glucose (>600 mg/dL) or test strip error
  • Retest with a new test strip.
  • If error persists and symptoms such as fatigue, excess urination, thirst, or blurry vision are present, seek medical attention immediately.
  • If no symptoms, retest with a new strip again.
  • If error continues without symptoms, call 1-800-803-6025 for assistance.

How to Report Adverse Events to FDA MedWatch:

  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download and complete forms from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form.
  • Return completed form to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178.

This labeling correction involves updates to the Owner's Booklets provided with the devices as well as online instructions. Trividia Health is working with the FDA and other regulatory bodies to ensure user safety and awareness.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers using TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems should review the updated instructions for E-5 error codes and follow the revised guidance. Contact Trividia Health at 1-888-835-2723 for support if needed.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Trividia Health Customer Care Department | 1-888-835-2723
Media: Annmarie Ramos | | ext. 3190 | (800) 342-7226

title: No Brand Dried Croaker Fish Recalled by Prime Food Processing LLC for Inadequate Evisceration
headline: Prime Food Processing LLC Recalls No Brand Dried Croaker Fish Due to Potential Bacterial or Toxin Risk
description: Prime Food Processing LLC recalled No Brand Dried Croaker Fish after FDA found the product was not adequately eviscerated, posing a potential health risk. No illnesses reported.
reason: Product was not adequately eviscerated and may harbor harmful bacteria or toxins

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prime Food Processing LLC has issued a voluntary recall of 80 cases of Dried Croaker Fish due to inadequate evisceration. The fish may contain internal organs that harbor harmful bacteria or toxins, posing a potential health risk to consumers. This action was taken in compliance with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations after the issue was identified through routine testing by the New York Department of Agriculture.

The affected product was imported from Vietnam and packaged in clear packaging with green trim. The recall specifically concerns products distributed to Asian grocery stores across multiple states including AL, AZ, CA, FL, GA, HI, ID, MA, MD, MO, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OK, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, and WI.

No illnesses or adverse reactions linked to this product have been reported, according to the FDA notice.

Item NumberProductLot CodeExpiration DatePackaging Description
AF1410Dried Croaker Fish2512812-28-27Clear Packaging with Green Trim
  • Distribution: Asian grocery stores in AL, AZ, CA, FL, GA, HI, ID, MA, MD, MO, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OK, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, and WI

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat the recalled No Brand Dried Croaker Fish. Check packaging for the affected lot and return it to the place of purchase if identified.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Prime Food Processing LLC | 718-963-2323

title: CHIPS AHOY Baked Brownie Bites Recalled for Choking Hazard, No Injuries Reported
headline: Mondelez Global Recalls CHIPS AHOY Baked Brownie Bites Due to Choking Risk
description: Mondelez Global has recalled select CHIPS AHOY Baked Brownie Bites due to a choking hazard from corn starch clumps. No injuries or illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelez Global LLC has expanded its voluntary recall of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products in the United States. This expansion includes an additional code date of 10MAY2026 and two more UPCs for product pouches within the recalled carton units. The recall addresses a product safety concern resulting from an incorrect mixing process that caused small corn starch clumps to form in the baked brownie bites.

These small starch clumps pose a choking hazard, particularly for vulnerable groups such as young children and the elderly. The recall is limited to CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products with specific "Best When Used By" dates listed below. Mondelez Global LLC confirms that no other CHIPS AHOY! or company products are affected by this recall.

No injuries or illnesses have been reported to date, according to the official FDA notice.

Product DescriptionRetail UPCCase GTINBest When Used By Dates

7.0 oz CA! BAKED BITES BROOKIE 5CT 12440000856501004400008565710MAY2026, 12MAY2026, 18MAY2026
1.4 oz CA! BAKED BITES BROOKIE POUCH4400008566N/A10MAY2026, 12MAY2026, 18MAY2026

content.text: Consumers should check the UPC and best by dates of their CHIPS AHOY Baked Brownie Bites and refrain from consuming affected products. Contact Mondelez Global for further guidance.

title: Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support Recalled for Undeclared Tadalafil
headline: Akkarco LLC Recalls Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support Due to Hidden Tadalafil
description: Akkarco LLC is recalling Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support after FDA found undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug, in the supplement.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Akkarco LLC of Lorton, Virginia, has voluntarily recalled its Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support due to the presence of undeclared tadalafil. Tadalafil is an FDA-approved prescription medication for erectile dysfunction and is not permitted in dietary supplements. The safety and effectiveness of tadalafil in this product have not been established.

The recalled honey-based supplement was distributed nationwide, including through online retailers. The presence of tadalafil may pose health risks, especially for individuals with certain medical conditions or those taking medications that could interact with tadalafil. Potential adverse effects include cardiovascular complications, changes in blood pressure, dizziness, and headache.

As of the FDA recall announcement on February 3, 2026, no confirmed adverse health events have been reported in connection with this product.

Product NameUPCBatch NumberExpiration Date
ASHFIAT ALHARAMAIN1234561870003ENGOT24October 2028

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support and check if their product matches the recalled batch. Discontinue use and contact Akkarco LLC for further guidance as directed in the FDA recall notice.

content.text:

Media: Shahzaib Amin Malik

title: Neogen Recalls Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution for Microbial Contamination
headline: Neogen Recalls Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution Nationwide Due to Microbial Contamination
description: Neogen Corporation has recalled all lots of Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution after microbial contamination was found. No injuries reported.
productDescription: Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On January 29, 2026, Neogen Corporation announced a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots within expiry of Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution. This veterinary product, distributed to animal health professionals across the United States, is used for topical wound management in dogs, cats, and horses. The recall was initiated after microbial contamination was detected in certain lots, compromising product sterility.

The FDA notice states that while adverse events have been reported in horses following intraarticular injection, no adverse events have been reported when the product is used as labeled for topical wound care. As a precaution, all 2 mL/20 mg vials are included in the recall while the investigation continues. No injuries or illnesses have been reported when used as directed.

Product VariantLot NumbersUPC Code
2 mL / 20 mg vials0236735, 0236736, 0336746, 0336747, 0536760, 0536761, 0636768, 0636769, 0836792, 0836785, 0836788, 0836789, 0936794, 0936795, 1036801, 1036802, 1036803, 1036804, 1136807, 1136808, 0246837, 0246838, 4L001B, 4L002726087089386
10 mL / 50 mg vials0136731, 0736777, 0346843, 5A001726087089393

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No other Neogen products are affected. Veterinarians are encouraged to report any adverse events related to this product to the FDA using Form FDA 1932a.

facts.product: Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution

content.text: Consumers and veterinarians should check the lot numbers of their Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution and discontinue use if affected. For questions or returns, contact Neogen Corporation as directed in the official FDA notice.

title: Why Not Natural Recalls Organic Moringa – Green Superfood for Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Organic Moringa – Green Superfood by Why Not Natural Recalled Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Why Not Natural has recalled its Organic Moringa – Green Superfood capsules nationwide after FDA findings of potential Salmonella contamination. See recall details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Why Not Natural, based in Houston, Texas, has initiated a voluntary recall of its Organic Moringa – Green Superfood due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, infection typically results in symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may include blood), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The affected product consists of Moringa Capsules, which were distributed nationwide via mail orders through Why Not Natural's own e-commerce platform as well as third-party online retailers, including Amazon.com. Sales occurred between July 2025 and January 2026. This recall follows observations by the FDA related to an outbreak investigation involving Salmonella contamination in other products containing moringa powder.

Production and distribution of the Moringa Capsules have been suspended while the FDA and Why Not Natural continue their investigation. The company has notified its customers about the recall. The FDA notice references adverse events associated with this recall.

ProductLot NumberExpiration Date
Why Not Natural Organic Moringa – Green Superfood (120 capsule bottle)A25G05107/2028

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately check if their Organic Moringa – Green Superfood matches the recalled lot and stop using it if affected, as advised in the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This summary is based on a U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) recall posting dated January 28, 2026. For the authoritative text, use the official FDA link below.

title: Fu Zhou Fish Balls Recalled for Undeclared Wheat and Sesame Allergens
headline: Koikoi Trading Inc. Recalls Fu Zhou Fish Balls Due to Undeclared Wheat and Sesame
description: Koikoi Trading Inc. recalled Fu Zhou Fish Balls after undeclared wheat and sesame allergens were found. No illnesses reported per FDA notice in January 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published January 28, 2026, Koikoi Trading Inc. has recalled specific lots of Fu Zhou Fish Balls due to undeclared wheat and sesame allergens. These allergens are not listed on the product packaging, posing a risk to individuals with wheat or sesame allergies. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date.

The recalled products were distributed through foodservice and e-commerce channels, including restaurant deliveries, distributors, and online orders. Distribution areas include California, Washington, Oregon, New York, Florida, Texas, New Jersey, Illinois, Massachusetts, Georgia, Arizona, Nevada, and Colorado. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the ingredient and allergen statements did not declare wheat and sesame. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Pack SizeUPCItem #Lot CodeBest By / Expiration DateRecall Scope
200g (Consumer pack)810093898662701008/12/202508/11/2027All units within 24-month shelf life
400g (Consumer pack)810093891312755009/02/202509/01/2027All units within 24-month shelf life
5LB (Foodservice pack, 4/5LB)810093891322753011/11/202511/12/2027All units within 24-month shelf life

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Fu Zhou Fish Balls from Koikoi Trading Inc. should check packaging for undeclared wheat or sesame. If affected, discontinue use and contact Koikoi Trading Inc. for further guidance.

content.text: Consumer support: Koikoi Trading Inc. | 323-588-9888

title: Gerber Recalls Select Arrowroot Biscuits for Potential Soft Plastic or Paper Contamination
headline: Gerber Voluntarily Recalls Arrowroot Biscuits Due to Possible Soft Plastic and Paper Pieces
description: Gerber has recalled specific batches of Arrowroot Biscuits after supplier issues led to possible soft plastic or paper contamination. No injuries reported.
reason: potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Gerber Products Company has voluntarily recalled limited batches of Gerber® Arrowroot Biscuits due to the potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces. This action follows a recall by the arrowroot flour supplier. Gerber has discontinued use of the affected supplier.

The recall applies to Gerber® Arrowroot Biscuits in 5.5oz packages produced between July 2025 and September 2025. No other Gerber® products are affected. The recall is nationwide in the United States. No illnesses or injuries have been reported as of the FDA announcement.

Consumers are advised to check the batch codes and best before dates on the product packaging to determine if their product is included in the recall.

title: Navitas Organics Recalls Select 8oz Organic Chia Seeds for Potential Salmonella Risk
headline: Navitas Organics Issues Recall of 8oz Organic Chia Seeds Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
description: Navitas Organics has recalled specific lots of its 8oz Organic Chia Seeds due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Navitas Organics has voluntarily recalled select lots of its 8oz Organic Chia Seeds after potential Salmonella contamination was identified. This recall is a precautionary measure following a similar action by the company's supplier. The affected products were distributed nationwide through retailers such as Whole Foods Market and Target, as well as online platforms including Amazon, iHerb, and Vitacost.com.

The recall is limited to specific lot codes and expiration dates of the 8oz Organic Chia Seeds listed below. According to the FDA notice, there have been no reports of Salmonella infections, illnesses, or adverse events associated with these products. No other Navitas Organics products are included in this recall.

Salmonella can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can become more severe if it spreads beyond the intestines.

Navitas Organics is working with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on this recall. The company urges consumers to review the lot codes and take appropriate action as outlined in the official notice.

title: IKM Recalls Metal Cookware Items for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: IKM Metal Cookware Recall Due to Lead Contamination Risk
description: IKM has recalled select metal cookware items after FDA testing found elevated lead levels, which may pose a foodborne illness risk. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, IKM of San Jose, California, has recalled specific metal cookware items due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead (Pb). Lead exposure from these products can pose significant health risks, especially to children and pregnant individuals. Even low levels of lead can cause serious health effects, including developmental delays and neurological changes.

The recall was initiated after routine FDA sampling detected unsafe lead levels in certain aluminum and brass cookware items. The affected products were distributed to grocery stores in several California cities, including Sunnyvale, Santa Clara, Fremont, and Hayward. IKM has halted distribution of the impacted cookware and is investigating the source of contamination. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses related to the recalled products have been reported.

ProductDescriptionQuantity Recalled
A-cook Aluminum Kadai size 5Silver-toned wok-style vessel with a wide, curved basin and no long handle43 pcs
Brass TopeStandard stock pot with a bright, golden finish10 pcs
IKM 4-quart Pital Brass PotDeep-bodied brass pot with a narrow mouth and a dull or matte golden appearance9 pcs
IKM Aluminum Saucepan wooden handle 9"Silver aluminum body with a 9-inch diameter and wooden handle56 pcs

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date, according to the official notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their IKM metal cookware matches the recalled products and stop using affected items. For further information, contact IKM as directed in the FDA notice.

title: Live it Up Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Pouches Recalled for Salmonella Risk
headline: Live it Up Super Greens Recalled Nationwide Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Superfoods, Inc. (Live it Up) recalled Super Greens Original and Wild Berry pouches and stick packs due to possible Salmonella contamination, with illnesses reported.
productDescription: Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs
companyName: Superfoods, Inc. DBA as Live it Up

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Superfoods, Inc., doing business as Live it Up, has issued a recall for certain batches of its Live it Up Super Greens dietary supplements due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall affects both Original and Wild Berry flavored products in pouch and stick pack forms. The company identified a possible health risk stemming from Salmonella, which can lead to serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis. According to the FDA notice, there have been 45 reported illnesses and 12 hospitalizations in the United States linked to this outbreak.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide in the U.S., including Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands, and have also been sold in the United Kingdom. Sales were made through Live it Up's direct-to-consumer website starting September 2024 and Amazon.com starting July 30, 2025. There is also possible unauthorized resale through third-party platforms such as eBay and Walmart.

ProductNet WeightUPC
Live it Up Super Greens Original (pouch)8.5 oz (240g)860013190804
Live it Up Super Greens Original, 30 - 0.28 oz (8g) stick packs8.47 oz (240g)850077468063
Live it Up Super Greens Wild Berry (pouch)9.49 oz (269.16g)860013190811
Live it Up Super Greens Wild Berry, 30 - 0.32 oz (9g) stick packs9.52 oz (270g)850077468070

The recalled products feature green packaging, with lot codes printed in black on the bottom back of the package. Original pouch products have lot codes beginning with "A," and stick pack products have lot codes beginning with "3." Expiration dates on the recalled items range from 08/2026 to 01/2028.

Production of the affected product has been suspended while the FDA and the company continue their investigation. No other Live it Up products are involved in this recall at this time.

facts.company: Superfoods, Inc. DBA as Live it Up
facts.product: Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs

content.text: If you have Live it Up Super Greens Original or Wild Berry pouches or stick packs, check the lot code and expiration date. Stop using affected products and contact Live it Up at recall@artofenso.com for refund instructions as directed in the FDA notice.

content.text: Consumer support: recall@artofenso.com

title: Genova Yellowfin Tuna Recalled by Tri-Union Seafoods for Potential Clostridium Botulinum Contamination
headline: Tri-Union Seafoods Recalls Additional Genova Yellowfin Tuna Due to Botulism Risk
description: Tri-Union Seafoods expanded its recall of Genova Yellowfin Tuna after previously recalled products were distributed, citing potential Clostridium botulinum contamination. See FDA notice for details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Tri-Union Seafoods has expanded its recall of Genova Yellowfin Tuna products due to the inadvertent distribution of previously recalled items. The initial recall, issued in February 2025, was related to a manufacturing defect in the pull tab can lids, which may compromise the product seal and increase the risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a potentially serious foodborne pathogen.

The company identified that quarantined cases from the initial recall were mistakenly shipped by a third-party distributor to select retailers in several states. Impacted retailers include Meijer (Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin), Giant Foods (Maryland, Virginia), and Safeway, Albertsons, Vons, and Pavilions (California).

The FDA notice advises consumers not to eat the recalled tuna, even if it appears normal, due to the risk of botulism. Anyone experiencing illness after consuming the product should seek medical attention. The recall includes the following products:

Description UPC Can Code Best if Used By Date
Genova Yellowfin Tuna in Olive Oil 5.0 oz 4 Pack 4800073265 S84N D2L 1/21/2028
Genova Yellowfin Tuna in Olive Oil 5.0 oz 4 Pack 4800073265 S84N D3L 1/24/2028
Genova Yellowfin Tuna in Extra Virgin Olive Oil with Sea Salt 5.0 oz 4800013275 S88N D1M 1/17/2028

The FDA notice references adverse events related to this recall. For further details, refer to the official FDA recall announcement.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Genova Yellowfin Tuna products for the listed UPCs and can codes. If affected, do not consume the product due to potential Clostridium botulinum contamination.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Tri-Union Seafoods | 833-374-0171 | support@thaiunionhelp.zendesk.com
Media: Media.Inquiries@thaiunion.com

title: Olympus Expands Recall of All ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles Due to Device Defect
headline: Olympus Recalls All Lots of ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles for Safety Concerns
description: Olympus has expanded its recall to include all ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles after reports of device defects. See FDA notice for details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Olympus Corporation has expanded its voluntary recall of ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles to include all lots due to safety concerns. The recall follows reports of device components ejecting or detaching during procedures. The initial recall in August 2025 was limited to certain lots, but further investigation identified risks affecting the entire product line.

Potential hazards include the risk of unintended foreign objects remaining in the tracheobronchial tree, which may require removal, and issues from degradation of the device's heat-shrink material, such as difficulty in sample extraction, fluid leakage, impaired needle operation, or breakage. No mention of injuries or adverse events was provided in the FDA notice.

The ViziShot 2 FLEX is used with ultrasound endoscopes for fine needle aspiration and biopsy of lesions in the tracheobronchial tree. Other EBUS-TBNA needles are not affected by this recall.

Product
ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles (all lots)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have Olympus ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles, check if your product is affected by the FDA recall. If impacted, contact Olympus for further guidance as directed in the official recall notice.

content.text: Consumer support: Olympus Technical Assistance Center (TAC) | 1-800-848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com

title: Spring & Mulberry Recalls Multiple Chocolate Bar Flavors for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Spring & Mulberry Expands Recall of Chocolate Bars Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Spring & Mulberry has recalled several chocolate bar flavors after routine testing found possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Spring & Mulberry expanded its voluntary recall of select chocolate bar flavors due to potential Salmonella contamination. The recall, which began with the Mint Leaf flavor, now includes additional varieties produced on the same equipment and during the same timeframe. The risk was identified after third-party testing detected Salmonella in finished products.

Salmonella infection may cause serious illness, especially in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms can include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe. The FDA states that no confirmed illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported to date in connection with these products.

The recalled chocolate bars were sold online and at select retail partners nationwide beginning September 15, 2025. Affected products can be identified by brand, flavor, lot code, and box color. Lot codes are printed on the back of the packaging and on the inner flow wrap.

FlavorLot CodeBox Color
Mint LeafML1025Green
Dark Chocolate Sea SaltDCSS1025Blue
Hazelnut CrunchHC1025Brown
Orange ZestOZ1025Orange

Consumers may request a refund or replacement by contacting Spring & Mulberry and providing a photo of the product's lot code, as detailed in the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Spring & Mulberry chocolate bars for the listed flavors and lot codes. Do not consume affected products; follow FDA recall instructions for disposal or replacement.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: recalls@springandmulberry.com

title: Abbott Recalls Select FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose Sensors for Incorrect Low Readings
headline: Abbott Recalls Certain FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose Monitoring Sensors Due to Low Reading Issue
description: Abbott is recalling specific FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus glucose sensors in the U.S. after internal testing found some may give incorrect low glucose readings.
productDescription: FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors
reason: Internal testing determined that some sensors may provide incorrect low glucose readings.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Abbott has initiated a medical device correction for certain FreeStyle Libre 3 and FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus glucose monitoring sensors in the United States. Internal testing identified that some sensors from one production line may provide incorrect low glucose readings, which could lead to inappropriate treatment decisions and potential health risks for people with diabetes.

The manufacturing issue has been resolved, and Abbott continues to supply sensors for replacements and new orders. Approximately 3 million sensors in the U.S. are affected, with about half already expired or used. Other FreeStyle Libre sensors, readers, apps, and Abbott biowearable products are not impacted by this correction.

Impacted ProductDetails
FreeStyle Libre 3 SensorsSubset from one production line; approximately 3 million devices in the U.S.
FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus SensorsSubset from one production line; included in the approximately 3 million devices in the U.S.

No injuries or adverse events have been reported as of the FDA notice date.

facts.product: FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should visit www.FreeStyleCheck.com to check if their FreeStyle Libre 3 or 3 Plus sensor is affected. If so, discontinue use and request a free replacement as directed by Abbott.

content.text: Consumer support: Abbott's Customer Service | 1-833-815-4273

title: Ambriola Company Recalls Multiple Cheese Brands for Listeria Risk
headline: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member's Mark, Pinna, and Boar's Head Cheese Recalled for Listeria Contamination
description: The Ambriola Company has recalled select cheese products nationwide due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
brandName: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member's Mark, Pinna, and Boar's Head
brandNames: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member's Mark, Pinna, and Boar's Head
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, The Ambriola Company has recalled certain cheese products after routine testing detected Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage and stillbirth.

The recalled cheese products were distributed nationwide to retail stores and distributors between November 3, 2025, and November 20, 2025. The recall includes products processed at the West Caldwell, New Jersey facility. No illnesses related to these products have been reported as of the FDA notice.

BrandProduct DescriptionLot NumbersExpiration DatesUPCDistribution Dates
AmbriolaCheese (Retail Exact Weight)Varies by packageVariesVariable Random WeightsNov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
LocatelliCheese (Retail Exact Weight)Varies by packageVariesVariable Random WeightsNov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Member's MarkCheese (Retail Exact Weight)Varies by packageVariesVariable Random WeightsNov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
PinnaCheese (Retail Exact Weight)Varies by packageVariesVariable Random WeightsNov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Boar's HeadCheese (Retail Exact Weight)Varies by packageVariesVariable Random WeightsNov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
VariousBulk Cheese ProductsVariesVariesN/ANov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025

The Ambriola Company is cooperating with the FDA and has suspended production and distribution of the affected products while reviewing food safety protocols.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their cheese products for the affected brands and distribution dates. Do not consume recalled products; dispose of them or return to the place of purchase as directed by the FDA notice.

title: Diva Fam Inc. Recalls True Sea Moss Gel Superfood Due to Potential Botulism Risk
headline: True Sea Moss Gel Superfood in 16 FL OZ Jars Recalled for Possible Botulism Hazard
description: Diva Fam Inc. has recalled all flavors of True Sea Moss Gel Superfood (16 FL OZ jars) due to potential risk of botulism. No illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: Sea Moss Gel Superfood in various flavors (16 FL OZ glass jars)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Diva Fam Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of all lots and flavors of its True Sea Moss Gel Superfood products in 16 FL OZ (473 mL) glass jars. The recall is due to a lack of required regulatory authorization and temperature monitoring records for pH-controlled foods, which may allow microbial growth and the production of toxins that can cause botulism, a rare but serious illness.

Symptoms of botulism may include weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, and in severe cases, muscle weakness or breathing difficulty. According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse effects have been reported to date. The recalled products were distributed nationwide through select retailers and online at truеsеamоss.cоm.

The recall covers all flavors and batch numbers of True Sea Moss Gel Superfood manufactured before January 9, 2026. The manufacture date is printed on the jar lid in MM/YYYY format. This action follows a California Department of Public Health inspection regarding regulatory status and production records. Diva Fam Inc. is cooperating with authorities and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Karison Recalls Panjiri, Pinni, and Laddoo for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Karison Foods & Snacks Inc Issues Recall of Panjiri, Pinni, and Laddoo Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Karison Foods & Snacks Inc recalled select Panjiri, pinni, and laddoo products for undeclared milk allergen. No illnesses have been reported per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Karison Foods & Snacks Inc of Port Washington, NY, has recalled 12 oz containers of PANJIRI, ALSI PINNI, PUNJABI PINNI, BESAN LADDOO, and NO SUGAR ADDED BESAN LADDOO due to undeclared milk allergen. These products were distributed in retail stores in New York, New Jersey, and Virginia.

The affected products are packaged in clear plastic containers with specific UPC codes and an expiration date of August 29, 2026. The undeclared milk allergen was discovered during a routine inspection. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date, as stated in the official FDA notice.

ProductContainer SizeUPC CodeExpiration Date
PANJIRI12 oz897307002437Aug 29, 2026
ALSI PINNI12 oz897307002512Aug 29, 2026
PUNJABI PINNI12 oz897307002154Aug 29, 2026
BESAN LADDOO12 oz897307002406Aug 29, 2026
NO SUGAR ADDED BESAN LADDOO12 oz897307002215Aug 29, 2026

People with a milk allergy or sensitivity risk a serious allergic reaction if they consume these products. The recall details and product information are attributed to the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased the recalled Karison Panjiri, pinni, or laddoo products should check the UPC and expiration date. If affected, do not consume and return to the place of purchase as directed in the FDA notice.

title: Spring & Mulberry Recalls Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Recall: Spring & Mulberry Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar Due to Potential Salmonella Risk
description: Spring & Mulberry is recalling its Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar, lot #025255, due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
keywords: Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar
productDescription: Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Spring & Mulberry has voluntarily recalled lot #025255 of its Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar (2.1 oz) due to potential Salmonella contamination. This action follows third-party laboratory testing that indicated the possible presence of Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may become severe.

The recalled product was distributed online and through select retailers nationwide beginning September 15, 2025. The affected bars are identified by the Spring & Mulberry brand, teal-colored box, Mint Leaf flavor, and lot code #025255, which appears on both the back of the box and the inner flow wrap. As of the FDA notice date, no illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported in connection with this product.

ProductLot NumberFlavorWeightBox Color
Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar025255Mint Leaf2.1 ozTeal

content.text: Consumers should check if they have the Spring & Mulberry Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar, lot #025255. If so, follow the FDA guidance for return or disposal of the product.

title: Outside the Breadbox Bread Crumbs Recalled for Undeclared Egg and Milk Allergens
headline: Recall: Outside the Breadbox Bread Crumbs May Contain Undeclared Egg and Milk
description: VH Foods Inc. is recalling Outside the Breadbox Bread Crumbs due to undeclared egg and milk allergens. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.
companyName: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Egg and Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, VH Foods Inc., doing business as Outside The Breadbox in Colorado Springs, CO, has recalled its 8 ounce packages of "Bread Crumbs" because they may contain undeclared egg and milk allergens. The recall applies to products distributed in Colorado retail stores and through mail orders from the company website between November 24, 2025, and December 1, 2025.

The recalled bread crumbs are packaged in clear stand-up pouches, 8 ounces each, with UPC code 873899001554 and an expiration date of May 20, 2026, printed on the bottom. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product labeling did not disclose the presence of egg and milk due to a temporary production and packaging issue. This recall is limited to a single production run.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to the undeclared allergens have been reported as of the FDA notice date.

title: HerbsForever LLC Recalls Dietary Supplements for Undeclared Wheat Allergen Risk
headline: Recall: HerbsForever Gastro Care and Hingwastika Supplements for Undeclared Wheat
description: HerbsForever LLC is recalling select Gastro Care and Hingwastika supplements due to undeclared wheat, posing a risk to those with wheat allergies. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Gastro Care capsule and Hingwastika powder and capsule dietary supplements

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
HerbsForever LLC of Los Angeles, California, has recalled certain dietary supplements due to undeclared wheat, as stated in the official FDA notice. The recall affects HerbsForever brand Hingwastik Churna Powder, Hingwastika Extract capsules, and Gastro Care capsules, distributed nationwide via mail order.

This action follows an FDA inspection at the manufacturing facility in India, which found that Hing (Ferula Asafoedita), an ingredient in these products, is dried with flour that may contain wheat. Individuals with wheat allergies face the risk of serious allergic reactions if they consume these products.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA's announcement. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Hingwastika Extract60 Veg capsules, 800 mg each807814001335133-14April-2029
Gastro Care90 Veg capsules, 800 mg each, white plastic bottle807814001243124-4January-2029

title: Silintan Dietary Supplement Capsules Recalled for Undeclared Meloxicam, FDA Warns
headline: Silintan Capsules Recalled Nationwide Due to Undeclared Meloxicam, FDA Notice
description: Silintan dietary supplement capsules are recalled nationwide after FDA found undeclared meloxicam, an unapproved drug. No adverse events have been reported.
productDescription: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches
reason: Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared meloxicam

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Silintan capsules, a dietary supplement marketed for relief of joint and body aches. FDA testing found the product contains undeclared meloxicam, a prescription NSAID not permitted in dietary supplements.

The FDA states that meloxicam is approved for osteoarthritis pain but is not allowed in supplements. The presence of meloxicam makes Silintan an unapproved new drug, and its safety and efficacy in this context have not been established.

Potential health risks from meloxicam exposure include blood clots, heart attack, stroke, hypersensitivity reactions, gastrointestinal bleeding, ulceration, perforation, and acute kidney injury, especially in elderly individuals. The FDA notes increased risk of dangerous drug interactions for those taking blood thinners or other NSAIDs.

No injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA's notice.

ProductDescriptionPackagingDistribution
Silintan capsulesDietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches25-count plastic bottles with blue labelsNationwide online distribution at 123herbals.com

Consumers with questions may contact Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC by email at hello@123herbals.com or by phone at 1-626-656-3202. Customers who purchased directly from 123Herbals.com may return product to:

Return Address
300 West Valley Blvd., P.O. Box 1925, Alhambra, CA 91803

Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

title: Modern Warrior Recalls 'Modern Warrior Ready' Dietary Supplement for Undeclared 1,4-DMAA, Aniracetam, and Tianeptine
headline: Recall: Modern Warrior Ready Dietary Supplement Contains Undeclared 1,4-DMAA, Aniracetam, and Tianeptine
description: Modern Warrior recalled all lots of its dietary supplement capsules after FDA testing found undeclared 1,4-DMAA, aniracetam, and tianeptine. Serious health risks are noted.
productDescription: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings
reason: Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared 1,4-DMAA and aniracetam, and tianeptine

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Modern Warrior has voluntarily recalled all lots of its dietary supplement "Modern Warrior Ready" after regulatory testing identified undeclared tianeptine, 1,4-DMAA, and aniracetam. The supplement was marketed for metabolism boosting, brain function improvement, and reducing cravings.

The FDA states that tianeptine is linked to serious health risks, including life-threatening events such as suicidal ideation or behavior in individuals aged 25 years and younger. Overdose symptoms may include confusion, seizures, drowsiness, dry mouth, and shortness of breath, which can be exacerbated by alcohol. Combining tianeptine with monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) antidepressants may result in severe complications, including stroke and death.

1,4-DMAA may cause elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular problems, such as heart attack, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. The product also contains synephrine, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure, especially when combined with caffeine, potentially causing adverse cardiovascular effects.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide online between April 2022 and December 8, 2025. Modern Warrior has stopped distribution, removed the product from sale, and quarantined remaining inventory.

Product NameDescriptionPackagingDistribution Dates
Modern Warrior ReadyDietary supplement capsules for metabolism boosting, brain function improvement, and cravings reductionBlack plastic bottle with black screw-top lid, black and gold tamper-evident shrink wrap, 60 capsules per bottleApril 2022 – December 8, 2025

The FDA notice references serious adverse events and health risks associated with the undeclared ingredients.

facts.product: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Modern Warrior Ready dietary supplement capsules and check their packaging for any identifying details. If the product matches the recall, discontinue use as advised in the FDA notice.

title: Klong Kone Shrimp Paste Recalled by Gusto Group Inc. for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Gusto Group Inc. Issues Recall of Klong Kone Shrimp Paste Due to Elevated Lead Levels
description: Gusto Group Inc. recalled Klong Kone Shrimp Paste (454g and 910g) after FDA testing found elevated lead. One illness report involving four children prompted the recall.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Gusto Group Inc. of Paterson, NJ, has recalled two sizes of Klong Kone Shrimp Paste due to the potential presence of elevated lead levels. The affected products are the 454 G. (1 LBS) and 910 G. (2 LBS) jars. Lead exposure can cause serious health issues, especially for children, including long-term neurological damage. Adults may also experience kidney problems, hypertension, and cognitive effects from chronic lead exposure.

The recalled shrimp paste was distributed between April 2023 and December 2023 to two retailers in Iowa and New Jersey. No online sales were reported. The product is packaged in a white plastic jar with a red cap and was packed and distributed by P. Prateepthong 2000 in Thailand.

The recall was initiated after one report involving four children who became ill with diarrhea and elevated blood lead levels following consumption of the product. FDA laboratory analysis confirmed the presence of unsafe lead levels. Gusto Group Inc. has stopped distribution and is investigating the source of contamination.

title: Primavera Recalls Select 4-Count Tamales for Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Primavera Nueva Inc. Recalls Tamales Due to Potential Listeria Risk
description: Primavera Nueva Inc. recalled select 4-count tamales after FDA found possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Primavera Nueva Inc. of Sonoma, CA, has voluntarily recalled select 4-count Primavera brand tamales due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. This organism can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women may face additional risks including miscarriage or stillbirth.

The recalled tamales were distributed to retail stores in California and Nevada. The recall was initiated after an FDA inspection on October 10, 2025, identified that internal cooking temperatures were not consistently verified during production, raising concerns about biological hazards. Primavera Nueva Inc. implemented enhanced cooking temperature monitoring as of October 11, 2025.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice.

title: Recall of Newway Import Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Newway Import Recalls Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Newway Import Inc. recalled Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil after discovering undeclared wheat, posing a risk to those with wheat allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Newway Import Inc., based in La Puente, California, has recalled "Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil" under the Cai Hua Xiang brand due to undeclared wheat. The presence of this allergen, not listed on the product label, may cause serious or life-threatening reactions in individuals with wheat allergies or sensitivities.

The affected product was distributed to retail grocery stores in California between early July 2025 and early November 2025. The recall was initiated after it was found that wheat was present but not declared on the packaging.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Product NameBrandLot CodeBest By DatePackaging Details
Preserved Mustard in Soybean OilCai Hua Xiang505202008May 20, 2026Packaging details not specified; lot code and best by date found on package exterior

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Newway Import Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil should check the packaging for the recalled product and discontinue use if present, especially if allergic to wheat.

title: Gold Star Distribution Recalls All FDA-Regulated Products for Salmonella and Contamination Risk
headline: Gold Star Distribution Recalls Drugs, Devices, Food, and Pet Food Due to Insanitary Conditions
description: Gold Star Distribution, Inc. recalled all FDA-regulated products held at its facility due to potential Salmonella, rodent, and avian contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food
productType: Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness
reason: Potential Salmonella contamination, presence of rodent and avian contamination and insanitary conditions during the storage process.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gold Star Distribution, Inc. has recalled all FDA-regulated products stored at its facility after the FDA identified potential Salmonella contamination and evidence of rodent and avian contamination, as well as insanitary storage conditions. The recall includes drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food under multiple brand names.

According to the official FDA notice, these conditions may expose products to bacterial contamination, posing health risks to both humans and animals. Salmonella infection can cause fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, with higher risk of severe illness in infants, elderly individuals, pregnant persons, and those with weakened immune systems. Animals consuming contaminated pet food may also be affected.

The recall does not include frozen or refrigerated products shipped directly to stores by distributors or manufacturers. As of the FDA notice date, no illnesses or adverse events related to these products have been reported.

facts.product: All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food
facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check any FDA-regulated products from Gold Star Distribution, Inc. for identifying information and follow the recall instructions. If affected, do not use the products and contact Gold Star Distribution, Inc. for further guidance.

title: Atwater's Recalls Cookie Tins for Undeclared Almonds, Pecans, and Walnuts
headline: Atwater's Cookie Tins Recalled Due to Undeclared Tree Nut Allergens
description: Atwater's has recalled 197 cookie tins distributed in Maryland due to undeclared almonds, pecans, and walnuts. No allergic reactions have been reported.
reason: Undeclared tree nut allergens, almonds, pecans, and walnuts.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Atwater's, based in Baltimore, MD, has issued a recall for 197 cookie tins due to undeclared tree nut allergens. The affected products contain almonds, pecans, and walnuts that were not disclosed on the product labels, posing a serious risk to individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these nuts.

The cookie tins were distributed between December 13, 2025, and December 22, 2025, in Baltimore, Towson, and Catonsville, MD. They were sold through retail stores and also gifted to three wholesalers. The tins are circular metal containers tied with a bow, marked with an "Atwater's cookie tin" label on the bottom.

The recall was voluntarily initiated after the labeling issue was identified, which appeared to be caused by a temporary breakdown in Atwater's labeling process. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported, according to the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check Atwater's cookie tins for undeclared tree nut allergens and avoid eating them if allergic to almonds, pecans, or walnuts.

content.text: Consumer support: 410-644-3435

title: Gregory's Foods Recalls White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Cookie Dough for Undeclared Peanuts
headline: Undeclared Peanuts Prompt Recall of Gregory's Foods White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Cookie Dough
description: Gregory's Foods recalled select Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough due to undeclared peanuts. No illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Gregory's Foods, Inc. of Eagan, MN, has recalled specific 2-pound 8.5-ounce packages of "Bag Full of Cookies" White Chocolate Macadamia Nut frozen cookie dough. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that some packages may contain Frozen Monster Cookie Dough, which includes undeclared peanuts.

This labeling error presents a risk for people with peanut allergies, as consuming the affected product could result in a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction. The recalled cookie dough was distributed to retail grocery stores and distribution centers in Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Nebraska.

The affected product is sold in a 2-pound 8.5-ounce plastic bag with a clear window and a "BEST BY 12/6/2026" date on the top front. Monster Cookie Dough, visible through the window, contains chocolate chips and colored candy-coated pieces, while the White Chocolate Macadamia Nut dough does not.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA recall announcement. The issue was limited to certain units produced on a single day, and corrective actions have been taken by the company.

Product NamePackage SizeBest By DateDistribution Area
Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough2 lb 8.5 oz12/6/2026Minnesota, Wisconsin, Nebraska

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Willy Pete's Chocolate Company LLC of Harwinton has recalled its Almond Despair chocolate bars because almonds were not declared on the ingredient label. The label incorrectly lists macadamia nuts instead of almonds, creating a risk for individuals with almond allergies. Ingestion could cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.

The affected Almond Despair chocolate bars are packaged in 2 oz purple wrappers with UPC code 0 987261 5. Distribution occurred in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and South Carolina.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA notice date. The recall details are provided by the FDA and Willy Pete's Chocolate Company LLC.

en.headline: Almond Despair Chocolate Bar Recalled by Willy Pete's Chocolates for Undeclared Almonds
en.description: Willy Pete's Chocolate Company LLC recalled Almond Despair chocolate bars due to undeclared almonds, posing a risk to those with almond allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Willy Pete's Chocolate Company LLC of Harwinton has recalled its Almond Despair chocolate bars because almonds were not declared on the ingredient label. The label incorrectly lists macadamia nuts instead of almonds, creating a risk for individuals with almond allergies. Ingestion could cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.

The affected Almond Despair chocolate bars are packaged in 2 oz purple wrappers with UPC code 0 987261 5. Distribution occurred in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and South Carolina.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA notice date. The recall details are provided by the FDA and Willy Pete's Chocolate Company LLC.

title: Virgo Rheumacare Capsules Recalled Nationwide for Elevated Lead Levels, FDA Notice 2025
headline: Handelnine Global Limited Recalls Virgo Rheumacare Capsules Due to High Lead Content
description: Handelnine Global Limited is recalling Virgo Rheumacare Capsules nationwide after FDA testing found elevated lead levels. Adverse health effects are possible.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA recall notice, Handelnine Global Limited, doing business as Navafresh, has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of Rheumacare Capsules by Virgo UAP Pharma Pvt. Ltd. (Virgo) due to elevated lead levels. FDA testing identified lead concentrations up to 11,100 ppm, which significantly exceed regulatory limits and may pose serious health risks.

The recalled Rheumacare Capsules are marketed for joint pain relief and are packaged in white plastic containers. Affected lot numbers are CAM040 and CALO79-N, distributed nationwide via the Navafresh website.

Exposure to high levels of lead can result in cognitive impairment, gastrointestinal symptoms, motor coordination issues, peripheral neuropathy, anemia, and reproductive health risks such as increased chance of miscarriage and reduced fertility. Acute lead poisoning may cause severe symptoms including seizures, coma, or death. The FDA notice states that adverse events have been reported in connection with this product.

ProductLot Number
Rheumacare CapsulesCAM040
Rheumacare CapsulesCALO79-N

Consumers who have experienced adverse effects should consult a healthcare professional. Adverse events can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using Virgo Rheumacare Capsules from the affected lots CAM040 and CALO79-N and follow the FDA recall instructions. Contact Handelnine Global Limited for further guidance as provided in the official recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Mr. Ankit Mota, Handelnine Global Limited | +1 442-223-0999 | legal@handelnine.com

title: Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro Frozen Raw Shrimp Recalled for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Direct Source Seafood LLC Recalls Frozen Raw Shrimp Due to Potential Cesium-137 Exposure
description: Direct Source Seafood LLC recalled Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro frozen raw shrimp due to possible exposure to low levels of cesium-137. No illnesses reported.
reason: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On December 19, 2025, Direct Source Seafood LLC announced a recall of approximately 83,800 bags of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. The affected products, sold under the Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro brands, may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions that could have exposed them to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137), according to the official FDA notice.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioisotope that can be present in the environment, particularly in areas with environmental contamination. Prolonged, repeated low-dose exposure to Cs-137 through contaminated food or water may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage in living cells. The recalled shrimp products were distributed in multiple states and sold between mid-2025 and 2027.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is investigating reports of Cs-137 contamination linked to frozen shrimp products and shipping containers manufactured in Indonesia by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods). According to the FDA, no contaminated products have been confirmed in the U.S. marketplace, and no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date.

RetailerBrandProduct SizeUPC CodeBest By DateStore LocationsDates of Sale

No illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with this recall as of the FDA notice date.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Market 32 or Waterfront Bistro frozen raw shrimp for the affected UPC codes and best by dates. If your product matches the recall details, do not consume it. Dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase.

title: Olympus Recalls Single-Use Ligating Device Due to PolyLoop Detachment Risk
headline: Olympus Single-Use Ligating Device Recall: PolyLoop May Fail to Detach
description: Olympus has issued a recall for its Single-Use Ligating Device after reports that the PolyLoop may not detach as intended, posing procedural risks. No deaths reported.
reason: PolyLoop device may fail to detach as expected and become anchored to patient anatomy

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA notice, Olympus Corporation has initiated a voluntary corrective action for its Single-Use Ligating Device ("Polyloop") due to a potential safety issue. The Polyloop is used with Olympus endoscopes to deliver a nylon loop snare for controlling bleeding after polypectomy of pedunculated polyps.

The recall follows reports that the device may fail to detach as expected, causing the loop to become anchored to patient anatomy. While no deaths have been reported, this issue can lead to complications such as bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, and may require emergency intervention or hospitalization.

Olympus notified customers in November 2025 and advised reviewing supplemental guidance and existing instructions for use. Customers are asked to acknowledge receipt of the notification through the Olympus recall portal. Adverse events or quality issues can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

Recall Details
Device: Single-Use Ligating Device ("Polyloop")
Issue: Device may fail to detach, anchoring loop to patient anatomy
Risks: Bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, need for emergency intervention
Notification Date: November 2025
Recall Number: 0476

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have received the Olympus Single-Use Ligating Device should check their product details at https://olympusamerica.com/recall to see if their device is affected by this recall.

content.text:
Consumer support: Olympus Technical Assistance Center | 1-800-848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com

title: Primavera Nueva Inc. Recalls Select No Brand 4-Count Tamales for Listeria Risk
headline: No Brand 4-Count Tamales Recalled by Primavera Nueva Inc. Due to Listeria monocytogenes Concern
description: Primavera Nueva Inc. is recalling select No Brand 4-count tamales due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primavera Nueva Inc., based in Sonoma, CA, has voluntarily recalled select 4-count tamale products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium may cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Pregnant women are also at risk, as infection can result in miscarriage or stillbirth.

The recall covers tamales produced between October 10, 2024, and October 10, 2025, distributed to retail stores in California and Nevada. No other Primavera Nueva products are affected. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after an FDA inspection identified gaps in verifying internal cooking temperatures required to control biological hazards, including Listeria monocytogenes.

Primavera Nueva Inc. has implemented enhanced cooking temperature monitoring and verification as of October 11, 2025. The affected tamales have date codes from 10/22/2024 through 10/22/2025.

Product Variety
Roasted Green Chile & Jack Cheese
Black Bean Bonanza & Jack Cheese
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles & Cheese
BBQ Chipotle Bean & White Cheddar
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa with Two Cheeses
Roasted Green Chile
Black Bean Bonanza
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the date codes on their No Brand 4-count tamales. If the products fall within the affected range, do not consume them and discard immediately. For more details, refer to the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Araceli Santoyo | 707-939-9350 | primaveranueva@aol.com

title: ReBoost and ClearLife 0.68 fl oz Nasal Sprays Recalled for Microbial Contamination
headline: MediNatura Recalls ReBoost and ClearLife Nasal Sprays Nationwide Due to Yeast, Mold, and Achromobacter Contamination
description: MediNatura New Mexico Inc. recalled all lots of ReBoost and ClearLife 0.68 fl oz nasal sprays after FDA found yeast, mold, and Achromobacter contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. has expanded a voluntary nationwide recall involving all lots of its ReBoost Nasal Spray and ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray. The recall is due to microbial contamination detected in both products, including yeast, mold, and the bacterium Achromobacter at levels above established specifications.

The ReBoost Nasal Spray is intended to temporarily relieve nasal congestion and related symptoms, while the ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray is used for minor allergy symptom relief. Both products are sold in 20 mL bottles packaged in distinct cartons and were distributed nationwide through retail outlets and online at medinatura.com.

There is a reasonable risk that using these contaminated products could result in serious infections, especially in immunocompromised individuals. According to the FDA recall notice, no injuries or adverse events have been reported to date.

ProductNDC NumberUPCExpiration DatesPackage Description
ReBoost Nasal Spray62795-4005-9787647 10186 312/2022 to 12/202520 mL bottle in white and yellow carton
ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray62795-4006-9787647 10188 712/2022 to 12/202520 mL bottle in white and green carton

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their ReBoost or ClearLife 0.68 fl oz nasal spray is included in the recall. If affected, stop using the product and contact MediNatura for return or replacement instructions as directed in the FDA recall notice.

en.headline: MediNatura Recalls ReBoost and ClearLife Nasal Sprays Nationwide Due to Yeast, Mold, and Achromobacter Contamination
en.description: MediNatura New Mexico Inc. recalled all lots of ReBoost and ClearLife 0.68 fl oz nasal sprays after FDA found yeast, mold, and Achromobacter contamination. No illnesses reported.
en.reason: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. has expanded a voluntary nationwide recall involving all lots of its ReBoost Nasal Spray and ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray. The recall is due to microbial contamination detected in both products, including yeast, mold, and the bacterium Achromobacter at levels above established specifications.

The ReBoost Nasal Spray is intended to temporarily relieve nasal congestion and related symptoms, while the ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray is used for minor allergy symptom relief. Both products are sold in 20 mL bottles packaged in distinct cartons and were distributed nationwide through retail outlets and online at medinatura.com.

There is a reasonable risk that using these contaminated products could result in serious infections, especially in immunocompromised individuals. According to the FDA recall notice, no injuries or adverse events have been reported to date.

ReBoost Nasal Spray62795-4005-9787647 10186 312/2022 to 12/202520 mL bottle in white and yellow carton
ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray62795-4006-9787647 10188 712/2022 to 12/202520 mL bottle in white and green carton

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their ReBoost or ClearLife 0.68 fl oz nasal spray is included in the recall. If affected, stop using the product and contact MediNatura for return or replacement instructions as directed in the FDA recall notice.

title: MR7 Super 700000 Dietary Supplement Recalled for Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: StuffbyNainax LLC Recalls MR7 Super 700000 Male Enhancement Supplement Due to Undeclared Drugs
description: StuffbyNainax LLC recalled all MR7 Super 700000 dietary supplement capsules after FDA found undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil. No lot numbers listed.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
StuffbyNainax LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of MR7 Super 700000 dietary supplement capsules. According to the official FDA notice, testing revealed the presence of undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, which are active pharmaceutical ingredients used in prescription medications for erectile dysfunction. The inclusion of these undeclared substances means the product is considered an unapproved new drug and is being recalled for safety reasons.

Use of products containing sildenafil or tadalafil without medical oversight can result in serious health risks, especially for individuals taking medications containing nitrates. Such interactions may cause a dangerous drop in blood pressure. The recall affects MR7 Super 700000 capsules sold online in the United States from August 2025 to November 2025. The product does not have lot numbers or expiration dates.

No reports of adverse events or illnesses related to this recall have been received as of the FDA announcement. This action is being taken in cooperation with the FDA to protect public health.

Product NameBrandFormLot NumberDistribution PeriodDistribution
MR7 SUPER 700000MR7CapsulesNot available / unlistedAugust 2025 – November 2025Sold online in the United States

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using MR7 Super 700000 dietary supplement and dispose of any remaining product, as directed by the FDA recall notice.

title: Ukrop's Recalls Decorated Pound Cakes for Undeclared Soy Allergen
headline: Ukrop's Decorated Pound Cakes Recalled Over Undeclared Soy Risk
description: Ukrop's Homestyle Foods is recalling decorated pound cakes due to undeclared soy, per FDA notice. No illnesses reported. Recall affects products with a 12/15/2025 best by date.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ukrop's Homestyle Foods, LLC, of Richmond, VA, has recalled four varieties of its Decorated Pound Cakes due to undeclared soy. The recall applies to products with a Best By Date of 12/15/2025. According to the FDA notice, a cake releasing agent containing soy was used in production but not listed on the label.

People with soy allergies may experience reactions ranging from mild symptoms to severe anaphylaxis. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA announcement. No other Ukrop's Homestyle Foods products are affected.

The recalled cakes were distributed to Kroger Mid-Atlantic stores in Virginia and West Virginia and Ukrop's Market Hall in Richmond, VA. Retailers have been notified and are removing affected products from shelves.

ProductBest By DateRetailers
Decorated Pound Cakes (4 varieties)12/15/2025Kroger Mid-Atlantic (28 locations in VA and WV), Ukrop's Market Hall (Richmond, VA)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with soy allergies should not eat the recalled decorated pound cakes. Check the best by date on your product. If affected, do not consume and return it to the place of purchase for a refund.

content.text: Consumer support: (804) 340-3050

title: Bengal King Jhal Chanachur Recalled for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: Recall: Bengal King Jhal Chanachur Food Treats May Contain Undeclared Peanuts
description: South Asian Food Inc. recalled Bengal King Jhal Chanachur due to undeclared peanuts. No illnesses reported. Check lot details if purchased.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
South Asian Food Inc., based in Maspeth, Queens, NY, has recalled its Bengal King Jhal Chanachur food treats because they may contain undeclared peanuts. This recall is important for individuals with peanut allergies, as consuming the product could result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected product is packaged in 12.34 oz (350 gm) clear plastic jars and was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail orders. The recall was initiated after routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets found peanuts in packages that did not declare them on the label.

According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses or allergic reactions related to this product have been reported to date.

ProductWeightLot NumberExpiration DateUPC Code
Bengal King Jhal Chanachur12.34 oz (350 gm)B No.HCNCU1205/24June-20268941153046243

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the packaging of Bengal King Jhal Chanachur for the specified lot number and UPC. If affected, do not consume the product due to the risk of undeclared peanuts.

title: Town Food Service Equipment Co. Recalls 1–3 Quart Aluminum Saucepans for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Recall of Town Food Service Equipment Co. Aluminum Saucepans Due to Lead Risk
description: Town Food Service Equipment Co. is recalling four sizes of aluminum saucepans (1–3 quarts) due to possible lead contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: 4 sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts
companyName: Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc.
reason: Aluminum saucepans recalled because they have the potential to be contaminated with lead (Pb) which may leach into food.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc. of Brooklyn, NY, is recalling certain aluminum saucepans due to potential lead (Pb) contamination, which may leach into food. Lead exposure poses serious health risks, particularly to children and fetuses, with no known safe exposure level. Even low levels can impair learning, IQ, and behavior, while higher levels may cause symptoms such as fatigue, headache, stomach pain, vomiting, or neurological changes.

The recall affects four sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts. These saucepans feature indented interior graduations and a plated steel handle riveted to the body. They were distributed between January 1, 2023, and November 30, 2025, through authorized food service equipment dealers including retailers across various states in the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Quebec City, Canada.

The recall stems from FDA testing that detected leachable lead in the finished products. Town Food Service Equipment Co. has halted production and distribution of the affected saucepans as the investigation continues. No illnesses have been reported to date, according to the FDA notice.
