title: Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk
headline: Bakkavor USA Recalls Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Bakkavor USA recalled select Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup sold at H-E-B stores in Texas due to undeclared milk. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Bakkavor USA of Charlotte, North Carolina, has recalled 16-ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup due to undeclared milk. The recall affects products with a BEST BY date of January 11, 2026. Individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities may be at risk of a serious allergic reaction if they consume this product.

The recalled soup was distributed between November 3, 2025, and December 8, 2025, exclusively at H-E-B retail stores in Texas. The product is packaged in clear plastic cups with the BEST BY date printed on the bottom. As of the recall notice date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

The recall was initiated after a consumer reported that the product labeled as Red Lentil Dal Soup contained poultry and rice soup instead. An internal review determined that the Turkey & Wild Rice Soup contains milk, while Red Lentil Dal Soup does not list milk as an ingredient. The labeling error is under investigation.

ProductSizeBest By DateRetailerDistribution Dates
Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup16 ounces1/11/2026H-E-B stores in Texas11/3/2025 – 12/8/2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with milk allergies or sensitivities should not eat the recalled Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup and should check the product's BEST BY date. If affected, avoid consumption and follow any additional guidance from the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (855)-432-4438

title: Nutrena Country Feeds Cracked Corn 50 lb. Bags Recalled for Elevated Aflatoxin Levels in Texas
headline: Cargill Recalls Nutrena Country Feeds Cracked Corn for Livestock Due to Excess Aflatoxin
description: Cargill Animal Nutrition & Health recalled a lot of Nutrena Country Feeds Cracked Corn 50 lb. bags in Texas after testing found aflatoxin above FDA limits. No injuries or illnesses reported.
productDescription: Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock, 50 lb. bag
reason: Product may contain aflatoxin levels that exceed the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cargill Animal Nutrition & Health has issued a voluntary recall of a single lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock due to aflatoxin levels exceeding the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals. According to the FDA notice, exposure to high aflatoxin levels can cause health issues in livestock, including weight loss, reduced feed intake, liver damage, immune suppression, and in severe cases, tumors. Dairy animals may pass aflatoxin into their milk.

The recalled product was distributed through retail stores in Texas. The recall was initiated after testing by the Office of the Texas State Chemist found an aflatoxin concentration of 35 parts per billion (ppb) in a sample. Cargill's retained sample from the same lot tested below 1 ppb. As of the recall notice, no reports of injury or illness related to this product have been received. Distribution of the affected lot has been halted while the investigation continues.

title: ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz Recalled for Microbial Contamination, No Injuries Reported
headline: MediNatura New Mexico Inc. Recalls ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz Due to Yeast, Mold, and Achromobacter Contamination
description: MediNatura New Mexico Inc. recalled one lot of ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz after FDA found yeast, mold, and Achromobacter contamination. No injuries reported.
reason: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On December 10, 2025, MediNatura New Mexico Inc. issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz. The recall was initiated after testing identified yeast, mold, and Achromobacter species at levels above established specifications. According to the FDA notice, there is a risk of adverse health consequences, especially for immunocompromised individuals, but no injuries or adverse events have been reported as of the recall date.

The affected product is distributed in a 20 mL bottle and was sold nationwide through retail stores and online. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product NameLot NumberExpiration DateNDC NumberUPCPackage Size
ReBoost Nasal Spray22426812/202762795-4005-9787647 10186 30.68 fl oz (20 mL)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers in possession of ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz, lot 224268, should stop using the product immediately and follow recall instructions from MediNatura New Mexico Inc. as provided in the official FDA notice.

content.text: Consumer support: (800) 621-7644 | recall@medinatura.com

title: Lunds & Byerlys Recalls Monster Cookies for Undeclared Peanut, Egg, and Soy Allergens
headline: Monster Cookies Recalled by Lunds & Byerlys Over Undeclared Allergens
description: Lunds & Byerlys has recalled Monster cookies sold in select stores due to undeclared peanut, egg, and soy allergens. No illnesses have been reported.
reason: May contain undeclared peanut, egg, and soy allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Lunds & Byerlys is voluntarily recalling its L&B Monster Cookies due to incorrect labeling that may result in undeclared peanut, egg, and soy allergens. These allergens can cause serious or life-threatening reactions in people with sensitivities.

The recalled cookies were sold between November 15, 2025, and December 9, 2025, at Lunds & Byerlys stores in the Twin Cities and St. Cloud areas. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice.

The labeling issue was identified by a store employee and reported to the company's Quality Assurance team.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased L&B Monster Cookies between November 15 and December 9, 2025, should return the product to any Lunds & Byerlys store for a full refund. Check packaging for recall details.

title: Grandma Belle's Tomato Basil Soup Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Lil' Turtles Recalls Grandma Belle's Tomato Basil Soup Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Lil' Turtles has recalled Grandma Belle's Tomato Basil Soup after a routine inspection found undeclared milk, posing a risk to those with milk allergies.
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Lil' Turtles has voluntarily recalled all lots of Grandma Belle's Tomato Basil Soup because milk was not declared on the product label. This poses a risk to people with milk allergies or sensitivities.

The affected soup is packaged in 17 oz glass jars and was distributed between September 23, 2022, and December 3, 2025, at various retail locations in several states. The undeclared allergen was identified during a routine inspection by the Ohio Department of Agriculture.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA's notice. Individuals with milk allergies could experience serious allergic reactions if they consume this product.

title: Lucky Foods Cinnamon Powder 40g Recalled for Elevated Lead Risk, Best Before 15.09.2027
headline: TBC Distribution Corporate Recalls Lucky Foods Cinnamon Powder 40g Due to Elevated Lead Levels
description: TBC Distribution Corporate has recalled Lucky Foods Cinnamon Powder 40g, best before 15.09.2027, due to possible elevated lead levels. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g, best before date 15.09.2027, packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label
reason: Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
TBC Distribution Corporate of Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. The affected product has a best before date of 15.09.2027 and is packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label.

Exposure to lead, even at low levels, can be harmful. Increased blood lead levels may be the only sign of exposure. Prolonged exposure, especially in children, may cause serious health issues including developmental defects and permanent damage to the central nervous system. Adults exposed chronically may experience kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects.

The recalled cinnamon powder was distributed to retail locations including grocery stores, convenience stores, supermarkets, delis, bakeries, and restaurants in multiple states between April 11, 2025, and September 1, 2025. The recall follows testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which detected elevated lead levels. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date, according to the FDA notice.

title: Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 34 oz Tubs and 11.5 oz Bags Recalled for Salmonella Risk
headline: Recall: Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 34 oz Tubs and 11.5 oz Bags Due to Salmonella Contamination
description: Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. recalled Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs, 11.5 oz bags) for possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags)
reason: Product may be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. has recalled Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted in 34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags due to potential Salmonella contamination. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The affected products were sold at Wegmans stores in Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. between November 3 and December 1, 2025. No illnesses related to these products have been reported as of the recall date.

The recall was initiated after routine supplier testing of raw pistachios used in the product detected Salmonella. The following products are included in the recall:

ProductPackagingUPCLot CodeBest By Date
Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 34 oz (964g)Plastic tub07789042131458041JUL 28, 2026
Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 11.5 oz (326g)Plastic bag07789042135258171AUG 10, 2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted in 34 oz tubs or 11.5 oz bags should not eat the product. Check the lot codes and best by dates, and follow the recall instructions from the FDA notice.

title: Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Recalled for Undeclared Peanut Allergen in Select States
headline: Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Recalled Due to Undeclared Peanut Allergen Risk
description: Mondelez Global LLC recalled select Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in eight states due to undeclared peanut allergen. No injuries or illnesses reported.
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Peanut

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA notice, Mondelēz Global LLC has issued a limited voluntary recall of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in select U.S. states due to a potential undeclared peanut allergen. The recall affects 70 cases of one specific SKU with certain "Best When Used By" dates and a plant code, distributed in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, and Alabama.

Individually wrapped packs inside the cartons may be mislabeled as the Cheese variety, though the product inside is the Peanut Butter variety. This labeling issue could pose a serious or life-threatening risk for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to peanuts. The outer cartons are correctly labeled and include an allergen advisory stating "contains peanuts."

This recall is not related to a previous recall from July and is being conducted voluntarily. No other Ritz or Mondelēz Global LLC products are affected. As of the FDA notice, there have been no reports of injury or illness associated with this product.

Product DescriptionRetail UPCBest When Used By DatesPlant Code
27.6 oz. RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches - 20 Count (20 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton)44000 07584 28 JAN 26, 15 JAN 26"AE" (located on top of package)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches for the affected UPC, dates, and plant code. Do not consume the product if it matches the recall details due to the risk of undeclared peanut allergen.

title: Vetoquinol USA Recalls Six Lots of FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle Due to Particulate Matter
headline: FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle Recalled by Vetoquinol USA Over Particulate Matter Risk
description: Vetoquinol USA is recalling six lots of FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle due to particulate matter found in the sterile diluent vial. No adverse events reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Vetoquinol USA, Inc. has recalled six lots of its FOLLTROPIN® Injectable Kits for Cattle after particulate matter was identified in the sterile diluent vial during routine sample examination. The product is used to induce superovulation in beef and dairy heifers and cows and is available by prescription in the U.S.

The presence of particulate matter in injectable products may cause injection site reactions or hypersensitivity responses in animals, such as pain, swelling, wounds, decreased activity, fever, or labored breathing. As of the recall announcement, no injuries or adverse events have been reported in connection with these lots.

This recall affects only the sterile diluent vial included in the FOLLTROPIN Kit. The FOLLTROPIN powder vials and the Dual Pack format (which does not contain diluent vials) are not impacted. Distribution occurred nationwide through veterinary distributors.

Recall Information
Product: FOLLTROPIN® Injectable Kits (porcine pituitary-derived FSH for injection)
Reason for Recall: Presence of particulate matter in sterile diluent vial
Impacted Product Format: FOLLTROPIN Kit (one vial of FSH powder + one vial of sterile diluent)
Unaffected Product Format: FOLLTROPIN Dual Pack (two vials of FSH powder, no diluent)
Distribution: Veterinary distributors nationwide, United States
Impacted Lots
Six lots of FOLLTROPIN sterile diluent vials (specific lot numbers referenced in the FDA notice)

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers in possession of recalled FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle should stop using the product and follow instructions from Vetoquinol USA regarding return or disposal.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: (800) 267-5707 | customerserviceusa@vetoquinol.com
Media: usa_news@vetoquinol.com

title: Wegmans Recalls Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese for Listeria Risk
headline: Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese Recalled by Wegmans Over Listeria Concern
description: Wegmans Food Markets, Inc. recalled Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Wegmans Food Markets, Inc. has recalled Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese sold with Wegmans' scale labels because of the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The affected cheese was sold at Wegmans stores in Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. between November 14 and November 24, 2025. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA announcement.

Symptoms of Listeria infection may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage or stillbirth if infected.

ProductDescriptionUPCSale Dates
Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano CheesePackaged in plastic tub with Wegmans scale label2-77580-XXXXX-7 (XXXXX varies by weight)November 14 – November 24, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they purchased Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese with Wegmans scale labels between November 14 and November 24, 2025, and avoid consuming any affected product.

title: Prairie Farms Recalls Gallon Fat Free Milk for Possible Cleaning Agent Contamination
headline: Prairie Farms Fat Free Milk Recalled Due to Possible Food-Grade Cleaning Agent Contamination
description: Prairie Farms is recalling select Gallon Fat Free Milk distributed to Woodman's stores in Illinois and Wisconsin due to possible food-grade cleaning agent contamination. FDA notice published November 26, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Prairie Farms has recalled select Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk due to potential contamination with food-grade cleaning agents. The affected milk was produced at the Dubuque, Iowa facility and distributed exclusively to Woodman's stores in Illinois and Wisconsin. Consumption of this product may cause illness.

The recall applies to Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk with a code date of DEC08 and plant code PLT19-145, produced during the timeframe of 17:51 to 21:23 on that date. Approximately 320 gallons of the affected milk were sold before the issue was identified on November 24, 2025. Prairie Farms removed all remaining impacted product from store shelves after discovery.

Other Prairie Farms products are not affected by this recall. The company is working with regulatory authorities and Woodman's Markets to investigate the incident and manage the recall process.

ProductCode DatePlant CodeProduction TimeframeUPCFacility
Prairie Farms Gallon FAT FREE MilkDEC08PLT19-14517:51–21:237273023117Dubuque, Iowa

Affected Woodman's store locations include:

  • Illinois: Bloomingdale, Buffalo Grove, Carpentersville, Lakemoor, North Aurora, Rockford
  • Wisconsin: Appleton, Beloit, Green Bay, Janesville, Kenosha, Madison, Menomonee Falls, Oak Creek, Onalaska, Racine, Sun Prairie, Waukesha

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk matches the code date DEC08 and plant code PLT19-145. Do not consume affected milk; dispose of it or return it to the store as directed in the official recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:

Media: Prairie Farm's Media Relations | | media@prairiefarms.com

title: Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby Recalled for Botulism Risk
headline: Mamtakim, Inc. Recalls Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby Due to Clostridium Botulinum Hazard
description: Mamtakim, Inc. is recalling Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, and Goby due to potential Clostridium botulinum contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mamtakim, Inc. has announced a recall of Belevini brand Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, and Goby due to the potential presence of Clostridium botulinum, as stated in the official FDA notice. Clostridium botulinum can cause botulism, a rare but serious illness that may result in life-threatening symptoms. The affected products are dried, uneviscerated fish, which can increase the risk of bacterial growth if not properly processed.

The recalled items were sold in transparent vacuum-sealed plastic pouches, net weight 5.29 oz (150 g), and stored refrigerated between 18°F and 68°F. Distribution was limited to walk-in customers at the Cherry Hill Emmons retail store in Brooklyn, NY. According to the FDA, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

Symptoms of botulism may include weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, trouble breathing, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. The recall was initiated following an FDA inspection that found the fish had not been properly eviscerated, increasing the risk of contamination.

Product NamePackageNet WeightBrandBarcode (UPC)StorageExpiration DateItem Number
DRIED WHOLE SMELTS SALTED (KORUSHKA)Transparent vacuum-sealed plastic pouch5.29 oz (150 g)BELEVINI0754684898995Refrigerated at 18°F–68°F04/08/2026LL0320

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No injuries or illnesses have been reported as of the recall date.

content.text: Consumers should check for Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, and Goby and avoid consuming these products if purchased. For questions, contact Mamtakim, Inc. as directed in the official recall notice.

title: Boar's Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap Recalled for Listeria Risk in 2025
headline: Supreme Service Solutions Recalls Boar's Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap Due to Listeria
description: Supreme Service Solutions, LLC. has recalled Boar's Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supreme Service Solutions, LLC. (doing business as Supreme Deli) is recalling certain Boar's Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap products due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes, as stated in the official FDA notice. The recall is linked to pecorino romano cheese supplied by Ambriola Company, which may be contaminated. Ambriola Company initiated a Class I recall after notification from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regarding possible contamination. As a precaution, Boar's Head has voluntarily withdrawn all products produced by Ambriola Company for Boar's Head, including items not directly affected by the contamination.

The recalled products were distributed in Kroger retail stores in Kentucky and Indiana, packaged in clear plastic grab-and-go containers. According to the FDA notice, no illnesses or consumer complaints related to this recall have been reported by Supreme Service Solutions.

Product NameBarcode UPCBest By Date
EverRoast Chicken Caesar Salad85004224414211/9/2025 - 11/22/2025
EverRoast Chicken Caesar Wrap8500422445511/9/2025 - 11/22/2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have the recalled Boar's Head Chicken Caesar Salad or Wrap and avoid consuming them. For more information or if you have the affected product, contact the company at info@ambriola.com as advised by the FDA notice.

content.text: Consumer support: info@ambriola.com

title: Prime Food Recalls Lava Buns for Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk
headline: Prime Food Processing LLC Recalls Lava Buns Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Prime Food Processing LLC recalled Lava Buns after discovering undeclared milk allergen. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA notice in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prime Food Processing LLC of Brooklyn, New York, has announced a voluntary recall of 2,243 cases of two varieties of dessert buns under the Prime Food brand due to undeclared milk allergens. The affected products contain milk derived from unsalted butter, which is not properly listed in the "Contains" allergen statement on the packaging as required by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

This recall poses a significant risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe milk sensitivities, as consuming these products could trigger a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction. The affected lava buns were distributed to Asian grocery stores in 38 states between April 2, 2025, and November 14, 2025.

No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after an internal product review identified the labeling issue, attributed to a temporary failure in the label review process at Prime Food Processing LLC.

Product NameUPCSizeItem NumberPackaging DescriptionLot Code RangeExpiration Date Range
Prime Food Lava Bun with Salted Egg Yolk9790370587324 ozPD4188Orange pouch approx. 10.25"W × 11.25"H25092 - 2531807/26 - 03/27
Prime Food Lava Bun with Green Tea Flavor9780370588324 ozPD4198Bright lime green pouch approx. 10.25"W × 11.25"H25092 - 2531807/26 - 03/27

The recalled products were sold in the following states: Alabama, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, Michigan, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, Nebraska, New Jersey, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington, and Wisconsin.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Prime Food Lava Buns should check the packaging for affected lot codes and avoid consuming the product if they have a milk allergy or sensitivity.

content.text: Consumer support: Prime Food Processing LLC | 718-963-2323

title: Majestic Chef Pan Milk Pan 24cm Recalled for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: SHATA TRADERS INC Recalls Majestic Chef Pan Milk Pan 24cm Due to Lead Risk
description: SHATA TRADERS INC is recalling Majestic Chef Pan Milk Pan 24cm after FDA testing found potential lead contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
reason: Product has the potential to be contaminated with significant levels of lead (Pb) which may leach into food

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
SHATA TRADERS INC, based in Brooklyn, NY, is recalling the Majestic Chef Pan Milk Pan 24cm due to the potential for significant levels of lead (Pb) to leach into food during use. Lead exposure is hazardous, especially for children and pregnant individuals, and can lead to serious health effects such as developmental delays and neurological changes.

The recall applies to Milk Pan 24cm distributed in retail stores across New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Maryland, Virginia, and Massachusetts. The product is sold individually and as part of a 6-piece box set. The FDA identified the issue after testing samples collected at a retail location. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date, according to the official FDA notice.

ProductSizeDistribution AreasPackaging
Majestic Chef Pan Milk Pan24 cmNY, NJ, CT, MD, VA, MASold individually, 6 pieces per box

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the Majestic Chef Pan Milk Pan 24cm and check if their product matches the recall description. For questions or further assistance, contact SHATA TRADERS INC at the number provided in the official FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Mahboob Rahman | (718) 369-7000

title: Jeni's Recalls Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars for Undeclared Wheat and Soy
headline: Jeni's Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Allergens
description: Jeni's Splendid Ice Cream is recalling Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens. No illnesses have been reported.
keywords: Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars
productDescription: Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jeni's Splendid Ice Creams has announced a voluntary recall of one batch of its Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars due to the presence of undeclared wheat and soy allergens. According to the FDA recall notice, a crunch topping containing wheat and soy was inadvertently mixed into the affected ice cream bars during production.

The recalled product may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in people with wheat or soy allergies. No illnesses or injuries related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. The affected product was distributed nationwide through grocery stores.

ProductBatch Code
Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars25-210

title: Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam Recalled for Undeclared Carmoisine
headline: Mangalm LLC Recalls Tops Jams Due to Undeclared Carmoisine Color Additive
description: Mangalm LLC recalled Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam after FDA found undeclared carmoisine. No illnesses reported. Recall published November 2025.
productDescription: Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Mangalm LLC of Newark, California, has recalled all batches of Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Tops Strawberry Jam due to the presence of carmoisine, an unapproved color additive. The affected products are 475g jars with batch number 4F1101 and a best before date of October 6, 2026.

The products were distributed in Utah, Portland (Oregon), Washington, and California. As of the recall announcement, no illnesses related to these products have been reported. The recall was initiated after an FDA inspection identified undeclared carmoisine in the jams.

title: Africa Imports Recalls Organic Moringa Leaf Powder for Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Africa Imports Recalls Organic Moringa Leaf Powder Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Africa Imports is recalling Organic Moringa Leaf Powder (1 kg box) sold after June 5, 2025, due to possible Salmonella contamination, per FDA notice.
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA notice, Africa Imports has voluntarily recalled its Organic Moringa Leaf Powder (1 kilogram box) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Severe cases can result in more serious health complications.

The recall applies to all Organic Moringa Leaf Powder purchased after June 5, 2025, through Africa Imports' website, africaimports.com. The product is packaged in 1-kilogram boxes labeled "Organic Moringa Leaf Powder." The recall was initiated after Salmonella was detected in samples from this batch.

No illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall, as stated in the FDA announcement. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

ProductPackagingRecall Details
Organic Moringa Leaf Powder1 kilogram boxAll product purchased after June 5, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Organic Moringa Leaf Powder from Africa Imports after June 5, 2025, should stop using the product and dispose of it. For questions, contact Africa Imports as directed in the official recall notice.

title: ByHeart Recalls All Batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Due to Potential Clostridium Botulinum Risk
headline: ByHeart Issues Nationwide Recall of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula 24 oz Cans and Packets for Botulism Risk
description: ByHeart is recalling all batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula 24 oz cans and 0.6 oz packets due to potential Clostridium botulinum contamination, per FDA notice.
productDescription: Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ByHeart Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of all batches of ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula in 24 oz cans and 0.6 oz Anywhere Pack™ packets due to the potential presence of Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause serious and potentially life-threatening illness. This action follows an investigation into a multistate outbreak of infant botulism potentially associated with this formula, as reported in the official FDA notice.

Infant botulism is a rare but serious condition caused by ingestion of Clostridium botulinum spores, which can produce neurotoxins in infants. Symptoms may include constipation, poor feeding, drooping eyelids, sluggish pupils, low muscle tone, difficulty sucking and swallowing, weak cry, generalized weakness, and respiratory problems. Immediate medical attention is advised if these symptoms appear.

The recall covers all batch codes and expiration dates of the affected ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula products, distributed nationwide through retail and online channels. Testing is ongoing in collaboration with the FDA and public health authorities.

ProductSizeUPCAdditional Info
ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula24 oz can5004496800All batch codes and expiration dates included in recall
ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Anywhere Pack™0.60 oz packets (14 packets per package)5004496802All batch codes and expiration dates included in recall

According to the FDA, as of November 19, 2025, 31 infants from 15 states with suspected or confirmed infant botulism and exposure to ByHeart formula have been reported. All were hospitalized; no deaths have been reported to date.

This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the California Department of Public Health (CDPH), and other partners. For more details, visit the ByHeart website or the FDA website.

facts.product: Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using all ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula 24 oz cans and 0.6 oz packets immediately and review the FDA recall notice for affected products. For further assistance or replacement options, contact ByHeart customer support or visit the FDA recall page.

title: Moonlight/Kroger Yellow and White Peaches Recalled for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Moonlight Companies Recalls Yellow and White Peaches Sold Nationwide Due to Listeria Risk
description: Moonlight Companies has recalled Moonlight/Kroger yellow and white peaches sold nationwide due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Moonlight Companies has voluntarily recalled California-grown conventional yellow and white peaches distributed under the Moonlight and Kroger brands due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women may face miscarriage or stillbirth risks.

The recalled peaches were sold at retail stores nationwide from September 16, 2025, through October 29, 2025, as individual fruit with PLU stickers or in multi-packs. Packages or PLU stickers labeled "Washington" or "Organic" are not included in this recall. The recall was initiated after Listeria monocytogenes was detected in the packing facility environment. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

Product DescriptionPLU CodesDates SoldNotes
Conventional Yellow Peaches3426September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025Individual fruit with PLU sticker, not "Washington" or "Organic"
Conventional White Peaches3427September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025Individual fruit with PLU sticker, not "Washington" or "Organic"
Conventional Yellow and White PeachesVarious (multi-packs)September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025Multi-pack, excluding "Washington" and "Organic" packages

Consumers and healthcare professionals can report adverse reactions or illnesses related to this product to the FDA's MedWatch system online, by phone, or by mail as listed in the official notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Moonlight/Kroger yellow or white peaches sold between September 16 and October 29, 2025, and avoid consuming recalled products. Follow FDA guidance for safe disposal or return if directed.

content.text: Consumer support: (855) 215-5017

title: ByHeart Recalls Two Batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula for Potential Clostridium botulinum Risk
headline: ByHeart Voluntarily Recalls Select Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Batches Amid FDA Investigation
description: ByHeart is recalling two batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula due to potential risk of Clostridium botulinum, as part of an FDA investigation. No contamination found in tested products.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ByHeart Inc. has initiated a voluntary recall of two batches of its Whole Nutrition Infant Formula following a broader investigation by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) into an outbreak of infant botulism. Infant botulism is a rare but serious illness caused by ingestion and colonization of Clostridium botulinum spores in an infant's intestinal tract, leading to potentially severe symptoms including constipation, poor feeding, muscle weakness, and respiratory difficulties.

The FDA reported approximately 83 cases of infant botulism nationwide since August 2025, with 13 of the affected infants having received ByHeart formula at some point. However, the FDA has not found a direct link between any infant formula and these cases, and there is no historical precedent for infant formula causing infant botulism. Botulism is typically associated with environmental sources such as soil and dust rather than dairy products or infant formulas.

ByHeart states it has found no contamination in its products and adheres to strict safety standards. The recall is a precautionary measure to eliminate any potential risk and maintain infant safety.

Batch CodeUse By DateUPC
251261P201 Dec 20265004496800
251131P201 Dec 2026

To report an illness or adverse event related to this recall, consumers can:

  • Contact an FDA Consumer Complaint Coordinator to speak directly with a representative.
  • Complete an electronic Voluntary MedWatch form online.
  • Complete a paper Voluntary MedWatch form and mail it to the FDA.

For further questions or support, parents and caregivers can contact ByHeart experts via email at hello@byheart.com or call 1 (833) 429-4327. Additional information is available on the company website at byheart.com.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula matches the recalled batch codes and, if so, follow the FDA recall instructions. For verification and further steps, visit the FDA recall page or contact ByHeart directly.

content.text:

Media: press@byheart.com

title: Fresenius Kabi Recalls Three Lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL for Endotoxin Concerns
headline: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL Recalled by Fresenius Kabi Due to Endotoxin Results
description: Fresenius Kabi USA, LLC is recalling three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL after out-of-specification endotoxin results were found. No injuries reported.
productDescription: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Fresenius Kabi USA, LLC has voluntarily recalled three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL (10 mg per mL), 2 mL fill in a 2 mL vial. The recall is due to out-of-specification endotoxin results identified in certain reserve samples from one lot, with two additional lots included as a precaution. The affected products were distributed in the United States.

No injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. Distributors and customers have been notified, and the company is coordinating the return of the recalled products.

Lot NumberExpiration DateFirst Ship DateLast Ship Date

en.title: Fresenius Kabi Recalls Three Lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL for Endotoxin Concerns
en.headline: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL Recalled by Fresenius Kabi Due to Endotoxin Results
en.description: Fresenius Kabi USA, LLC is recalling three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL after out-of-specification endotoxin results were found. No injuries reported.
en.productDescription: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Fresenius Kabi USA, LLC has voluntarily recalled three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL (10 mg per mL), 2 mL fill in a 2 mL vial. The recall is due to out-of-specification endotoxin results identified in certain reserve samples from one lot, with two additional lots included as a precaution. The affected products were distributed in the United States.

No injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. Distributors and customers have been notified, and the company is coordinating the return of the recalled products.

title: African Food on Wheels Inc. Recalls Oven Dried Fish Due to Potential Clostridium botulinum Contamination
headline: Recall of Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) by African Food on Wheels Inc. for Botulism Risk
description: African Food on Wheels Inc. is recalling Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) sold in Bronx, NY, due to possible Clostridium botulinum contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
African Food on Wheels Inc., based in Bronx, New York, has announced a recall of its Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) due to a potential health risk. The recalled product consists of dried, un-eviscerated fish greater than 5 inches in length, which may be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal illness.

Consumers are advised not to consume the product, even if it appears or smells normal. Botulism symptoms include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms should seek immediate medical care.

The affected products were sold only to walk-in customers at African Food on Wheels Inc., located at 1376 Randall Avenue, Bronx, NY 10474. The products are packaged in corrugated brown boxes. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

The recall followed an FDA inspection that found the fish had not been properly eviscerated. The information is based on the official FDA notice published November 6, 2025.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased African Food on Wheels Inc. Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) should check their product for potential contamination. If you have this product, contact African Food on Wheels Inc. for assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (646) 632-2911 | africanfoodonwheelsinc@gmail.com

title: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. Recalls English Muffins for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: English Muffins Recalled by Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. recalled English muffins sold in Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana due to undeclared milk. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of its English muffins because the product contains undeclared milk. The recall applies to English muffins packaged in 13.04 oz sealed plastic bags, distributed and sold fresh at select retail locations in Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana. This labeling issue may pose a risk to people with milk allergies.

The affected products were produced from April 24, 2025, through October 24, 2025. As of the recall notice, no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this product.

title: SiluetaYa Tejocote Root Dietary Supplement Recalled for Yellow Oleander Contamination
headline: SiluetaYa Recalls Tejocote Root Dietary Supplement Due to Toxic Yellow Oleander
description: SiluetaYa is recalling its Tejocote Root dietary supplement after FDA found yellow oleander, a toxic plant. No illnesses have been reported to date.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, SiluetaYa is recalling its Tejocote Root dietary supplement after FDA analysis identified the presence of Thevetia peruviana, also known as yellow oleander. Yellow oleander contains cardiac glycosides, which are highly toxic and can cause serious neurological, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular health effects in humans and animals.

Potential symptoms of yellow oleander poisoning include nausea, vomiting, dizziness, diarrhea, abdominal pain, and changes in heart rhythm. The recalled product was distributed online in 8-ounce containers. As of the FDA's announcement, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with this product.

For additional details, refer to the official FDA recall notice.

ProductContainer SizeDistribution
Tejocote Root dietary supplement (SiluetaYa brand)8-ounce containerOnline distribution

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not use the SiluetaYa Tejocote Root dietary supplement due to the presence of yellow oleander. Avoid consuming the product and contact SiluetaYa for further information or support as directed in the official recall notice.

title: Haagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars Recalled for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Recall: Haagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars May Contain Undeclared Wheat
description: Dreyer's Grand Ice Cream, Inc. recalled select Haagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars due to undeclared wheat. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On November 3, 2025, Dreyer's Grand Ice Cream, Inc. voluntarily recalled a specific batch of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars because they may contain undeclared wheat. This poses a risk for people with wheat allergies or sensitivities, as consuming the product could cause a serious allergic reaction.

The recall affects the 6-count package of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars with batch code LLA519501 and a Best By date of January 31, 2027. These products were distributed to Kroger and Giant Eagle stores in select states.

No illnesses or injuries related to this recall have been reported, according to the official FDA notice.

title: First & Last Tomato Sauces Recalled for Potential Botulism Risk
headline: First & Last Marinara, Puttanesca, and Meat Pasta Sauces Recalled Over Botulism Hazard
description: First and Last Bakery LLC recalled select tomato sauces due to potential Clostridium botulinum risk from unapproved manufacturing. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce
reason: Potential for Clostridium botulinum hazard as the product is manufactured without an approved schedule

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
First and Last Bakery LLC, based in Hartford, CT, has recalled certain First & Last brand tomato sauce products after the Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection identified that the products were manufactured without an approved scheduled process. This may allow the growth of Clostridium botulinum, which can produce a potentially fatal toxin.

The recall includes Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce, distributed in Connecticut and Massachusetts, mainly through Big Y stores and possibly other retailers. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported as of the FDA notice. Sales and production of these sauces have been suspended while regulatory compliance is addressed.

Clostridium botulinum can cause botulism, a serious illness with symptoms such as weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, and muscle paralysis. Immediate medical attention is advised if symptoms occur after consuming these products.

  • Distribution dates: September 22, 2025 to October 28, 2025

title: Olympus Recalls Bronchofiberscopes and Bronchovideoscopes Due to Endobronchial Combustion Risk
headline: Olympus Recalls Bronchoscopes After Reports of Endobronchial Combustion and Injuries
description: Olympus is recalling certain bronchofiberscopes and bronchovideoscopes after reports of endobronchial combustion, including serious injuries and one fatality.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has issued a voluntary global labeling update for its bronchofiberscopes and bronchovideoscopes used with laser therapy, argon plasma coagulation, or high-frequency cauterization equipment. This update is intended to clarify instructions for safe use during therapeutic procedures in the tracheobronchial tree.

The action follows a previous corrective measure in 2023 after incidents of endobronchial combustion involving Olympus bronchoscopes, including one fatality. Since that time, Olympus has identified the need for additional instructions to reduce combustion risk. Four additional serious injury incidents have been reported globally since 2023, including one in the United States.

Safety recommendations include maintaining more than 4 centimeters separation between the endoscope and endotracheal tube, keeping oxygen concentration below 40%, limiting energy output to less than 40 watts, avoiding prolonged energy application in one spot, and using suction to remove smoke. Endobronchial combustion can result in severe internal burns, additional medical interventions, extended hospitalization, or death. Device damage may also cause injury or require surgical removal of fragments.

Users are advised to review the updated instructions provided in the September 2025 customer letter. Adverse events can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

  • Olympus Technical Assistance Center: 1-800-848-9024, Option 1
  • Email: complaints@olympus.com
  • FDA MedWatch reporting: https://www.fda.gov/safety/medwatch-fda-safety-information-and-adverse-event-reporting-program/reporting-serious-problems-fda

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have an Olympus bronchofiberscope or bronchovideoscope, review the updated labeling and check if your device is affected. Contact Olympus for support or more information as directed in the official recall notice.

content.text: Consumer support: (800) 848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com

title: ICU Medical Recalls 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection Due to Mislabeled Overwrap
headline: ICU Medical Recalls 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection for Overwrap Labeling Error
description: Otsuka ICU Medical LLC recalled one lot of 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection after the overwrap was mislabeled as 10 mEq, posing overdose risk. No injuries reported.
reason: Potential for Potassium chloride overdose: 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection is Mislabeled As 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Otsuka ICU Medical LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of a specific lot of their 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection due to a labeling error. The overwrap of the recalled lot incorrectly identifies the product as 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection, which could lead to a potassium overdose if the mislabeled dosage is used. The correct dosage of 20 mEq is printed on the product bag label but is obscured by the incorrect overwrap.

Potassium chloride overdose may cause serious health risks including hyperkalemia, which can result in neuromuscular dysfunction, cardiac dysrhythmias, or cardiac arrest. Certain patient populations such as premature infants, those with kidney issues, or patients on potassium-sparing medications are at higher risk for severe complications. To date, no adverse events related to this issue have been reported, according to the FDA notice.

The affected product lot, manufactured on April 15, 2025, was distributed in the United States between May 23, 2025, and August 26, 2025.

Product DescriptionLot NumberExpiration DateNDC
Potassium Chloride Injection 20 mEq (overwrap labeled as 10 mEq)103061309-30-20260990-7077-14 (Product), 0990-7074-26 (Overwrap)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the label on their Potassium Chloride Injection products for the affected lot and concentration. If the mislabeled product is found, follow the recall instructions from Otsuka ICU Medical LLC or contact the company at (844) 654-7780 for guidance.

title: Monarch Premium Kratom Powder Recalled for Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Recall: Monarch Premium Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant Kratom Powder
description: Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG is recalling select Monarch Premium Kratom powders due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant Kratom powder
companyName: Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 31, 2025, Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG of Boise, Idaho, issued a recall for Monarch Premium Kratom powders, including Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant varieties. The recall was initiated after the Florida Department of Agriculture & Consumer Services detected Salmonella in a retail sample. Salmonella can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection may include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe if it spreads to the bloodstream.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide via retail stores and mail order from April 2023 through September 2023, except in Alabama, Arkansas, Indiana, Rhode Island, and Wisconsin. According to the FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

title: Hoque Dry Ghoinnya Fish Recalled for Uneviscerated Product Risk, FDA Alert 2025
headline: Hoque Dry Ghoinnya Fish Recalled Nationwide for Botulism Risk Due to Uneviscerated Fish
description: New Hoque & Sons Inc. recalled Hoque Dry Ghoinnya Fish after FDA found the product was uneviscerated, posing a botulism risk. No illnesses reported.
reason: Product was found to be uneviscerated. Uneviscerated fish have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice published October 29, 2025, New Hoque & Sons Inc. of Maspeth, NY, has recalled its "Dry Ghoinnya Fish" because the product was found to be uneviscerated. The recall applies to packages distributed nationwide through retail stores. Uneviscerated fish may contain Clostridium botulinum spores in the viscera, which have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.

The recalled product is packaged in clear plastic bags weighing 10 to 12 pounds, with an expiration date of 5/19/25 stamped on the bottom. The UPC code is 908172635412. The recall was initiated after routine sampling and testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets identified improper evisceration before processing.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning can include dizziness, blurred or double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal bloating, and constipation. The FDA reports that, as of the recall date, no illnesses have been associated with this product.

ProductPackage WeightExpiration DateUPC Code
Dry Ghoinnya Fish (uneviscerated)10-12 pounds5/19/25908172635412

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Hoque Dry Ghoinnya Fish should not consume the product. Check for the UPC and expiration date, and follow the company's instructions or contact New Hoque & Sons Inc. for guidance.

content.text: Consumer support: (718) 391-0992

title: Teasdale Foods Recalls Martin's and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits for Undeclared Milk
headline: Undeclared Milk Prompts Recall of Martin's and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits by Teasdale Foods
description: Teasdale Foods, Inc. recalls select Martin's and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits due to undeclared milk. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Teasdale Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of certain Taco Dinner Kits due to the potential presence of undeclared milk. According to the FDA notice, this recall is important for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming the affected products could cause serious allergic reactions.

The recall covers specific lots of Martin's and Giant Crunchy Taco Dinner Kits and Casa Mamita Soft Taco Dinner Kits. The issue was identified after consumer complaints revealed that cocoa mix packets containing milk were mistakenly included instead of taco seasoning packets, due to a third-party supplier error. As a result, milk is present in products without being listed on the label.

The affected products were distributed through Giant, Martin's, and Aldi stores in several states, including Alabama, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, New York, Ohio, and Pennsylvania. The FDA reports that no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date in connection with these products.

en.headline: Undeclared Milk Prompts Recall of Martin's and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits by Teasdale Foods
en.description: Teasdale Foods, Inc. recalls select Martin's and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits due to undeclared milk. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Teasdale Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of certain Taco Dinner Kits due to the potential presence of undeclared milk. According to the FDA notice, this recall is important for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming the affected products could cause serious allergic reactions.

The recall covers specific lots of Martin's and Giant Crunchy Taco Dinner Kits and Casa Mamita Soft Taco Dinner Kits. The issue was identified after consumer complaints revealed that cocoa mix packets containing milk were mistakenly included instead of taco seasoning packets, due to a third-party supplier error. As a result, milk is present in products without being listed on the label.

The affected products were distributed through Giant, Martin's, and Aldi stores in several states, including Alabama, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, New York, Ohio, and Pennsylvania. The FDA reports that no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date in connection with these products.

title: Hampton Farms Mixed Nuts Recalled for Undeclared Hazelnut Allergen in 2025
headline: Hampton Farms Recalls Mixed Nuts Due to Undeclared Hazelnut Allergen Risk
description: Hampton Farms recalled select Mixed Nuts 8 oz packages after discovering undeclared hazelnut allergen. No illnesses reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Hampton Farms has recalled its Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted 8 oz and Mixed Nuts Roasted and Salted 8 oz packages due to an undeclared hazelnut allergen. Hazelnuts are listed in the ingredient statement but were omitted from the allergen warning label, creating a risk for those with hazelnut allergies. Consumption may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.

The labeling issue was identified on September 15, 2025, during a routine paperwork review. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported. The recalled products were distributed between September 2 and September 9, 2025, and sold in Stop&Shop stores in Massachusetts, Connecticut, New Jersey, New York, and Rhode Island.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Pacific Recalls Italian Parsley Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Pacific International Marketing Issues Recall of Italian Parsley for Salmonella Risk
description: Pacific International Marketing recalled Italian Parsley distributed in eight states due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Pacific International Marketing has recalled 474 cases of bulk Italian Parsley due to potential Salmonella contamination. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Severe cases can lead to complications such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recalled Italian Parsley was distributed to wholesalers in Arizona, California, Florida, Minnesota, Michigan, Georgia, Ohio, and Nevada between September 22 and September 25, 2025. The product was sold in bulk cases of 30 or 60 bunches with a twist tie, and in 24 count bunches in bags. The shelf life is 18 days from harvest, with an expiration date of October 10, 2025.

The recall was initiated after a sample collected on October 6, 2025, tested positive for Salmonella. As of the FDA announcement, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported. Pacific International Marketing is cooperating with the FDA and conducting an internal review.

content.text: If you purchased Pacific brand Italian Parsley, check the lot and expiration details. If your product matches the recall, do not consume it. Contact Pacific International Marketing at 831-759-8301 or customerservice@pim4u.com for further instructions.

title: Dwater Recalls Purity Products Dietary Supplement for Bladder Support Due to E. coli Contamination Risk
headline: Recall: Purity Products Dietary Supplement for Bladder Support Over Potential E. coli Contamination
description: Dwater has recalled Purity Products Dietary Supplement for bladder support due to possible contamination with Escherichia coli O7:K1 and 1303. No injuries reported.
reason: Potential contamination with Escherichia coli O7:K1 and 1303

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Dwater is recalling Purity Products MyBladder dietary supplement due to potential contamination with Escherichia coli strains O7:K1 and 1303. These bacteria may pose health risks, especially for neonates, young children, older adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. The recall is a precaution to protect consumer health.

The affected product was distributed in the United States through direct delivery, Walmart, and Amazon. It is packaged in a white HDPE 150 CC bottle with a white cap, containing 60 clear capsules of brown powder. The recalled lot is number 030425176.

As of the FDA announcement, no illnesses or adverse events related to this recall have been reported.

ProductLot NumberDescription
MyBladder Dietary Supplement030425176White HDPE 150 CC bottle with white cap, 60 clear capsules containing brown powder

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Purity Products MyBladder dietary supplement with lot number 030425176. If so, stop using it and follow the recall instructions provided by Dwater or the FDA.

title: Twin Sisters Creamery Recalls Cheese Products for Potential E. coli Contamination
headline: Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed Cheeses Recalled Due to STEC and E. coli O103 Risk
description: Twin Sisters Creamery is recalling select cheese products due to potential contamination with STEC and E. coli O103. Three illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn and Mustard Seed cheese products
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Twin Sisters Creamery, Inc. of Ferndale, Washington, is recalling Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed cheese products due to potential contamination with Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103. The affected cheeses are made from raw, unpasteurized milk and were aged for at least 60 days.

These bacteria can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, older adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include severe abdominal cramps, bloody diarrhea, and vomiting, and can appear 1 to 10 days after exposure. In rare cases, infection may lead to Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome (HUS), which can cause kidney failure.

The recalled products were distributed between July 27, 2025, and October 22, 2025, to distributors in Oregon and Washington and may have reached retail outlets. The FDA notice reports three cases of STEC infection linked to these products, including one in Oregon associated with Farmhouse cheese.

Whatcom Blue250618B

Farmhouse250616B
Peppercorn250603P

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and the Washington Department of Agriculture.

content.text: Consumers should not eat the recalled Twin Sisters Creamery cheese products. Check batch codes, dispose of affected items, and clean surfaces that may have come into contact with the cheese. Contact Twin Sisters Creamery for questions or concerns.

title: Twin Sisters Creamery Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse Cheese Recalled for E. coli Risk
headline: Peterson Company Recalls Twin Sisters Creamery Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse Cheese for STEC and E. coli O103 Contamination
description: Peterson Company recalled Twin Sisters Creamery Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheeses due to potential STEC and E. coli O103 contamination, with illnesses reported.
productDescription: Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 26, 2025, Peterson Company of Auburn, Washington, issued a voluntary recall of Twin Sisters Creamery brand Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products due to potential contamination with Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103, as stated in the FDA notice. These raw milk cheeses may pose a risk of serious foodborne illness.

STEC infections can cause severe complications, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include severe abdominal cramps, bloody diarrhea, and vomiting, typically developing 1 to 10 days after exposure. E. coli O103 infection can result in Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome (HUS), a serious condition that may cause kidney failure.

The recalled Whatcom Blue (item #28855) and Farmhouse (item #29608) cheeses were distributed to retailers, distributors, caterers, and restaurants in Colorado, Idaho, Oregon, and Washington between August 14, 2025, and October 24, 2025. The products are 5 to 6 ounces, half-moon shaped, and wrapped in clear plastic, with manufacturer codes ink jet printed or on tan-to-orange stickers.

According to the FDA, three confirmed STEC infections linked to E. coli O103 have been reported in Oregon and Washington, including one case in Oregon associated with Farmhouse cheese consumption. The recall was initiated after Twin Sisters Creamery notified Peterson Company, following third-party and government testing that confirmed the presence of the bacteria. Consumers are advised not to consume these products and to clean any surfaces that may have contacted the cheese.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 27, 2025, E. A. Sween Company voluntarily recalled specific lots of its Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwich due to the potential presence of plastic foreign particles, as announced in the official FDA notice. The presence of plastic could pose a choking hazard if consumed. The affected sandwiches were distributed to convenience stores, grocery stores, and mass retailers throughout the Continental U.S., Hawaii, and Guam.

The recall covers certain lot codes of the Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwich, UPC 0-41433-13087-2, sold between January 16 and October 23, 2025. According to the FDA, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

title: Haitai, Inc. Recalls Haetae Ground Cinnamon Powder 8 oz for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Haetae Ground Cinnamon Powder 8 oz Recalled Nationwide for Possible Lead Contamination
description: Haitai, Inc. has recalled Haetae Ground Cinnamon Powder 8 oz due to potential lead contamination. No illnesses or injuries have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice published October 17, 2025, Haitai, Inc. of Cerritos, CA, has recalled Haetae (HT) brand Cinnamon Powder 8 oz due to potential contamination with lead. Lead exposure can pose serious health risks, especially to children, including permanent central nervous system damage and developmental issues. In adults, acute lead poisoning may cause abdominal pain, muscle weakness, and other symptoms, while chronic exposure is linked to kidney problems and neurocognitive effects.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide in a square plastic bottle with a medium brown color and orange-red undertones. The affected lot is identified by UPC 0 20914 81415 9 and Best By date 02/09/25. The recall was initiated after the FDA detected elevated lead levels in product samples. No injuries or illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the notice date.

BrandProductPackage SizeUPCBest By Date
Haetae (HT)Cinnamon Powder8 oz0 20914 81415 902/09/25

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Haetae Ground Cinnamon Powder 8 oz with UPC 0 20914 81415 9 and Best By 02/09/25, and discontinue use if affected.

title: Nat's Nuts Recalls Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans for Undeclared Cashews Risk
headline: Nat's Nuts Recalls Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans Due to Undeclared Cashews
description: Nat's Nuts is recalling Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans after undeclared cashews were found. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA's 2025 notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nat's Nuts, based in Rochester, NY, has recalled its Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans due to the potential presence of undeclared cashews. This poses a risk for individuals with cashew allergies, as consuming the product could cause serious allergic reactions.

The recall covers about 600 bags of Nat's Nuts Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans (4 oz.), distributed to 25 retail stores in Connecticut, New York, New Jersey, New Hampshire, Pennsylvania, Ohio, Nevada, Florida, and Wisconsin from October 8 to October 16, 2025. The product was also sold online via Amazon and NatsNuts.com between October 9 and October 16, 2025.

The issue was discovered after a retailer found cashews in a package during sampling. The company traced the problem to a temporary production and packaging process failure. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date.

ProductSizeUPCLot CodeBest By Date
Nat's Nuts Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans4 oz.8600021467201.229/29/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Nat's Nuts Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans and avoid eating them if allergic to cashews. Refer to the official FDA notice for further instructions.

title: Jody's Inc. Recalls Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn for Undeclared Peanuts
headline: Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn Recalled by Jody's Inc. Over Peanut Allergen Risk
description: Jody's Inc. recalled Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn due to undeclared peanuts, posing a risk to those with peanut allergies. No illnesses reported.
reason: Due to presence of undeclared peanuts

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Jody's Inc. has voluntarily recalled Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn 6 oz (170 g) bags due to the presence of undeclared peanuts. Individuals with peanut allergies or sensitivities are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled popcorn was distributed beginning July 23, 2025, to distribution centers in California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Massachusetts, Maryland, North Carolina, Oregon, and Texas, and then to retail locations nationwide. The product is sold in foil printed bags and six-pack boxes.

The recall was initiated after two consumer complaints about peanut presence. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

ProductUPCLot NumberBest If Enjoyed By
Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn 6 oz (170 g) bag8 50016 94430 62519907B1July 15, 2026

title: Kenz Henz Recalls Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs for Potential Salmonella Risk
headline: Recall: Kenz Henz Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs Due to Salmonella Contamination Risk
description: Kenz Henz is recalling Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs sold in Houston, TX, due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
keywords: Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs
productDescription: Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Kenz Henz of Santa Fe, TX, has recalled its 12-count Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs due to the potential presence of Salmonella. Salmonella can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Typical symptoms include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The affected eggs were distributed to retail stores in Houston, TX. As of the FDA announcement, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. The FDA inspection of the Black Sheep Egg Company's facility found environmental samples positive for Salmonella strains, but the facility has not been linked to any ongoing outbreak.

Product DescriptionUPC CodeJulian Date RangeBest By Date RangePackage Size
Kenz Henz Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs86949400030241 to 244 and 246 to 24710/11 to 10/14 and 10/16 to 10/1712 count carton

title: AquaStar Recalls Frozen Raw Shrimp for Possible Cesium-137 Exposure
headline: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix Frozen Raw Shrimp Recalled Over Cesium-137 Risk
description: AquaStar Corp has recalled select frozen raw shrimp brands due to potential exposure to very low levels of cesium-137. No illnesses have been reported.
brandName: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix
brandNames: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix
reason: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 17, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. According to the official FDA notice, the recall was initiated because the products may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions where they could have been exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope of cesium.

The FDA states that none of the recalled products have tested positive for Cs-137, and no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date. This recall is a precautionary measure conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to ensure consumer safety.

title: Ben's Original Recalls Select Ready Rice Products for Possible Small Stone Contamination
headline: Ben's Original Voluntarily Recalls Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice Due to Small Stones
description: Ben's Original recalled certain Ready Rice products after small stones were found in select lots. No injuries or illnesses have been reported to date.
productDescription: Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice
reason: Potential Foreign Body Contaminant – Small Stones

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Ben's Original has voluntarily recalled select lots of Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice in the United States due to the potential presence of small stones. The affected products may contain small stones that could pose a risk of oral or digestive tract injury if consumed.

This recall applies only to specific batch codes and best by dates, which are printed on the bottom seal of the back of the package. Distribution included multiple retailers through Associated Grocers, C&S, and Dot Foods during August through September. No other Ben's Original products are affected by this recall.

As of the recall announcement, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with these products, per the official FDA notice.
