title: Foodynamics Recalls Freeze Dried Pet Treats for Salmonella Risk, FDA Notice 2025
headline: Foodynamics Issues 2025 Recall of Freeze Dried Pet Treats Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Foodynamics has recalled select freeze-dried pet treats due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published October 9, 2025, Foodynamics of Wales, Wisconsin, has voluntarily recalled specific lots of freeze-dried pet treats under the Raw Dog Barkery, BellePepper Cats, Kanu Pets, and What's In the Bowl brands due to potential Salmonella contamination. This poses a health risk to both pets and humans who handle the products.

Salmonella infection may cause symptoms in humans such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. Rarely, more serious complications can occur. Pets with Salmonella may show lethargy, diarrhea, fever, vomiting, or decreased appetite, but can also be asymptomatic and transmit the bacteria. The FDA reports no illnesses have been linked to this recall as of the notice date.

The recalled products were distributed via UPS to retailers in Wisconsin, New York, and Florida. Foodynamics has traced all affected products, notified direct customers, and is cooperating with the FDA. Production and distribution have been suspended as a precaution.

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop feeding the recalled freeze-dried pet treats to pets, check the lot numbers listed in the FDA notice, and discontinue use of affected products. Contact Foodynamics for further instructions if needed.

title: Recall: Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls for Undeclared Shrimp Allergen
headline: Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls Recalled for Undeclared Shrimp Allergen
description: TAI FOONG USA has recalled select Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls sold at ALDI due to undeclared shrimp, per FDA notice. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Tai Foong USA of Seattle, Washington, has recalled a limited quantity of Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls, net weight 10 oz, due to undeclared shrimp. This allergen may cause serious or life-threatening reactions in those with shrimp or shellfish allergies.

The recalled product was distributed exclusively to ALDI stores nationwide. Only the 10-ounce retail package with UPC 4099100222258 and Best Before date 05/17/2027 is affected. No other Fusia Asian Inspirations products are included.

The recall was initiated after shrimp spring rolls were mistakenly packaged as veggie spring rolls. Tai Foong USA is investigating the labeling error. The recall is conducted in cooperation with ALDI and the FDA. As of the recall notice, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

BrandProductNet WeightUPCBest Before DateRetailer
Fusia Asian InspirationsVeggie Spring Rolls10 oz (283.5 g)409910022225805/17/2027ALDI (nationwide)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls match the recalled UPC and Best Before date. Those with shrimp allergies should not eat the product. For questions, contact Tai Foong USA by phone or email as listed in the FDA notice.

title: Durra Ground Cinnamon 100g Recalled for Possible Lead Contamination
headline: Eureka Inc. Recalls Durra Ground Cinnamon 100g Due to Potential Lead
description: Eureka Inc. recalled Durra Ground Cinnamon 100g after FDA testing found possible lead contamination. No illnesses have been reported as of October 2025.
productType: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 6, 2025, Eureka Inc. of Pomona, CA, initiated a recall of Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 gram plastic containers due to potential contamination with lead, as identified by FDA testing. Lead exposure can pose significant health risks, especially to children and those with prolonged or high-level exposure.

Short-term exposure to low levels of lead may not show symptoms, but increased blood lead levels can be detected. High levels of exposure may cause abdominal pain, muscle weakness, nausea, vomiting, and neurological effects. Chronic exposure may result in kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and permanent central nervous system damage, particularly in children. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this product.

The recalled product was distributed to grocery stores in California and Michigan between August 24, 2024, and October 6, 2025. This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Meters Recalled for Defective LCD Displays
headline: Trividia Health Recalls Select TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Meters Due to LCD Display Defect
description: Trividia Health is recalling certain TRUE METRIX blood glucose meters due to a potential LCD display defect. No injuries or adverse events have been reported.
reason: Potential for defective LCD displays that can affect product performance

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of a limited number of TRUE METRIX® self-monitoring blood glucose meters co-branded under the Good Neighbor Pharmacy® brand and distributed in the United States. The recall applies to 601 meters from lot number KD0746, manufactured on September 4, 2025, and distributed between September 8 and September 16, 2025. These units may have defective LCD displays that could affect product performance.

The issue with the LCD display may cause partial or missing numerical segments or characters, or ghosting (fading) of these characters. This defect could potentially lead users to misinterpret test results or experience delays in obtaining results. For individuals with low blood glucose (hypoglycemia), such delays could impact timely treatment or therapy decisions. According to the official FDA notice, no injuries or adverse events have been reported related to this recall.

Consumers can identify affected meters by checking the serial number on the back of the device or the lot number (KD0746) printed on the side of the product box. Trividia Health is notifying customers, pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies that sell these meters in the U.S. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

ProductLot NumberManufacture DateDistribution DatesQuantity
TRUE METRIX® Self-Monitoring Blood Glucose Meter (Co-branded Good Neighbor Pharmacy®)KD0746September 4, 2025September 8, 2025 – September 16, 2025601 units

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their TRUE METRIX blood glucose meter for lot number KD0746. If your device is affected, contact Trividia Health for assistance.

This defect could potentially lead users to misinterpret test results or experience delays in obtaining results. For individuals with low blood glucose (hypoglycemia), such delays could impact timely treatment or therapy decisions. According to the official FDA notice, no injuries or adverse events have been reported related to this recall.

Consumers can identify affected meters by checking the serial number on the back of the device or the lot number (KD0746) printed on the side of the product box. Trividia Health is notifying customers, pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies that sell these meters in the U.S. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Giant Eagle Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad Recalled for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Giant Eagle Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad Recall Due to Listeria Risk
description: Giant Eagle has recalled its Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported as of the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Giant Eagle, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad sold in prepared foods departments at Giant Eagle and Market District stores in western Pennsylvania, Ohio, Maryland, and Indiana. This recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, following a related nationwide recall from Nate's Fine Foods.

Listeria monocytogenes may cause serious or fatal infections in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience mild symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage or stillbirth. As of the FDA announcement, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

title: Nature's Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Treats Recalled for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Recall: Nature's Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Treats Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Best Buy Bones, Inc. has recalled select lots of Nature's Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Treats after FDA testing found Salmonella. See affected lots and consumer guidance.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Best Buy Bones, Inc., based in Mount Morris, Michigan, has initiated a recall of four lots of Nature's Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 1 Pound pet treats due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The affected product comes in 16 oz. resealable bags with an expiration date of September 2027. Salmonella poses a health risk to both animals consuming the treats and humans handling them, particularly if proper handwashing is not observed after contact.

Salmonella infection in healthy individuals can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. In rare cases, more severe illness may occur, including arterial infections, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract symptoms. Those experiencing symptoms after handling the product should seek medical advice promptly.

Pets affected by Salmonella may show lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Even asymptomatic pets can carry and transmit the bacteria to other animals or humans. Veterinarians should be contacted if pets develop these symptoms after consuming the product.

The recall involves 34 bags distributed primarily in Minnesota and sold through retail locations in Wisconsin after September 23, 2025. The investigation began after FDA testing of product samples on September 11, 2025, confirmed the presence of Salmonella. The company has halted production and distribution of the affected lots while the FDA and Best Buy Bones continue their investigation.

ProductLot NumberExpiration DateUPCPackage Size
Nature's Own Pet Chews Bully Bites19379, 19380, 19381, 19382September 202773959890075016 oz. resealable pouch

No injuries or illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this recall, according to the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Nature's Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Treats for the affected lot numbers and expiration date. Discontinue use of recalled products and return them to the place of purchase or dispose of them safely as directed in the FDA notice.

title: Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini Recalled for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Demers Food Group Recalls Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini Due to Possible Listeria Risk
description: Demers Food Group recalled select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini bowls due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Demers Food Group has voluntarily recalled specific lots of Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini bowls due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This recall follows an ingredient recall by Nate's Fine Foods, the supplier of the linguini pasta. Only the listed lots of Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini are affected; no other Scott & Jon's products are included.

No illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with this recall as of the FDA notice dated October 2, 2025. The affected products were distributed nationwide to wholesale distributors and operators between September 15, 2025, and September 25, 2025.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. The FDA advises cleaning and sanitizing surfaces and containers that may have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination.

content.text: Consumers should check the lot codes and best by dates of their Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini bowls. If your product matches the recalled lots, follow the guidance provided by Demers Food Group and the FDA for safe handling and further assistance.

title: Albertsons Recalls Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals for Possible Listeria Risk (2025)
headline: Albertsons Companies Recalls Multiple Brand Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals Due to Listeria Concerns
description: Albertsons Companies has recalled select deli Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals under multiple brand names due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled certain store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods. This action follows an ingredient recall by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser's Fine Foods, Inc., due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination in bowtie pasta produced by Nate's Fine Foods in Roseville, California.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage or stillbirth. As of the FDA announcement, no illnesses or injuries have been reported in connection with these products.

The recalled deli items were sold under multiple brand names at Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Pavilions, Andronico's Community Markets, and other banners in several states. The affected products and details are listed below.

title: Sea Port Recalls Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo 16/20 for Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Sea Port Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo 16/20 Recalled for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
description: Sea Port Products Corp recalled select Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo 16/20 due to potential Cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
keywords: Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp
productDescription: Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20
reason: Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Sea Port Products Corp of Kirkland, WA is voluntarily recalling specific lots of Sea Port brand Frozen Raw Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20 count per pound. The recall is due to potential contamination with Cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioactive isotope, possibly resulting from insanitary conditions.

Cesium-137 is present at low levels in the environment but may be found at higher concentrations in contaminated food. Long-term exposure to Cs-137 can increase cancer risk due to DNA damage. The affected shrimp was distributed in limited quantities to retailers in Alaska, California, Hawaii, Montana, Oregon, Washington State, and American Samoa between July 10, 2025, and September 22, 2025. Consumers should check their freezers for the recalled shrimp using the lot codes and UPCs listed below.

No illnesses or adverse events related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. The FDA is investigating Cs-137 contamination reports associated with shrimp from PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. The FDA states that no products testing positive for Cs-137 have entered U.S. commerce.

title: Signature Enoki Mushrooms Recalled by New Age International for Listeria Risk
headline: Signature Enoki Mushrooms Recalled Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
description: New Age International Inc. recalled Signature Enoki Mushrooms after routine testing found possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Product may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Age International Inc., based in Brooklyn, NY, has recalled its 200g (7.05 OZ) Signature Enoki Mushrooms due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, as stated in the FDA notice. Listeria can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The affected mushrooms were distributed to retail stores and distributors in New York City and are packaged in clear 200g plastic containers labeled with GGN: 4063061794758. According to the FDA, no illnesses have been reported to date. The contamination was identified through routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets and confirmed by laboratory analysis.

ProductPackage SizeLot Code / UPCDistribution Area
Signature Enoki Mushrooms200g (7.05 OZ) clear plastic packageGGN: 4063061794758New York City

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Signature Enoki Mushrooms with the listed lot code should not consume the product. Dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase as directed in the FDA recall notice.

title: Albertsons Companies Recalls Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals for Possible Listeria Risk
headline: Albertsons Companies Issues Recall of Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
description: Albertsons Companies has recalled select Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled select store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, following an ingredient recall due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. The affected bowtie pasta ingredient was manufactured by Nate's Fine Foods of Roseville, California.

Listeria monocytogenes may cause serious infections in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms can include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Infection during pregnancy can result in miscarriage or stillbirth. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

The recalled products were sold at Albertsons Companies stores under various banners, including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Randalls, Tom Thumb, Pavilions, Carrs-Safeway, and others, across multiple states. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

title: Wholesale Produce Supply Recalls Fresh Cut Cantaloupe for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Fresh Cut Cantaloupe Recalled by Wholesale Produce Supply Due to Listeria Risk
description: Wholesale Produce Supply, LLC. has recalled fresh cut cantaloupe products due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
productType: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Wholesale Produce Supply, LLC. of Minneapolis, Minnesota, is recalling fresh cut and processed cantaloupe because of potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illness, particularly in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women may face miscarriage or stillbirth risks if infected.

The recalled products were sold under the "Harvest Cuts" and "Fresh and Finest" brands, distributed in Nebraska, North Dakota, and Wisconsin, and possibly other states. The products were packaged in plastic clamshell containers and included both cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit varieties.

This recall was initiated after routine sampling detected Listeria monocytogenes in finished products. The company has suspended production and distribution of the affected lots while investigating the source. As of the FDA notice date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

Fresh and FinestFresh Cut Cantaloupe (cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit containers)Nebraska, North Dakota, Wisconsin (and possibly other states)Plastic clamshell containers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Georgia Nut Company, a third-party manufacturer for Tru Fru, LLC, has voluntarily recalled specific varieties of Tru Fru Freeze Dried Fruit snacks due to the potential presence of metal fragments. The recall was initiated after a consumer report identified the issue. No injuries or illnesses have been reported to date.

The affected products were distributed nationwide and may have been sold at various retailers, including Albertson's, CVS, Food Lion, H-E-B, Hungryroot, Ingles Markets, Kroger, Stew Leonard's, and Target. content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Georgia Nut Company, a third-party manufacturer for Tru Fru, LLC, has voluntarily recalled specific varieties of Tru Fru Freeze Dried Fruit snacks due to the potential presence of metal fragments. The recall was initiated after a consumer report identified the issue. No injuries or illnesses have been reported to date.

The affected products were distributed nationwide and may have been sold at various retailers, including Albertson's, CVS, Food Lion, H-E-B, Hungryroot, Ingles Markets, Kroger, Stew Leonard's, and Target. Consumers can identify recalled products by the manufacturing code on the back of the package, with impacted lots listed below.

title: G&L Food Dried Bean Curd Recalled for Undeclared Wheat Allergen, FDA 2025
headline: G&L Food Dried Bean Curd Recalled Due to Undeclared Wheat Allergen
description: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd. has recalled G&L Food Dried Bean Curd after FDA found undeclared wheat allergen. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd.
companyName: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on September 27, 2025, Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd. has voluntarily recalled its 500g bags of G&L Food Dried Bean Curd. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that wheat, present in the soy sauce ingredient, was not declared on the product label. This poses a risk to individuals with wheat allergies or sensitivities, as consuming the product could result in a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction.

The recalled products were distributed primarily to restaurants and retail stores in the Bay Area of Northern California. The affected items are packaged in 500g food plastic bags with expiration dates of 25/10/2025, 11/06/2026, and 20/06/2026. The undeclared allergen was identified during an FDA inspection. As of the recall date, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported in connection with this product.

BrandProductPackage SizeUPCExpiration DatesContainer TypeRecall Reason

content.subtitle: What You Should Do

title: Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon Recalled for Elevated Lead Levels by SLR Food Distribution
headline: SLR Food Distribution Recalls Wise Wife Cinnamon Powder Due to Potential Lead Contamination
description: SLR Food Distribution has recalled Wise Wife brand ground cinnamon powder after FDA testing found elevated lead levels. No illnesses have been reported.
productType: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
reason: Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
SLR Food Distribution, Inc., based in Hicksville, NY, has issued a recall for Wise Wife brand Ground Cinnamon due to the potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. This recall involves 1.76 oz (50g) clear plastic jars with black lids, distributed to retailers in New Jersey, New York, Florida, Maryland, Minnesota, Oklahoma, and Ohio between February 15, 2024, and June 28, 2025.

Exposure to lead, even at low levels, can pose health risks. While short-term low-level exposure may not cause immediate symptoms, increased blood lead levels could be the only sign. Prolonged or high levels of lead exposure can lead to serious health issues including learning disorders and developmental defects in children, as well as kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects in adults.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration detected lead levels above acceptable limits. SLR Food Distribution has ceased sales of the product as of June 28, 2025.

ProductPackage SizeUPCDistribution DatesRetail States
Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon1.76 oz (50g) jar0 688474 30285302/15/2024 – 06/28/2025NJ, NY, FL, MD, MN, OK, OH

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Wise Wife Cinnamon powder distributed by SLR Food Distribution should check their product for possible recall due to elevated lead levels. Do not use the product until you confirm its safety with the company.

title: AquaStar Shrimp Skewers Recalled for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: AquaStar Recalls Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers Due to Potential Radionuclide Risk
description: AquaStar (USA) Corp has recalled certain Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers due to possible Cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice published on September 25, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp is voluntarily recalling approximately 8,000 bags of AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). The affected shrimp skewers, imported from Indonesia, may have been processed under unsanitary conditions, leading to concerns about radioactive contamination.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that can be present in the environment. Long-term exposure to Cs-137 through food consumption may increase cancer risk due to DNA damage. The recalled shrimp skewers, each 1.25 pounds, were distributed to Food Lion stores in Delaware, Georgia, Kentucky, Maryland, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia, and sold between July 7, 2025, and September 20, 2025. As of the recall date, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with this product.

UPCLot CodeBest If Used By Date
73114939001010662 5127 1011 07 2027
73114939001010662 5128 1111 08 2027

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot codes on AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers and avoid consuming products included in the recall due to possible Cesium-137 contamination.

title: Goot Essa Recalls Der Mutterschaf Cheese for Listeria Risk in Batch 33
headline: Der Mutterschaf Cheese by Goot Essa Recalled Due to Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Goot Essa LLC has recalled specific lots of Der Mutterschaf Cheese due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Goot Essa LLC of Howard, Pennsylvania, is recalling batch number 33 of its Der Mutterschaf Cheese due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Infections during pregnancy can result in miscarriage or stillbirth.

The recall affects 64 pounds of cheese distributed to seven customers in New Jersey, Virginia, Connecticut, and Montana through retail stores and restaurants. The affected product is packaged in clear plastic in 4 oz and 8 oz sizes, with UPC codes and lot number 33 on the back label.

The contamination was discovered during routine company testing. No illnesses have been reported to date. Goot Essa is cooperating with the FDA in the ongoing investigation.

Product NamePackage SizeUPCLot Number
Goot Essa Der Mutterschaf Cheese4 oz81015456018933
Goot Essa Der Mutterschaf Cheese8 oz81015456019633

title: Sprout Organics Recalls Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouches for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Sprout Organics Issues Recall of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouches Due to Lead Risk
description: Sprout Organics has recalled select Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz pouches for possible lead contamination. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA.
keywords: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach pouch
productDescription: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sprout Organics has expanded its voluntary recall of the Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch to include additional lot codes due to potentially elevated lead levels. According to the FDA notice, lead exposure, even at low levels, may increase blood lead levels, with greater risk depending on exposure amount and duration. Children are especially vulnerable, as prolonged lead exposure can impact learning, development, and cause other long-term health effects.

The recalled product was distributed at Walgreens, independent retailers in select states, and online. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with this recall. The recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and does not affect other Sprout Organics products.

  • Check expiration date and lot code on the back bottom strip of the pouch.
  • Return affected product to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • More about Sprout Organics testing program: https://sproutorganics.com/pages/faq

The recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and does not affect other Sprout Organics products.

title: Southwind Foods Recalls Multiple Brands of Frozen Shrimp for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Frozen Shrimp from Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street Recalled Due to Potential Cesium-137
description: Southwind Foods, LLC has recalled select frozen shrimp products distributed under five brands in 2025 due to possible Cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses reported.
brandName: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandNames: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC of Carson, California, has issued a voluntary recall of certain frozen shrimp products due to potential contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cs-137 is a manmade radioactive isotope that may be present in the environment and can accumulate in food from affected areas. According to the FDA, long-term exposure to low doses of Cs-137 may increase cancer risk by damaging DNA in living cells.

The recalled frozen shrimp products were distributed between June 24 and September 16, 2025, to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers in 35 states. The recall follows an FDA investigation into Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. As of the recall notice, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported.

Sand Bar / Arctic Shores / Best Yet / Great American / First StreetFrozen ShrimpJune 24 – September 16, 2025Alabama, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Montana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington, Wisconsin, Wyoming

For more details, see the official FDA recall notice at FDA Recalls, Market Withdrawals & Safety Alerts.

title: Olympus Recalls ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles Due to Detachment Risk
headline: Olympus Recalls Certain ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles for Device Component Detachment
description: Olympus has recalled specific lots of ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles after reports of device components detaching during procedures. See FDA notice for details.
reason: Device Components May Detach During Procedures

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Olympus Corporation has issued a voluntary recall of certain lots of its ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles manufactured before May 12, 2025. The recall was initiated after reports indicated that device components may detach during medical procedures, potentially posing a risk to patient safety.

The ViziShot 2 FLEX needle contains a hypotube and sheath for device stability. Damage to the sheath, which may occur during manufacturing or use, can result in detachment of the hypotube and plastic components. Detached parts are not visible on X-rays, increasing the risk of retained fragments in the tracheobronchial tree and possible need for removal procedures.

Olympus has implemented enhanced quality controls, including automated inspections, for products manufactured after May 12, 2025. The company advises customers to quarantine and return affected products as instructed. Healthcare providers should confirm that needles are from unaffected lots and monitor patients as appropriate. No injuries or adverse events have been reported to date, per the FDA notice.

ProductModelLot NumbersManufacture Date
ViziShot 2 FLEX EBUS-TBNA Needle19GAll lots manufactured before May 12, 2025Prior to May 12, 2025
  • Report adverse events or quality problems to the FDA's MedWatch program online: https://www.fda.gov/safety/medwatch-fda-safety-information-and-adverse-event-reporting-program/reporting-serious-problems-fda
  • Contact Olympus Technical Assistance Center at 1-800-848-9024, Option 1
  • Email Olympus complaints at: complaints@olympus.com
  • Follow Olympus instructions for quarantining and returning affected devices

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Check if your Olympus ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles are included in the recall by reviewing lot details on the FDA website. Follow Olympus's instructions for quarantining and returning affected products if applicable.

title: Aquastar Corp Recalls Kroger, Kroger Mercado, and AquaStar Shrimp Products for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Recall: Kroger, Kroger Mercado, and AquaStar Shrimp Due to Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
description: Aquastar Corp recalled select Kroger, Kroger Mercado, and AquaStar shrimp products due to possible Cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: Raw Shrimp, Cooked Shrimp, Shrimp Skewers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On September 19, 2025, Aquastar Corp announced a recall of certain frozen shrimp products due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137), as detailed in the official FDA notice. The affected products include Kroger Raw Colossal EZ Peel Shrimp, Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, and AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers. These products may have been prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, leading to possible contamination.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioisotope that can be present in the environment at background or elevated levels in contaminated areas. Long-term, repeated exposure through food or water intake may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage in living cells. According to the FDA, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

The recalled shrimp products were distributed to multiple grocery retailers, including Baker's, City Market, Dillons, Food 4 Less, Foodsco, Fred Meyer, Fry's, Gerbes, Jay C, King Soopers, Kroger, Mariano's, Metro Market, Pay Less Supermarkets, Pick 'n Save, Ralphs, Smith's, and QFC, and were available between June 12, 2025, and September 17, 2025.

title: Haifa Smoked Fish Recalls Cold Smoked Salmon and Seabass for Listeria Risk
headline: Haifa Smoked Fish Issues Recall of Cold Smoked Salmon and Seabass Due to Listeria
description: Haifa Smoked Fish has recalled Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass nationwide due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Haifa Smoked Fish of Jamaica, NY, has recalled its "Cold Smoked Salmon" 8 oz. (Lot #219) and "Cold Smoked Seabass" 8 oz. (Lot #212) due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Listeria infection may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Infections can be especially severe for pregnant women, potentially resulting in miscarriages or stillbirths. The recalled products were distributed nationwide to retail stores and distributors.

The contamination was identified through sampling and testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets. According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall as of the announcement date.

ProductSizeLot Number
Cold Smoked Salmon8 oz.219
Cold Smoked Seabass8 oz.212
  • Potential hazard: Listeria monocytogenes contamination
  • Risk groups: young children, elderly, pregnant women, immunocompromised individuals
  • Distribution: nationwide to retail stores and distributors
  • Packaging: vacuum packed on a paper board with flexible plastic

facts.product: Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Haifa brand Cold Smoked Salmon (Lot #219) or Cold Smoked Seabass (Lot #212) and avoid consuming these products if they match the recalled lots.

title: Sprout Organics Recalls Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouch for Possible Lead Contamination
headline: Sprout Organics Issues Recall of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouch Due to Elevated Lead Levels
description: Sprout Organics has recalled one lot of its Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch due to potential elevated lead levels. No illnesses have been reported.
keywords: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach pouch
productDescription: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch
reason: Product may contain elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Sprout Organics has voluntarily recalled one lot of its Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch because the product may contain elevated levels of lead. Lead exposure can increase blood lead levels and may result in serious health risks, particularly for children. Long-term or high-level exposure to lead can affect learning, development, and cause other health complications.

The affected lot was distributed mainly through Walgreens and select independent stores in the US South region, with most sales between September and December 2024. The product was not distributed through other major grocery chains. No illnesses or adverse events related to this recall have been reported. No other Sprout Organics products are included in this recall, as stated in the official FDA notice.

content.text: Consumers should check if their Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch matches the recalled lot code and expiration date. If affected, follow the FDA's guidance to stop using the product and return it to the place of purchase.

title: BD Alaris Pump Module Model 8100 Recall Expands to Additional Infusion Sets
headline: BD Alaris Pump Module Model 8100 Recall: Performance Variations May Impact Infusion Delivery
description: BD has expanded its 2025 recall for the Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with certain infusion sets due to pump performance variations that may affect infusion accuracy.
productDescription: BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets
reason: Pump performance variations compared to the performance described in the user manual could impact infusion delivery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has expanded its Class I voluntary recall announced earlier in 2025 for the BD Alaris™ Pump Module model 8100 when used with certain compatible pump infusion sets. This recall update includes 15 additional pump infusion sets, some of which have been discontinued but may still be in inventory based on their expiration dates. The recall addresses performance variations that differ from the user manual specifications, potentially impacting infusion delivery accuracy.

These performance issues involve flow rate inaccuracies, delays in occlusion alarms, and over-infusion of post-occlusion bolus volume. Such deviations could lead to serious adverse events, including patient death, particularly affecting vulnerable groups such as neonates and critically ill patients. The factors contributing to these issues include design features of the pump infusion sets, such as filters and other in-line components. According to the official FDA notice, injuries and adverse events have been reported.

BD has provided updated corrective actions and risk mitigation recommendations, including suggestions to use alternative BD Alaris™ Pump infusion sets if clinically appropriate. The recalled products were distributed nationwide across the United States, including Guam and Puerto Rico, as well as in Canada, Belgium, and South Africa.

Impacted Pump Infusion Sets (15 Additional Models)
Details of the specific 15 additional pump infusion sets were provided in BD's customer update (not included specifically in the source text).

facts.product: BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and healthcare providers using the BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 with compatible pump infusion sets should check their devices for inclusion in the recall due to potential pump performance variations. Verify product details and follow BD's instructions if your device is affected.

title: Gooder Foods Recalls Goodles Flavored Pasta for Undeclared Milk and Cashews
headline: Gooder Foods Recalls Goodles Vegan Is Believin' and Here Comes Truffle Pasta Due to Undeclared Allergens
description: Gooder Foods recalled select Goodles pasta products after undeclared milk and cashews were found. Eight allergic reactions have been reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Gooder Foods, Inc. has recalled specific lots of Goodles Vegan Is Believin' – Plant Based White Cheddar with Spirals and Goodles Here Comes Truffle – Creamy Truffle Flavored Cheddar and Shells due to undeclared milk and cashews. The recall affects five lots of Vegan Is Believin' and three lots of Here Comes Truffle, distributed nationwide between April 29 and August 5, 2025. Individuals with milk or cashew allergies may face serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume these products.

The FDA notice states that eight allergic reactions have been reported in connection with these products—six for Here Comes Truffle and two for Vegan Is Believin'. Gooder Foods is working with the FDA and has notified distributors, retailers, and consumers about the recall.

title: Deep Frozen Fruits and Vegetables Recalled by Chetak LLC Group for Salmonella Risk
headline: Chetak LLC Group Expands Recall of Deep Frozen Fruits and Vegetables Due to Salmonella
description: Chetak LLC Group has expanded its recall of Deep frozen fruits and vegetables nationwide due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Chetak LLC Group has expanded its voluntary recall of Deep-brand frozen vegetable and fruit products due to potential Salmonella contamination. The recall, first announced on July 16, 2025, now includes additional lots produced between December 18, 2024, and August 18, 2025.

Salmonella infection may cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms can include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this recall, as stated in the official notice.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide and packaged in flexible plastic bags. Consumers are advised to check lot numbers and use-by dates on the back panel of the packages. Production of the affected products has been suspended while the company investigates the source of contamination. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Deep-brand Sprouted Mat (Moth)IN 24353K, IN 24354K, IN 25072K

Deep-brand Premium Select Surti Undhiu MixIN 25158K
Deep-brand Premium Select SingodaIN 25150K

title: Gina Marie Bakery Recalls No Brand Cookies for Undeclared Almonds, Sesame, and Food Dyes
headline: No Brand Cookies Recalled by Gina Marie Bakery for Undeclared Allergens and Dyes
description: Gina Marie Bakery recalled No Brand cookies due to undeclared almonds, sesame, and food dyes. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA notice.
reason: Undeclared almonds, sesame, Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, Red 40, Yellow 6

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Gina Marie Bakery of Waterbury, Connecticut, has recalled several No Brand cookie products due to undeclared allergens, including almonds, sesame, and food dyes such as Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, and Yellow 6. The affected cookies include Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies, Italian Sesame Cookies, Vanilla Chocolate Dipped Cookies, Vanilla with Apricot Jam Cookies, Toasted Almond & Cherry Biscotti, and Vanilla Cookies with Raspberry Peach Jam.

The recalled cookies were distributed mainly in Connecticut at grocery and specialty stores, including Big Y, Stop and Shop, Labonne's Supermarkets, Adam's Markets, Highland Park Markets, Price Chopper, Kensington Market, and all Nardelli's Connecticut stores. Most packages are 1 lb (454g) clamshell containers, with Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies also available in 2 lb (908g) size.

No illnesses related to these products have been reported as of the FDA recall notice. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product labels did not declare the presence of these allergens and dyes.

en.headline: No Brand Cookies Recalled by Gina Marie Bakery for Undeclared Allergens and Dyes
en.description: Gina Marie Bakery recalled No Brand cookies due to undeclared almonds, sesame, and food dyes. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA notice.
en.reason: Undeclared almonds, sesame, Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, Red 40, Yellow 6

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Gina Marie Bakery of Waterbury, Connecticut, has recalled several No Brand cookie products due to undeclared allergens, including almonds, sesame, and food dyes such as Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, and Yellow 6. The affected cookies include Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies, Italian Sesame Cookies, Vanilla Chocolate Dipped Cookies, Vanilla with Apricot Jam Cookies, Toasted Almond & Cherry Biscotti, and Vanilla Cookies with Raspberry Peach Jam.

The recalled cookies were distributed mainly in Connecticut at grocery and specialty stores, including Big Y, Stop and Shop, Labonne's Supermarkets, Adam's Markets, Highland Park Markets, Price Chopper, Kensington Market, and all Nardelli's Connecticut stores. Most packages are 1 lb (454g) clamshell containers, with Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies also available in 2 lb (908g) size.

No illnesses related to these products have been reported as of the FDA recall notice. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product labels did not declare the presence of these allergens and dyes.

Italian Mixed Vanilla CookiesAlmonds, Sesame, Red 40, Red 3, Blue 11 lb (454g), 2 lb (908g)

Vanilla with Apricot Jam CookiesRed 40, Yellow 61 lb (454g)
Toasted Almond & Cherry BiscottiRed 401 lb (454g)
Vanilla Cookies with Raspberry Peach JamRed 3, Blue 11 lb (454g)

title: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op and Sunrise Creamery Recall Multiple Cheese Products for Listeria Risk
headline: Recall of Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op and Sunrise Creamery Cheeses Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op and Sunrise Creamery have recalled select cheese products due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
brandName: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery
brandNames: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op is recalling several cheese products produced at its Middlefield, Ohio facility due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Infection during pregnancy can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth.

The affected products were produced on May 30, 2025, and August 13, 2025, and distributed between July 7, 2025, and August 14, 2025, in Ohio, Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Texas. The recall includes Organic Gouda, Mozzarella/Provolone, Colored Cheddar, Swiss, Pepper Jack, Monterey Jack, and Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack cheeses in various package sizes and lot codes.

The recall was initiated after company testing detected Listeria monocytogenes in finished products and on equipment. As of the FDA notice date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

ProductPackage SizeLot Code / LabelSell By Date
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Organic Gouda8 oz. packages-2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Mozzarella/Provolone (shredded)5 lb. bags-9/3/2025
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Colored (Yellow) Cheddar (Smoked Cheddar and All Natural Mild Cheddar)5 lb. loaves-2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Colored (Yellow) Cheddar (Shredded Cheddar Cheese)5 lb. bags (shredded)-9/3/2025
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Swiss Cheese5 lb. loaves-2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Pepper Jack (All Natural Pepper Jack)5 lb. loaves-2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Pepper Jack (shredded)5 lb. bags-9/3/2025
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Monterey Jack5 lb. loaves-2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Monterey Jack (shredded)5 lb. bags-9/3/2025
Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack with Pickles6 oz. packageLot 2530541-7-26, 1-11-26, 2-16-26
Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack with Pickles1.25 lb. packageLot 25305412-30-25
  • Lot codes and sell-by dates for Sunrise Creamery products are on the side of each package.
  • Sell-by dates for Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op products are on stickers on each 5 lb. loaf or on the box for shredded cheese bags.

For more details, see the official FDA recall notice.

title: Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream Recalled for Undeclared Almond Allergen by Ice Cream Factory
headline: Recall: Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream Contains Undeclared Almond Allergen
description: Ice Cream Factory has recalled Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream due to undeclared almond allergen. No illnesses reported. FDA recall notice issued September 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Ice Cream Factory of Mount Vernon, NY, has recalled certain Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream containers due to undeclared almond allergen. The recall includes 40 pints, 8 quarts, and 3 half gallons distributed in New York State. People with tree nut allergies or sensitivities risk serious allergic reactions if they consume the affected product.

The recalled Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream was sold at Golden Krust retail stores in Mount Vernon and White Plains, NY, and Farm Country Supermarket in Queens, NY. Products were distributed via direct delivery and are packaged in plastic pints and half gallons, and white paper-coated quart containers. All affected products have an expiration date of March 2027.

The recall was initiated after the presence of undeclared almond was discovered, attributed to a temporary production and packaging issue. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

Product SizeUPC CodeExpiration Date
Pint8 56781 00361 3March 2027
Quart8 56781 00362 0March 2027
Half Gallon8 56781 00363 7March 2027

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream packaging for the listed UPC codes and expiration date. Do not consume the product if you have an almond allergy or are unsure if your item is affected. For more information, contact Ice Cream Factory at the number provided in the official FDA notice.

title: Hans Kissle Recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad Recalled Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Hans Kissle recalled select Red Bliss Potato Salad after discovering undeclared wheat, posing a risk to those with wheat allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hans Kissle LLC has voluntarily recalled 120 units of Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad because the product contains undeclared wheat, which may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in individuals with wheat allergies. The recall was initiated after containers of Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad were mislabeled with Red Bliss Potato Salad top labels, omitting the wheat allergen declaration.

The affected product was distributed to Stop and Shop retail stores in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. The product is packaged in a 16-ounce clear plastic container with a white lid, labeled as Red Bliss Potato Salad but containing Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad with wheat.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice.

ProductSizeUPC CodeUse By DatesDistribution Areas
Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad (mislabeled Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad)16 oz, clear plastic container with white lid0362176737068/20/25, 9/25/25MA, RI, CT, NJ, NY (Stop and Shop retail locations)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad should check the UPC and use by dates. If affected, do not consume the product and return it to the store for a full refund.

title: Southwind Foods Recalls Frozen Shrimp for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Frozen Shrimp Recalled by Southwind Foods Over Potential Cesium-137 Risk
description: Southwind Foods, LLC is recalling select frozen shrimp sold under multiple brands due to possible Cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
brandName: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandNames: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text: According to the FDA, Southwind Foods, LLC of Carson, California, is voluntarily recalling certain frozen shrimp products due to potential contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cs-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that may increase cancer risk with prolonged, repeated exposure. The affected shrimp was distributed between July 17 and August 8, 2025, to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers in Alabama, Arizona, California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Utah, Virginia, and Washington. This recall follows an FDA investigation into Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) of Indonesia. No illnesses or adverse events related to this issue have been reported as of the recall notice.

facts.brand: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the FDA recall website for affected lot details. If you have purchased these frozen shrimp products, do not consume them.

title: Green Lumber Dietary Supplement Recall for Undeclared Tadalafil, FDA Alert 2025
headline: Green Lumber Recalls Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared Prescription Drug Tadalafil
description: Green Lumber Holding, LLC is recalling certain dietary supplements after FDA testing found undeclared tadalafil in counterfeit products. Consumers are urged to verify lot numbers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Green Lumber Holding, LLC has issued a consumer alert for counterfeit dietary supplement products labeled as Green Lumber after FDA testing identified undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug, in these items. Authentic Green Lumber products do not contain tadalafil. The presence of this drug can pose serious health risks, particularly for individuals taking nitrates or medications for heart conditions and blood pressure.

An internal investigation by the company found that a former employee diverted authentic packaging and customer information to distribute counterfeit and adulterated products. Consumers who suspect they have counterfeit products are encouraged to report them to Green Lumber and the FDA's MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program. title: Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp Recalled for Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Aquastar Corp Issues Recall of Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp Due to Cs-137 Risk
description: Aquastar Corp has recalled select Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp due to possible cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 27, 2025, Aquastar (USA) Corp of Seattle, WA announced a recall of approximately 18,000 bags of Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp (net weight 2 lbs) due to potential contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope. The shrimp may have been prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, which could have led to contamination.

Cs-137 is present in the environment at varying levels, especially in areas affected by environmental contamination. Long-term exposure to Cs-137 through consumption of contaminated food or water can increase cancer risk due to DNA damage. The recalled shrimp were sold between July 24, 2025 and August 11, 2025 at retailers including Baker's, Gerbes, Jay C, Kroger, Mariano's, Metro Market, Pay Less Supermarkets, and Pick 'n Save across multiple states.

The FDA is investigating reports of Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and frozen shrimp products processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) of Indonesia. According to the FDA, no illnesses have been reported and no products testing positive for Cs-137 have entered U.S. commerce. The recall is a precaution for products from shipments tied to the supplier after detection of contamination.

UPCLot CodeBest Before DateProduct Description
01111062619610662 513911/19/2027Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, 2 lbs
01111062619610662 514011/20/2027Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, 2 lbs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp for the affected lot codes and UPCs listed in the recall. Do not consume recalled products; dispose of them or return to the place of purchase for a refund, as advised by the FDA.

title: DermaRite Expands Recall of Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, and Deodorant for Potential Burkholderia cepacia Contamination
headline: DermaRite Issues Expanded Nationwide Recall for Multiple Skin Care Products Due to Burkholderia cepacia Risk
description: DermaRite Industries has expanded its nationwide recall of hand sanitizers, cleansers, skin protectants, and deodorant due to potential Burkholderia cepacia contamination, according to the FDA.
productDescription: Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, Deodorant
reason: Device & Drug Safety - Potential Burkholderia cepacian contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, DermaRite Industries, LLC has expanded its voluntary nationwide recall of certain over-the-counter products due to potential contamination with Burkholderia cepacia complex, a microorganism that can cause serious infections. This recall expansion follows an initial recall announced on July 16 and now includes additional products and lots. The affected products are used for skin protection, cleansing, deodorizing, and antifungal purposes.

The FDA states that Burkholderia cepacia complex poses significant risk especially to immunosuppressed individuals, who may suffer life-threatening infections if exposed. Individuals with minor skin lesions could experience local infections, and the infection could potentially enter the bloodstream and lead to sepsis in vulnerable populations. Healthy individuals with intact skin are at a lower risk.

The recall covers a variety of over-the-counter (OTC) products, including hand sanitizers, skin protectants, antifungal creams, and antiseptic cleansers. These products were distributed nationwide in the United States and Puerto Rico. DermaRite has notified distributors and customers to check their inventory and follow facility protocols for destruction of impacted products.

As of the FDA notice dated August 28, 2025, no injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported.

Product NameDescription
4-N-1No rinse wash cream to temporarily protect and relieve chafed or cracked skin
DermaCerinSkin protectant moisturizing cream to relieve dry, chapped, or cracked skin and protect against drying effects of wind and cold
DermaFungalOTC antifungal cream for treatment and prevention of athlete's foot, jock itch, and ringworm
DermaKleenOTC healthcare antiseptic lotion soap with Vitamin E for handwashing to reduce bacteria
DermaMedOTC skin protectant to dry oozing and weeping from poison ivy, oak, sumac, and other skin irritations
Gel RiteOTC instant gel hand sanitizer with Vitamin E to decrease bacteria, recommended for repeated use
Hand E FoamOTC foaming hand sanitizer with Vitamin E for handwashing to decrease bacteria, recommended for repeated use
KleenFoamOTC antimicrobial foam soap with Aloe Vera for handwashing to decrease bacteria after diaper changes, assisting ill people, or before contact with medically treated persons
LantisepticOTC skin protectant to temporarily protect minor cuts, scrapes, burns, and chapped or cracked skin
PeriGieneOTC antiseptic cleanser for use in the perineal area
PeriGuardOTC skin protectant to relieve and prevent rashes and irritation due to wetness from incontinence, and protect chafed skin
Renew DimethiconeOTC skin protectant to help treat and prevent diaper rash and protect minor skin irritations
Renew PeriprotectOTC skin protectant for treatment and prevention of diaper rash, minor skin irritation, and sealing out wetness
Renew Skin RepairOTC skin cream to temporarily protect and relieve chapped or cracked skin on face, hands, body, and legs
UltraSureOTC antiperspirant and deodorant to reduce underarm wetness

  • Email inquiries can be directed to dermarite5186@sedgwick.com
  • Consult a healthcare provider if you experience any health issues related to these products
  • Original recall notice available at FDA.gov

title: Aqua Star Cocktail Shrimp 6oz Recalled for Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Aqua Star Cocktail Shrimp 6oz Recalled Due to Possible Cesium-137 Contaminant
description: Aquastar Corp recalled select Aqua Star Cocktail Shrimp 6oz sold at Walmart in 2025 for possible cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses reported per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 28, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp of Seattle, WA announced a recall of approximately 26,460 packages of their Cocktail Shrimp 6oz product imported from Indonesia. The recall was initiated due to potential contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope that may pose health risks if consumed over time through contaminated food.

The affected shrimp were distributed exclusively at Walmart stores in 29 states, including Alaska, Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, and others, between July 31 and August 16, 2025. The product is packaged in a clear plastic tray with a red and white label, identified by UPC 19434612191 and specific lot codes. It is refrigerated and has a 12-day shelf life.

According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to protect consumer health.

title: Country Eggs, NIJIYA, GOLDEN YOLK Large Brown Cage Free Eggs Recalled for Salmonella Risk
headline: Recall: Country Eggs, NIJIYA, GOLDEN YOLK Large Brown Cage Free Eggs Due to Salmonella
description: Country Eggs, LLC has recalled Large Brown Cage Free Eggs sold under Country Eggs, NIJIYA, and GOLDEN YOLK brands due to potential Salmonella contamination. 92 illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Country Eggs, LLC of Lucerne Valley, California has recalled its Large Brown Cage Free "Sunshine Yolks" eggs because of potential Salmonella contamination, according to the official FDA notice. Salmonella can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can lead to more severe complications.

The recalled eggs were distributed in California and Nevada to grocery stores and food service distributors. Products were sold in retail cartons under several brand names and in bulk packaging, with specific code dates and produce codes listed below. Production has been suspended while the FDA and Country Eggs, LLC investigate the source of contamination.

As of the recall notice, 92 illnesses have been reported in connection with these eggs. No deaths have been reported. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product VariantCode Dates (Sell By)Produce Code
Nagatoshi Produce Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks7/1/25 through 9/18/25No. CA 7695
Misuho Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks7/1/25 through 9/18/25No. CA 7695
Nijiya Markets Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks7/1/25 through 9/18/25No. CA 7695
Country Eggs Bulk Pack (1/15 Dozen) - Large Brown Sunshine Yolks for Foodservice7/1/25 through 9/18/25Not specified

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Country Eggs, LLC of Lucerne Valley, California has recalled its Large Brown Cage Free "Sunshine Yolks" eggs because of potential Salmonella contamination, according to the official FDA notice. Salmonella can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can lead to more severe complications.

The recalled eggs were distributed in California and Nevada to grocery stores and food service distributors. Products were sold in retail cartons under several brand names and in bulk packaging, with specific code dates and produce codes listed below. Production has been suspended while the FDA and Country Eggs, LLC investigate the source of contamination.

As of the recall notice, 92 illnesses have been reported in connection with these eggs. No deaths have been reported. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Unichem Pharmaceuticals Recalls Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg Due to Mislabeling
headline: Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg Recalled by Unichem Pharmaceuticals for Mislabeling
description: Unichem Pharmaceuticals recalled one lot of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg after bottles were found to contain Meloxicam 7.5 mg. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA) Inc. has voluntarily recalled one lot of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg nationwide due to mislabeling. The affected bottles labeled as Cyclobenzaprine 10 mg (90 count) actually contain Meloxicam 7.5 mg tablets. This labeling error presents a risk to patients who may unknowingly take Meloxicam instead of Cyclobenzaprine.

Meloxicam is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to treat conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Taking Meloxicam unknowingly can lead to serious adverse events, especially for patients using other NSAIDs, blood thinners, or those with allergies or underlying health conditions. According to the official FDA notice, no adverse events or injuries related to this recall have been reported.

The affected product can be identified by lot number GMML24026A, expiration date September 2027, and NDC 29300-415-19 on the 90-count bottle label. This lot was distributed nationwide to distributors, retailers, and consumers. Unichem Pharmaceuticals is notifying all downstream partners and coordinating the return of the affected product through their third-party recall provider, Inmar.

ProductLot NumberExpiration DateNDCCount
Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg (mislabeled, contains Meloxicam 7.5 mg)GMML24026ASeptember 202729300-415-1990

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg are from lot GMML24026A and, if so, return them to the pharmacy where purchased. Do not use tablets from this lot.

title: Viva Raw Recalls Ground Beef and Chicken Pet Foods for Salmonella and Listeria Risk
headline: Viva Raw Recalls Ground Beef and Chicken Pet Foods Due to Salmonella and Listeria Contamination
description: Viva Raw LLC recalled select lots of Ground Beef and Ground Chicken pet foods after Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes were detected. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Ground Chicken for Dogs and Cats
productDescription: Ground Beef for Dogs and Ground Chicken for Dogs and Cats
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 25, 2025, Viva Raw LLC, based in Charlotte, NC, issued a voluntary recall of two lots of dog and cat foods due to contamination with Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes, as stated in the official FDA notice. The recall includes Lot 21495 (Viva Ground Beef for Dogs, Viva Beef & Turkey for Puppies) and Lot 21975 (Viva Ground Chicken for Dogs, Viva Chunked Chicken for Dogs, Viva Chicken for Cats, Viva Pure Chicken).

According to the FDA, Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes can pose health risks to both pets and humans. Pets consuming contaminated food may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea, fever, and vomiting. People handling the affected products are also at risk of illness. The affected products were distributed frozen in 1 lb. vacuum-sealed bricks between July 2, 2025, and August 21, 2025, through direct sales nationwide and select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, and AZ. The lot number is printed on the top of the packaging. Routine testing by the North Carolina Department of Agriculture identified the contamination. No illnesses related to these products have been reported to date.

Lot NumberProduct NamesDistribution DatesPackagingDistribution Locations
21495Viva Ground Beef for Dogs; Viva Beef & Turkey for PuppiesJuly 2, 2025 – August 21, 2025Frozen 1 lb. bricks, clear vacuum packagingNationwide (direct to consumers), select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, AZ
21975Viva Ground Chicken for Dogs; Viva Chunked Chicken for Dogs; Viva Chicken for Cats; Viva Pure ChickenJuly 2, 2025 – August 21, 2025Frozen 1 lb. bricks, clear vacuum packagingNationwide (direct to consumers), select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, AZ

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

title: Southwind Foods Recalls Multiple Brands of Frozen Shrimp for Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Recall: Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, First Street Frozen Shrimp Due to Cesium-137
description: Southwind Foods, LLC has recalled select frozen shrimp products sold under several brands due to potential Cesium-137 contamination. No illnesses reported.
brandName: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandNames: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC, based in Carson, California, has voluntarily recalled specific frozen shrimp products due to potential contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cesium-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that may increase cancer risk with long-term exposure. The affected shrimp products were distributed from July 17 to August 8, 2025.

Products were sold under the brands Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street, and distributed in Alabama, Arizona, California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Utah, Virginia, and Washington. The FDA is investigating Cs-137 contamination linked to shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. According to the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported to date.

title: B. Braun Recalls Lactated Ringer's and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL for Particulate Matter Risk
headline: B. Braun Recalls Lactated Ringer's and Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL Nationwide for Particulate Matter
description: B. Braun Medical, Inc. recalled certain lots of Lactated Ringer's and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL due to particulate matter risk. No injuries reported.
productDescription: Lactated Ringer's Injection USP 1000 mL/0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL
reason: Due to the presence of particulate matter.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
B. Braun Medical, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of two lots of Lactated Ringer's Injection USP 1000 mL and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL. According to the FDA recall notice, the recall was initiated due to the presence of particulate matter inside the containers, which poses a potential risk to patients.

No serious injuries, deaths, or adverse events have been reported to date. The FDA states that particulate matter in intravenous solutions may cause complications such as pulmonary emboli, blood vessel occlusions, phlebitis, systemic immune activation, organ dysfunction, and hemolysis. If particulate matter is detected before use, treatment may be delayed while a replacement is obtained.

Lactated Ringer's Injection USP 1000 mL is used for hydration and electrolyte replacement, while 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL is indicated for extracellular fluid replacement and treatment of mild sodium depletion. Both products were distributed nationwide in boxes of 12 units.

0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mLE80000264-7800-09V3K77015 NOV 2023 - 25 SEP 202431 JAN 2026US

Consumers and healthcare providers are encouraged to report any adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA's MedWatch program. This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. All information is based on the official FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
B. Braun Medical, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of two lots of Lactated Ringer's Injection USP 1000 mL and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL. According to the FDA recall notice, the recall was initiated due to the presence of particulate matter inside the containers, which poses a potential risk to patients.

No serious injuries, deaths, or adverse events have been reported to date. The FDA states that particulate matter in intravenous solutions may cause complications such as pulmonary emboli, blood vessel occlusions, phlebitis, systemic immune activation, organ dysfunction, and hemolysis. If particulate matter is detected before use, treatment may be delayed while a replacement is obtained.

Lactated Ringer's Injection USP 1000 mL is used for hydration and electrolyte replacement, while 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL is indicated for extracellular fluid replacement and treatment of mild sodium depletion. Both products were distributed nationwide in boxes of 12 units.

ProductCatalog NumberNDC NumberLot NumberDistribution RangeExpiration DateRegion Distributed

0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mLE80000264-7800-09V3K77015 NOV 2023 - 25 SEP 202431 JAN 2026US

Consumers and healthcare providers are encouraged to report any adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA's MedWatch program. This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. All information is based on the official FDA recall notice.
