title: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op Recalls Multiple Cheese Products for Listeria Risk
headline: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op Issues Recall of Pepper Jack, Horseradish, Monterey Jack, and Farmers Cheese Due to Listeria monocytogenes
description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op is recalling select cheese products sold in Ohio due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
brandName: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese
brandNames: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op has recalled several cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage and stillbirth.

The recall affects 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack Cheese, Horseradish Flavored Cheese, Monterey Jack Cheese, and Farmers Cheese produced on June 16, June 24, and July 16, 2025. These products were distributed in Ohio between July 14 and August 7, 2025. The recall was initiated after company testing detected Listeria monocytogenes, and production has been suspended while the source is investigated.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with these products as of the FDA notice.

title: Recall of Select t:slim X2 Insulin Pumps by Tandem Diabetes Care Due to Speaker Issue
headline: Tandem Diabetes Care Recalls Select t:slim X2 Insulin Pumps for Potential Speaker-Related Malfunction
description: Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. is recalling select t:slim X2 insulin pumps due to a speaker issue that may cause insulin delivery to stop. No deaths have been reported.
reason: Potential speaker-related issue that can trigger an error resulting in a discontinuation of insulin delivery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. has initiated a voluntary medical device correction for certain t:slim X2 insulin pumps due to a potential speaker-related issue. This defect can trigger a Malfunction 16 alarm, resulting in the discontinuation of insulin delivery and loss of communication between the pump and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) device. The interruption in insulin delivery and CGM data may lead to hyperglycemia, which in severe cases might require hospitalization or medical intervention.

As of the announcement, there have been 700 confirmed adverse events involving high blood sugar or the need for medical intervention, along with 59 reported injuries. No deaths have been reported. Customers in the United States affected by this issue were notified directly between July 22 and 24, 2025, with guidance on how to respond if they encounter the Malfunction 16 alarm.

Tandem Diabetes Care plans to release a software update aimed at improving early detection of speaker failure. This update will also add persistent vibration alerts to enhance safety. All pump users will be informed when the update is available and encouraged to install it promptly.

Impacted U.S. customers with questions can contact Tandem's Technical Support Team 24/7 by email at techsupport@tandemdiabetes.com or phone at (877) 801-6901. International users should contact their local distributors; contact details are available at tandemdiabetes.com. Additional resources, including a searchable list of affected serial numbers and the customer notification letter, are available online.

ResourceLink
Customer Notification LetterPDF document
Searchable List of Impacted Serial Numbershttps://tandemdiabetes.com/mal16-2025
Tandem Diabetes Contact Informationhttps://tandemdiabetes.com

Important safety information for current users includes:

  • The Control-IQ+ system should not be used in children under 2 years of age with Type 1 diabetes or under 18 years of age with Type 2 diabetes.
  • It is not intended for patients requiring less than 5 units of insulin per day or weighing less than 20 pounds (9 kg).
  • Users must follow all instructions for use to avoid risks of insulin overdelivery or underdelivery, which could lead to hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia.
  • Additional safety information is available at tandemdiabetes.com/safetyinfo.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers using a t:slim X2 insulin pump should check their device for related notifications and contact Tandem Diabetes Care for support or further instructions, as directed in the official recall notice.

content.text:

title: Max Mobility/Permobil Expands Recall of Speed Control Dial for SmartDrive MX2+ Due to Safety Risks
headline: Max Mobility/Permobil Recalls All Speed Control Dial Components for SmartDrive MX2+ Over Performance and Safety Concerns
description: Max Mobility/Permobil has expanded a worldwide recall of Speed Control Dial components for SmartDrive MX2+ due to potential safety and performance issues, per FDA notice.
reason: Due to potential safety and performance concerns

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Max Mobility, LLC., under the Max Mobility/Permobil brand, has issued a worldwide expanded recall and removal of all SpeedControl Dial components used with the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. The recall addresses potential safety and performance concerns related to intermittent electrical connections between the SpeedControl Dial and the SmartDrive MX2+ motor.

The affected SpeedControl Dials were manufactured and distributed from April 25, 2022, to July 8, 2025. The FDA notice states that users have reported incidents where the device continued to drive when the dial was pressed or activated the motor unintentionally, which may result in involuntary movement or failure to stop. These issues pose risks of minor to serious injuries, including cuts, bruises, bone fractures, and concussions.

This expanded recall supersedes previous recalls related to a printed circuit board assembly issue affecting units manufactured and distributed between August 17, 2023, and March 10, 2025. The FDA reports that Max Mobility/Permobil has received 781 complaints, including seven involving serious injuries, since the SpeedControl Dial was introduced.

The SpeedControl Dial will no longer be distributed or offered as a controller option for the SmartDrive MX2+. Alternative control options, such as the wired SwitchControl and app-based controls, remain available. Users are encouraged to consult their SmartDrive provider or healthcare professional for alternative solutions.

Part NumberDescriptionManufacture and Distribution Dates
MX2-3DCKSpeedControl Dial ComponentApril 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025
MX2-3DCSpeedControl Dial ComponentApril 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025
MX2-3DCMCSpeedControl Dial ComponentApril 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025

Distribution of the SpeedControl Dial has been suspended. The SmartDrive MX2+ device itself is not affected when used with other control methods.

For further details, see the official FDA recall notice: FDA Recall Notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the Speed Control Dial component for the SmartDrive MX2+ and switch to an alternative control method as advised in the FDA recall. Check product part numbers and dates, and contact Max Mobility/Permobil for support or replacement options as outlined in the official recall notice.

title: Clover Valley Instant Coffee Recalled by Dollar General for Potential Glass Contamination
headline: Dollar General Recalls Clover Valley Instant Coffee Over Potential Glass Fragments
description: Dollar General has recalled select lots of Clover Valley Instant Coffee due to possible glass contamination. No injuries or illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to an official FDA notice, Dollar General Corporation has voluntarily recalled three lots of Clover Valley® Instant Coffee due to the potential presence of glass fragments. The recall affects 8-ounce jars sold exclusively at Dollar General stores across multiple U.S. states between July 9 and July 21, 2025.

Ingesting glass can cause serious injuries, including lacerations or other harm. The FDA states that, as of the recall date, no injuries or adverse events have been reported in connection with this product.

Product DescriptionUPCLot NumberBest By Date
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee876941004069L-516312/13/2026
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee876941004069L-516412/13/2026
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee876941004069L-516512/14/2026

The recalled product was distributed in the following states: Alabama, Arkansas, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Mississippi, Montana, North Carolina, North Dakota, Nebraska, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, Nevada, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Vermont, Washington, Wisconsin, West Virginia, and Wyoming.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). For further details, refer to the official FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Clover Valley Instant Coffee matches the recalled lot numbers and, if so, stop using it immediately. Contact Dollar General at 1-888-309-9030 or customercare@dollargeneral.com for guidance on next steps.

title: DermaRite Recalls OTC Antiseptic Soaps and Cleansers for Burkholderia cepacia Contamination
headline: Nationwide Recall: DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene OTC Soaps Due to Bacterial Contamination
description: DermaRite Industries recalled select OTC antiseptic soaps and cleansers due to Burkholderia cepacia contamination. No adverse events have been reported.
productDescription: OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
DermaRite Industries, LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of specific lots of OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers. The recall includes products under the DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene brands. According to the FDA notice, the recall was initiated after detection of Burkholderia cepacia contamination, a bacterium that can cause serious infections, particularly in immunocompromised individuals.

The FDA states that Burkholderia cepacia complex poses a risk of potentially life-threatening infections, especially for those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals with minor skin lesions may develop local infections, while immunosuppressed users are at higher risk for bloodstream infections and sepsis. As of the FDA announcement, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported.

The affected products were distributed in the United States, including Puerto Rico. Distributors and customers have been notified to inspect and destroy affected inventory as directed by DermaRite. The FDA encourages reporting of any adverse reactions or quality issues through the MedWatch program.

title: Hans Kissle Recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad Recalled Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Hans Kissle recalled select Red Bliss Potato Salad sold at Stop and Shop stores due to undeclared wheat. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hans Kissle LLC has voluntarily recalled 66 units of its Red Potato Bliss Salad because wheat, a known allergen, was not declared on the product's top lid label. This poses a risk for individuals with wheat allergies or sensitivities, as consuming the product could result in serious or life-threatening reactions.

The recalled product was distributed to Stop and Shop stores in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. The affected salad is packaged in a 16-ounce clear plastic container with a white lid. The top lid label incorrectly identifies the product as Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad, while the container actually holds Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad, which contains wheat not listed on the top label.

The front label correctly states Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad. The recall was initiated after 66 containers were found with the incorrect top label. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date.

ProductContainer SizeUPC CodeUse By DateDistribution Locations
Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad (mislabeled top lid on Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad)16 oz (clear plastic container with white lid)0362176737068/20/25Stop and Shop stores in MA, RI, CT, NJ, NY

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad with the specified UPC and use by date should not consume it if they have a wheat allergy. Check the FDA recall page for details and contact Hans Kissle for further assistance.

title: Member's Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack Recalled for Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC Issues Recall of Member's Mark Freeze Dried Fruit for Listeria Risk
description: Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC recalled Member's Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On July 31, 2025, Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC announced a recall of Member's Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack (15 count) due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the FDA. Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria are at risk for miscarriages or stillbirths.

The recall includes multiple lots of the Member's Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack, identified by specific lot numbers and expiration dates. The affected products were distributed to Sam's Club stores in numerous states between July 1 and July 25, 2025. Packaging is in foil pouches inside corrugated boxes, with lot numbers and expiration dates printed on the bottom of the case.

No illnesses or adverse events related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

title: High Noon Recalls Beach Variety Packs and Celsius Energy Drinks Due to Alcohol in Cans
headline: High Noon and Celsius Energy Drink Recall: Vodka Seltzer Found in Select Energy Drink Cans
description: High Noon recalls certain Beach Variety Packs and Celsius Astro Vibe Energy Drinks after some cans were found to contain alcohol. No illnesses reported per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, High Noon has recalled specific High Noon Beach Variety packs (12-pack, 12 fluid ounce cans) due to a packaging error resulting in some CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink cans being filled with vodka seltzer instead of energy drink. The affected CELSIUS cans have silver lids, differing from the usual black lids. No illnesses or adverse events have been reported as of the recall announcement. The recall covers certain production lots distributed to retailers in Florida, Michigan, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Virginia, and Wisconsin between July 21 and July 23, 2025.

Consumers are advised to check the lot codes and lid color of their CELSIUS ASTRO VIBE Energy Drink cans. Only cans with silver lids and the specified lot codes are affected. This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and the Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their High Noon Beach Variety Packs and Celsius Astro Vibe Energy Drink cans for the specified lot codes and silver lids. If affected, do not consume the product and contact High Noon for further guidance.

title: Hillside Orchard Farms Fruit Breads & Fritters Recalled for Undeclared Egg Allergen
headline: Recall: Hillside Orchard Farms Fruit Breads & Fritters for Undeclared Egg
description: Hillside Orchard Farms recalled select Fruit Breads and Fritters due to undeclared egg, posing a risk to those with egg allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Hillside Orchard Farms has recalled several varieties of their 23 oz Fruit Breads and 7.5 oz Fritters due to undeclared egg. The recall applies to products with labels missing egg as an ingredient after a label reprint. Distribution occurred between November 18, 2024, and July 16, 2025.

Individuals with egg allergies or sensitivities may experience serious or life-threatening reactions if these products are consumed. As of the recall notice, no illnesses have been reported.

The affected products were sold mainly at farm markets and roadside stands in Alabama, Georgia, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and South Carolina. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Albertsons Expands Recall of Tuna Salad Products for Listeria Risk in AR, LA, OK, TX
headline: Randalls, Albertsons, Tom Thumb Tuna Salad Products Recalled Due to Listeria monocytogenes Concern
description: Albertsons has expanded its recall of select tuna salad products sold at Randalls, Albertsons, and Tom Thumb stores in four states due to potential Listeria contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Albertsons Companies has expanded a voluntary recall of select tuna salad products containing ingredients from Reser's Fine Foods. The affected products were sold at Albertsons, Randalls, and Tom Thumb stores in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas. This action follows a supplier recall of breadcrumbs used in the tuna salad, which may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth if infected.

As of the FDA recall announcement, there have been no reports of illnesses or injuries associated with these products.

title: Tropicale Foods Recalls La Michoacana and Helados Mexico Ice Cream for Undeclared Milk
headline: Recall: La Michoacana and Helados Mexico Ice Cream Products Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Tropicale Foods recalled select La Michoacana and Helados Mexico ice cream products nationwide for undeclared milk. One illness has been reported, per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published July 26, 2025, Tropicale Foods of Ontario, California, is recalling certain ice cream products under the Helados Mexico and La Michoacana brands due to undeclared milk. The recall affects products with specific best by dates, as the ingredient list includes "cream" but does not explicitly state "milk," which may pose a risk to people with milk allergies or sensitivities.

Individuals with a milk allergy who consume these products may experience serious or life-threatening allergic reactions. The recalled items were distributed nationwide through retail stores. The recall was initiated following a company audit of product labels. The FDA reports one illness associated with this issue.

BrandProduct DescriptionBest By DateUPC
Helados MexicoPaletas Ice Cream Bars, assorted flavors04/20/2024123456789012
La MichoacanaIce Cream Cups, various flavors04/25/2024987654321098

Consumers should verify product details on packaging. For adverse reactions, reports can be submitted to the FDA's MedWatch program via the FDA MedWatch website, by phone at 1-800-332-1088, or by mail using the online MedWatch form.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the labels of La Michoacana and Helados Mexico ice cream products for affected best by dates and avoid consuming them if they have a milk allergy. Contact Tropicale Foods with questions or concerns as directed in the official FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Communications Department | Communications@tropicalefoods.com

title: Albertsons and Randalls Tuna Salad Products Recalled for Possible Listeria Contamination in Four States
headline: Albertsons and Randalls Recall Tuna Salad Products Due to Potential Listeria Risk in AR, LA, OK, TX
description: Albertsons and Randalls have recalled select tuna salad products in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies stores, including Randalls and Tom Thumb locations in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas, are voluntarily recalling select tuna salad products supplied by Reser's Fine Foods. The recall is due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes found in the breadcrumbs used as an ingredient in the tuna salad.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth due to infection.

To date, there have been no reported illnesses or injuries associated with these products, according to the FDA and company notice.

title: Jewel Osco Recalls Tuna Salad Products in IL, IN, IA for Listeria Risk
headline: Jewel Osco Tuna Salad Products Recalled in Illinois, Indiana, and Iowa Due to Listeria Monocytogenes
description: Jewel Osco has recalled select tuna salad products in Illinois, Indiana, and Iowa due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jewel Osco stores in Illinois, Indiana, and Iowa have voluntarily recalled select tuna salad products supplied by Reser's Fine Foods. The recall follows an ingredient recall by Reser's Fine Foods due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination in breadcrumbs used in the tuna salad.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage or stillbirth.

According to the FDA recall notice, no injuries or adverse reactions related to these products have been reported.

title: Nirwana Foods Recalls Golden Raisins for Undeclared Sulfites Risk
headline: Golden Raisins by Nirwana Foods Recalled Due to Undeclared Sulfites
description: Nirwana Foods LLC recalled Golden Raisins 28oz pouches after undeclared sulfites were found. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nirwana Foods LLC of New Jersey has recalled its Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouches due to undeclared sulfites, as announced in the official FDA notice. Sulfites were not listed on the product label, posing a risk to individuals with sulfite allergies. Exposure may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive persons.

The recall affects Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouches with lot number 24/351-2410 and expiration date October 31, 2025. These products were distributed at Maharaja Super Market in New York and Villager Farmers Market in New York and New Jersey. The product is packaged in clear plastic with UPC code 712321993144.

The issue was identified during routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets. According to the FDA, no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this product.

ProductLot NumberExpiration DateUPC Code
Golden Raisin 28Oz Pouch24/351-2410Oct 31, 2025712321993144

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have the affected Golden Raisin 28oz pouch and follow the recall instructions from Nirwana Foods LLC. If you have a sulfite allergy, do not consume the product.

title: BD Updates Recall of Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules Serviced with Recalled Bezel Kits
headline: BD Expands Recall for Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules Due to Recalled Bezel Kit Assemblies
description: Becton, Dickinson and Company updated its recall of Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules after discovering some units were serviced with previously recalled bezel kit assemblies.
reason: May have been serviced with previously recalled bezel kit assemblies

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Becton, Dickinson and Company (BD) has issued an updated voluntary global recall for certain Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules. The recall concerns pump modules that may have been serviced with bezel kit assemblies previously recalled in 2019, manufactured between April 2011 and June 2017. BD identified through customer complaints that some affected bezel kit assemblies were still in use for servicing, despite the earlier recall.

The recalled bezel kit assemblies use a specific plastic (FR-110 bezels) that can weaken over time, potentially causing the bezel bosses to separate or break. This may result in serious infusion issues, including free flow, over-infusion, under-infusion, or interruption of infusion. The recall has been associated with reports of serious injury and patient deaths.

All Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules are included in this recall, as any unit may have been serviced with affected parts. BD recommends customers dispose of any remaining affected bezel kit assemblies and review service records to determine if their pump modules were serviced with these parts. Visual inspection of the bezel is advised, and any pump found with an affected bezel should be removed from service. Customers should contact BD for further guidance.

Adverse reactions or events can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch Program online, by phone, mail, or fax. See the FDA notice for full details.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Alaris™ or BD Alaris™ Pump Module was serviced with a recalled bezel kit assembly by reviewing product details and service records. For support, visit the BD website or contact the company at 1-844-823-5433.

title: L'espoir and Drycapot Cookies Recalled for Undeclared Milk and Tree Nuts, FDA Alert
headline: L'espoir and Drycapot Cookies Recalled Due to Undeclared Milk and Tree Nuts
description: Jalux Americas, Inc. (dba J.sweets) recalled select L'espoir and Drycapot cookies for undeclared milk and tree nuts. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA.
reason: Undeclared milk and tree nuts (almonds and macadamia nuts)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jalux Americas, Inc. (dba J.sweets) has recalled specific L'espoir and Drycapot cookies due to undeclared allergens, including milk, almonds, and macadamia nuts. The recall affects 32 units of L'espoir cookies and 28 units of Drycapot cookies sold at J.sweets retail locations in California, Illinois, and Washington between late May and the end of June 2025. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported, according to the FDA notice.

The issue was identified during an inventory audit, which found that some products containing milk or tree nuts were distributed without proper allergen labeling. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

ProductPackagingLot CodeBest Before DateBarcodeUndeclared Allergen
L'espoir CookiesGold plastic bag, 5 cookies/bagL4FN09/26/20254 942737 200147Milk
Drycapot CookiesGold plastic bag, 5 cookies/bagD4FN09/26/20254 942737 210191Tree Nuts (Almonds and Macadamia Nuts)
  • Recall Date: July 14, 2025
  • Affected Sales Locations: J.sweets stores in Torrance, CA; San Jose, CA; Arlington Heights, IL; and Lynnwood, WA
  • Sales Period: L'espoir from May 26 to June 30, 2025; Drycapot from May 31 to June 30, 2025
  • No online sales of the recalled products

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased L'espoir or Drycapot cookies from J.sweets stores should check packaging for the affected lot codes and avoid eating them if allergic to milk or tree nuts. For more information, contact Jalux Americas, Inc. as listed in the official FDA notice.

title: Deep Sprouted Moong (Sprouted Mung Beans) Recalled for Salmonella Risk by CHETAK LLC GROUP
headline: CHETAK LLC GROUP Recalls Deep Sprouted Moong Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: CHETAK LLC GROUP has recalled Deep Sprouted Moong (sprouted mung beans) nationwide due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported as of July 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published July 16, 2025, CHETAK LLC GROUP is recalling frozen 16 oz. packages of "Deep Sprouted Moong" and "Deep Sprouted Mat (Moth)" due to potential Salmonella contamination. Salmonella can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe and lead to complications such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail stores under specific lot numbers. The recall was initiated after routine FDA testing detected Salmonella. As of the recall date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products. Production has been suspended while the FDA and the company investigate the source of the contamination.

Product NamePackage SizeReason for Recall
Deep Sprouted Mat (Moth)16 oz.Potential Salmonella contamination
Deep Sprouted Moong16 oz.Potential Salmonella contamination

The recall involves multiple company locations, including:

  • Chetak New York LLC, Edison, NJ
  • Chetak San Francisco LLC, Union City, CA
  • Chetak Chicago LLC, Streamwood, IL
  • Chetak Orlando LLC, Kissimmee, FL
  • Chetak Los Angeles LLC, Pico Rivera, CA
  • Zeenat Inc., Sugarland, TX

For more details, see the official FDA recall notice: FDA Recall Announcement.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Deep Sprouted Moong (sprouted mung beans) is included in the recall and avoid consuming the product if affected, as advised in the FDA notice.

content.text: Consumer support: 908-209-8878

title: KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA Recalls ARAL SILVER VOBLA Dry Salted Fish for Botulism Risk
headline: ARAL SILVER VOBLA Dry Salted Fish Recalled Due to Potential Clostridium botulinum Contamination
description: KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA recalled ARAL SILVER VOBLA dry salted fish after inspections found improper evisceration, posing a botulism risk. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA, located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for its "ARAL SILVER VOBLA" brand product named "EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER." The recall was initiated after routine inspections by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets found the fish were not properly eviscerated before processing.

The affected product consists of two whole fish, vacuum packaged in clear plastic bags with a blue label, marked as "Product of Kazakhstan." This uneviscerated condition increases the risk of Clostridium botulinum contamination, as spores tend to concentrate in the fish's viscera, which can lead to serious foodborne illness including botulism.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning include dizziness, blurred or double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, muscle weakness, and breathing difficulties. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product should seek immediate medical attention. No illnesses have been reported so far in connection with this product.

title: Sandoz Recalls Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm Vials Due to Mislabeled Penicillin G Potassium Packaging
headline: Sandoz Expands Recall of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm Vials After Mislabeled Penicillin G Potassium Vials Found
description: Sandoz recalled certain Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vials after some were incorrectly labeled as Penicillin G Potassium. No injuries or adverse events reported.
productDescription: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
reason: Vials incorrectly labelled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection contain Cefazolin for Injection

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Sandoz, Inc. has expanded its voluntary nationwide recall to include one additional lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial. This follows a report of four vials labeled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, 20 million Units found in cartons of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial. Testing confirmed these vials contained Cefazolin for Injection.

The FDA states that mislabeling may result in serious health risks, including incorrect dosing, treatment failure, or allergic reactions. No injuries or adverse events have been reported as of the recall notice, though one complaint involved the incorrect product being administered to a patient.

Cefazolin for Injection is used to treat bacterial infections and for surgical infection prevention, while Penicillin G Potassium is indicated for severe infections such as septicemia and meningitis. Accurate labeling is critical due to differences in indications and dosing.

Cefazolin for Injection, USP (25 by 1g vials)0781-3451-700781-3451-96PG43602027-NOVSandoz GmbHSandoz Inc
Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP0781-6136-94N/APG43602027-NOVSandoz GmbHSandoz Inc
Cefazolin for Injection, USP (25 by 1g vials)0781-3451-700781-3451-96PG43622027-NOVSandoz GmbHSandoz Inc
Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP0781-6136-94N/APG43622027-NOVSandoz GmbHSandoz Inc

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No adverse events have been reported to date. For full details, refer to the official FDA notice.

title: Nostrum Laboratories Recalls Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 Gram Due to Company Closure
headline: Nationwide Recall of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 Gram by Nostrum Laboratories After Bankruptcy
description: Nostrum Laboratories is recalling all lots of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram within expiry due to company closure and halted quality activities. No adverse events reported.
reason: Company closure and discontinuation of quality activities.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nostrum Laboratories, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram currently within expiry. According to the official FDA notice, this recall follows the company's closure and discontinuation of quality control activities after its Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing on September 30, 2024. The recall affects Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram manufactured after June 2023.

The product was distributed to wholesalers, retailers, manufacturers, medical facilities, and repackagers throughout the United States. The company states it can no longer ensure these products meet required identity, strength, quality, and purity standards for their shelf life.

No injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported, per the FDA notice. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Adverse reactions or quality problems may be reported to the FDA's MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program.

ProductStrengthLot NumbersExpiration Dates
Sucralfate Tablets USP1 gramAll lots manufactured after June 2023All within expiry

For reporting adverse events to the FDA MedWatch program, consumers and healthcare professionals may use the following options:

  • Submit a report online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Download and complete a reporting form from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Request a reporting form by calling 1-800-332-1088, then mail it to the address on the form or fax it to 1-800-FDA-0178

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers in possession of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram from Nostrum Laboratories should check product details at https://nostrumlabsrecall.com/ and follow instructions provided on the recall website.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: recallcoordinator@nostrumlabsrecall.com

title: Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif Recalled by World Market for Salmonella Risk
headline: World Market Issues Recall of Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif Due to Salmonella
description: World Market recalled Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif after FDA testing found Salmonella. No illnesses have been reported as of July 2025.
keywords: Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif
productDescription: Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, World Market has recalled Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif, 9.7 oz, due to potential Salmonella contamination. The affected batch is marked with best before date April 01, 2027, and batch number 250401. FDA testing confirmed Salmonella in product samples.

Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Rarely, complications such as bloodstream infections may occur.

The recalled product was distributed in World Market stores in multiple states between June 11 and July 9, 2025. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

title: Hartford Bakery Recalls Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf for Undeclared Hazelnuts
headline: Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf Recalled for Undeclared Hazelnuts by Hartford Bakery
description: Hartford Bakery, Inc. recalled select Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf bread due to undeclared hazelnuts. FDA notice cites allergy risk and one reported digestive discomfort.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hartford Bakery, Inc. has recalled specific lots of its "Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf" bread after undeclared hazelnuts were found in the product, according to the FDA notice. This recall was initiated because the presence of hazelnuts poses a risk of serious allergic reactions for those with nut allergies. The recall covers twelve lot codes distributed in several Midwest and Southern states.

The affected bread is packaged in a 12-ounce (340 grams) flexible plastic bag with UPC 24126018152 and an expiration date of July 13, 2025. Although the label states "May Contain Tree Nuts," it does not specify hazelnuts. The issue was discovered after visible hazelnuts were found in approximately 883 loaves from six production lots, attributed to a packaging error.

The FDA notice states there have been no major reports of illness. One consumer reported digestive discomfort, and others noticed the nuts before consuming the product.

title: LLK TRADING INC. Recalls Needle Mushrooms for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: LLK TRADING INC. Issues Recall of Needle Mushrooms Due to Listeria Risk
description: LLK TRADING INC. recalled 200g Needle Mushrooms after FDA testing found possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, LLK TRADING INC. of Linden, New Jersey, has recalled its 200g packages of Needle Mushrooms due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illness in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Listeria infection may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. In pregnant women, infection may result in miscarriage or stillbirth. The recalled product was distributed to Bally Produce Corp. in Maspeth, NY, and subsequently sold to DATANG SUPERMARKET INC.

The affected Needle Mushrooms are packaged in 200 gram containers with a clear plastic top and blue, non-transparent bottom. The recall was initiated after routine FDA testing identified the contamination. As of the recall date, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with this product.

ProductPackage SizePackaging DescriptionDistribution Locations
Needle Mushrooms200 gramsClear plastic top, blue non-transparent bottomBally Produce Corp., 4900 Maspeth Ave, Maspeth, NY 11378;
DATANG SUPERMARKET INC.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers in possession of the recalled Needle Mushrooms should not eat the product. Check your packaging and return it to the place of purchase for a refund, as directed in the official FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: LLK Trading Inc. | (908)-290-3061
Media: Ashley LIU | | (908)-209-3061

title: Peeters and Aunt Mid's Sliced Mushrooms Recalled for Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Recall of Peeters and Aunt Mid's Fresh Sliced and Cremini Sliced Mushrooms Due to Listeria Risk
description: Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited has recalled select Peeters and Aunt Mid's sliced mushrooms due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
productDescription: Fresh sliced and Cremini sliced mushrooms

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited of Charing Cross, Ontario, Canada, has issued a recall for certain sliced mushroom products due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected products include Aunt Mid's Fresh Sliced Mushrooms, Peeters Mushroom Farm Cremini Sliced Mushrooms, and Peeters Mushroom Farm Thick Slice Mushrooms. These items were distributed in Michigan and Ohio.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause serious infections, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at increased risk as infection can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths.

The recall was initiated after testing by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency detected Listeria monocytogenes in 227g packages of Aunt Mid's Fresh Sliced Mushrooms. Other products produced on the same line are included as a precaution.

No illnesses or adverse events related to these products have been reported as of the recall notice.

Peeters Mushroom Farm Thick Slice Mushroom10 lb cardboard boxN/AN/A

title: Sheehan Brothers Vending Recalls Sandwiches and Subs for Undeclared Sesame Allergen
headline: Recall: Sheehan Brothers Vending Sandwiches and Subs Due to Undeclared Sesame
description: Sheehan Brothers Vending recalled select sandwiches and subs sold in Ohio, Northern Kentucky, and Eastern Indiana due to undeclared sesame. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and BBQ Riblet w/coleslaw

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sheehan Brothers Vending has issued a voluntary recall of several food products due to undeclared sesame. The affected items are Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney, and BBQ Riblet with coleslaw. The recall was initiated after a routine inspection by the Ohio Department of Agriculture found sesame was not listed on ingredient labels.

The recalled products were distributed in individually wrapped plastic packaging between July 2, 2025, and July 8, 2025, through Micro Markets and vending machine locations in Ohio, Northern Kentucky, and Eastern Indiana.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. Individuals with a sesame allergy or severe sensitivity may risk serious or life-threatening reactions if these products are consumed.

title: Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf Recalled for Undeclared Hazelnuts
headline: Hartford Bakery Recalls Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf Due to Undeclared Hazelnuts
description: Hartford Bakery, Inc. recalled select Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf bread after undeclared hazelnuts were found. No major injuries reported, per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hartford Bakery, Inc. has voluntarily recalled six lots of its "Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf" bread due to undeclared hazelnuts. The recall was initiated after approximately 883 loaves containing visible hazelnuts were mistakenly packaged in bags labeled as the Artisan Style ½ Loaf. The packaging does not indicate the presence of hazelnuts, posing a risk for individuals with nut allergies or sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed to retailers in Michigan, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Alabama, and Mississippi, including stores such as Kroger and Walmart. The issue was caused by a packaging error that led to hazelnut-containing bread being placed in white bread packaging.

According to the official FDA notice, no major injuries or allergic reactions have been reported. One consumer reported digestive discomfort, and others noticed nuts in the product before consumption.

  • Phone: 1-812-425-4642
  • Hours: Monday through Friday, 8:00am to 3:00pm CST (excluding holidays)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf bread with the affected lot codes and UPC. If so, do not consume the product due to the risk of undeclared hazelnuts. For more information or assistance, contact Hartford Bakery, Inc. as listed in the FDA recall notice.

title: Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) Recalled for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Natureen International Inc. Issues Recall of Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Natureen International Inc. recalled Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) after potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported as of July 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Natureen International Inc. of Walnut, California, has recalled 244 bags of Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) due to possible Salmonella contamination. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can lead to more severe conditions if it spreads beyond the digestive tract.

The recalled product was distributed in Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, Ohio, and Wisconsin through retail stores and supermarkets supplied by the Wei-Chuan Chicago Branch. Production has been suspended while the FDA and the company investigate the source of the contamination. As of the recall notice date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

ProductLot NumberExpiration DatePackage SizeDistribution Areas
Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus SliceXJ172026-04-172.5 oz (bag)IL, IN, IA, KS, KY, MI, MN, MO, NE, OH, WI

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) matches the recalled lot XJ17 and avoid consuming it if affected. For further information, contact Natureen International Inc. as directed in the official FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information

title: Payback Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster Recalled for Elevated Copper
headline: CHS, Inc. Recalls Payback Champion Lamb Feed Due to Elevated Copper Levels
description: CHS, Inc. has recalled Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster after elevated copper levels were found, posing health risks to sheep.
productDescription: Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, CHS, Inc. is recalling approximately seven tons of Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster due to potentially elevated levels of copper, which can cause copper toxicity in sheep. Symptoms may include lethargy, anemia, teeth grinding, increased thirst, loss of appetite, pale or yellow mucous membranes, discolored urine, inability to stand, and death within one to two days after symptoms appear. Continued consumption may result in serious illness or death.

The recalled product was manufactured at the CHS facility in Great Falls, Montana, and distributed to dealers in Montana, North Dakota, and Wyoming after February 19, 2025. It was sold in 40 lb. Payback Champion feed bags. The affected lot number and label date are listed below as provided in the official recall notice.

Product NameLot NumberLabel Date
Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power BoosterM#13430002/14/25

The FDA notice reports that adverse events have been associated with this product.

facts.product: Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster
facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster, lot M#134300, label date 02/14/25. If so, stop using the product immediately. Refer to the FDA recall notice for further information.

title: Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Recalled for Undeclared Peanut Allergen, FDA 2025
headline: Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
description: Mondelez Global LLC recalled select Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in 2025 due to undeclared peanut allergen. No injuries or illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published July 8, 2025, Mondelēz Global LLC voluntarily recalled four carton sizes of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches distributed in the United States due to a labeling error. Some individually wrapped packs inside the cartons are labeled as Cheese variety, but actually contain Peanut Butter sandwiches, posing a risk to those with peanut allergies.

The recall includes 8-pack, 20-pack, and 40-pack cartons of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches, as well as the 20-pack RITZ Filled Cracker Sandwich Variety Pack. The outer cartons are labeled correctly, but the inner packs may be mislabeled. No other RITZ or Mondelēz Global LLC products are affected.

No reports of injury or illness have been received as of the recall date. The issue was traced to a supplier packaging error. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product DescriptionRetail UPCBest When Used By DatesPlant Code Location
RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches - 8 Count (8 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton)0 44000 88210 5Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025Top of package (AE)
RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches - 20 Count (20 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton)0 44000 07584 2Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025; Jan 2, 2026 – Jan 22, 2026Top of package (AE)
RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches – 40 Count (40 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton)0 44000 07819 5Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025; Jan 2, 2026 – Jan 22, 2026Top of package (AM)
RITZ Filled Cracker Sandwich 20-Count Variety Pack (10 Cheese & 10 Peanut Butter 1.38-oz. packs)0 44000 08095 2Nov 2, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025Top of package (RJ)

Some individually wrapped packages inside these cartons may be incorrectly labeled as Cheese variety and resemble the following product:

Product DescriptionRetail UPCBest When Used By DatesPlant Code Location
RITZ Cheese Cracker Sandwiches (1.38 oz. pack)0 44000 00211 4Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025; Jan 2, 2026 – Jan 22, 2026Side of package (AE)

Cartons containing only RITZ Cheese Cracker Sandwiches or with different dates and plant codes are not affected. No injuries or illnesses have been reported as of the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the packaging of Ritz Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches for affected lot codes and avoid consuming any recalled products. If you have a peanut allergy, do not eat the product. For questions, contact Mondelez Global LLC as directed in the official FDA recall notice.

title: Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Recall of Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. recalled select Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils for undeclared milk. No illnesses reported, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. of Warren, OH, has recalled Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils due to undeclared milk allergen. This may cause serious or life-threatening reactions in those with milk allergies. No illnesses have been reported to date.

The recalled product was distributed at Wegmans retail stores in Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington D.C. The product is packaged in an 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) plastic tub labeled Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils, UPC 0 77890 49787 6.

The recall was initiated after it was found that the packaging did not declare milk due to a temporary manufacturing process breakdown. The following lot codes and best by dates are affected:

Lot CodeBest By DateProduct DescriptionUPC
55021DEC 28, 2025Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g0 77890 49787 6
55031DEC 29, 2025Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g0 77890 49787 6
55491FEB 13, 2026Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g0 77890 49787 6
55501FEB 14, 2026Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g0 77890 49787 6
56061APR 11, 2026Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g0 77890 49787 6
56071APR 12, 2026Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g0 77890 49787 6

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA's June 30, 2025 notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot code and best by date on their Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils. If the product matches any of the recalled codes, discontinue use and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

title: Sandoz Recalls Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm Vial Due to Penicillin G Potassium Vial Mispackaging
headline: Sandoz Recalls One Lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm Vial for Potential Penicillin G Potassium Inclusion
description: Sandoz Inc. is recalling one lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial after Penicillin G Potassium vials were found in some cartons. No injuries reported.
productDescription: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
reason: Potential Presence of Penicillin G Potassium Injection Vial

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sandoz Inc. has initiated a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial due to product mispackaging. This recall affects a single lot in which four vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, were mistakenly included in cartons labeled as Cefazolin for Injection. Each carton contains 25 vials.

The presence of Penicillin G Potassium in place of Cefazolin poses serious health risks, including potential allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis, ineffective treatment of infections, antibiotic resistance, drug interactions, cardiac arrhythmias due to elevated potassium levels especially in patients with kidney impairment, and delayed recovery. Although Sandoz has received a complaint about the incorrect product being administered to a patient, no adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

Cefazolin for Injection is an antibacterial medication used to treat or prevent bacterial infections in various parts of the body, including pneumonia and pre/post-surgical infections. Penicillin G Potassium treats a range of serious infections such as septicemia, meningitis, anthrax, and others. Despite both being beta-lactam antibiotics, their approved uses, dosing, and target infections differ.

Product NameDosage FormLot NumberQuantity per CartonIssue
Cefazolin for Injection, USP1 gm vialOne (1) lot (specific lot number not provided)25 vials per cartonFour vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection included by mistake

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No injuries or adverse events have been reported as of the FDA notice.

en.title: Sandoz Recalls Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm Vial Due to Penicillin G Potassium Vial Mispackaging
en.headline: Sandoz Recalls One Lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm Vial for Potential Penicillin G Potassium Inclusion
en.description: Sandoz Inc. is recalling one lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial after Penicillin G Potassium vials were found in some cartons. No injuries reported.
en.productDescription: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
en.reason: Potential Presence of Penicillin G Potassium Injection Vial
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sandoz Inc. has initiated a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial due to product mispackaging. This recall affects a single lot in which four vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, were mistakenly included in cartons labeled as Cefazolin for Injection. Each carton contains 25 vials.

The presence of Penicillin G Potassium in place of Cefazolin poses serious health risks, including potential allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis, ineffective treatment of infections, antibiotic resistance, drug interactions, cardiac arrhythmias due to elevated potassium levels especially in patients with kidney impairment, and delayed recovery. Although Sandoz has received a complaint about the incorrect product being administered to a patient, no adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

Cefazolin for Injection is an antibacterial medication used to treat or prevent bacterial infections in various parts of the body, including pneumonia and pre/post-surgical infections. Penicillin G Potassium treats a range of serious infections such as septicemia, meningitis, anthrax, and others. Despite both being beta-lactam antibiotics, their approved uses, dosing, and target infections differ.

Product NameDosage FormLot NumberQuantity per CartonIssue
Cefazolin for Injection, USP1 gm vialOne (1) lot (specific lot number not provided)25 vials per cartonFour vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection included by mistake

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No injuries or adverse events have been reported as of the FDA notice.

title: Face Rock Creamery Recalls Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds for Listeria Risk
headline: Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds Recalled by Face Rock Creamery Due to Listeria Concerns
description: Face Rock Creamery has recalled specific lots of Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Face Rock Creamery of Bandon, Oregon, has issued a voluntary recall of two lots of its Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds (6oz cups) after routine product testing identified potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illness, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The recalled product was distributed at Trader Joe's stores in Northern California (including Monterey, Fresno, and areas north) and Northern Nevada (Carson City, Reno, Sparks). According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported to date.

Face Rock Creamery is working with the U.S. FDA and the Oregon Department of Agriculture to investigate the source of the contamination and ensure product safety.

title: Sabores A Tu Mesa Mousse Desserts Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Sabores Fit Bakery Recalls Mousse Desserts Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk
description: Sabores Fit Bakery recalled Sabores A Tu Mesa Mousse Desserts after FDA found undeclared milk allergen. No illnesses reported as of June 20, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on June 20, 2025, Sabores Fit Bakery, under the brand Sabores A Tu Mesa, has recalled its Mousse Desserts due to undeclared milk. The products may also contain eggs, soy (soybean oil, soy lecithin), wheat, and tree nuts (almonds, hazelnuts) not listed on the label, posing a risk for those with allergies or sensitivities.

The recalled mousse desserts were distributed in retail stores in the Kissimmee and Orlando areas of Florida. Packaged in 8 oz plastic cups, the affected flavors include Choco Mousse, Passion Fruit Mousse, 4 Milk Mousse, 3 Milk Mousse, and 3 Milk Strawberry, with expiration dates from June 26 to July 02, 2025.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to these products have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after a routine inspection identified the labeling issue. The company is working to improve labeling and food safety protocols.

Product NameFlavorPackagingExpiration Date
Mousse DessertsChoco Mousse8 oz plastic cupJune 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse DessertsPassion Fruit Mousse8 oz plastic cupJune 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts4 Milk Mousse8 oz plastic cupJune 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts3 Milk Mousse8 oz plastic cupJune 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts3 Milk Strawberry8 oz plastic cupJune 26 & July 02, 2025
  • Individuals with allergies or sensitivities to milk, eggs, soy, wheat, or tree nuts should not consume these products.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Sabores A Tu Mesa Mousse Desserts should check the packaging for affected flavors and expiration dates and avoid consumption if they have milk allergies or sensitivities.

title: SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Recall of SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils have been recalled due to undeclared milk, posing a risk to those with milk allergies. No illnesses reported as of June 20, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, International Foodsource, LLC of Randolph, NJ, is recalling 10 oz packages of SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils due to the presence of an undeclared milk allergen. This may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in individuals with a milk allergy.

The recalled product was distributed by C&S Wholesale Grocers (Southeastern Grocers) and sold at retail stores in Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana. The product is packaged in clear 10 oz bags labeled as SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils.

The recall was initiated after International Foodsource, LLC was notified on June 16, 2025, by their supplier, Weaver Nut Company, which voluntarily recalled its Nonpareil, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (Christmas Seeds) due to undeclared milk. The root cause and corrective actions are pending from the supplier.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. For more information, consumers may contact International Foodsource, LLC as listed in the official recall notice.

content.text: Consumers who purchased SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils should not eat the product if they have a milk allergy. title: JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen by Lipari Foods
headline: Lipari Foods Recalls JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Lipari Foods recalled JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils nationwide after undeclared milk was found. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lipari Foods of Warren, Michigan, has recalled its 14-ounce packages of JLM Branded "Dark Chocolate Nonpareils" because they contain undeclared milk. The presence of this allergen, which is not listed on the product packaging, poses a risk to individuals with milk allergies. The recall applies to products distributed nationwide through retail stores.

The affected items are packaged in clear plastic tubs and can be identified by specific lot numbers printed on the bottom label. The recall follows a related action by Weaver Nut Company. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported to date.

ProductPackage SizeLot Numbers
JLM Branded Dark Chocolate Nonpareils14-ounce clear plastic tub28202501A, 29202501A, 23202504A, 14202505A, 15202505A, 03202506A

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot numbers on their JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils and stop using the product if it is part of the recall. For questions or concerns, contact Lipari Foods as directed in the official FDA notice.

title: Weaver Nut Company Recalls Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Recall: Weaver Nut Company Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Weaver Nut Company recalled select lots of semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas seeds due to undeclared milk. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Semi sweet chocolate pareils with white or Christmas colored seeds

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Weaver Nut Company, Inc. has issued a recall for certain lots of its semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas colored seeds due to the presence of undeclared milk allergens. According to the official FDA notice, this poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recall was initiated after a wholesale customer complaint and laboratory testing confirmed the presence of milk in affected lots, which do not declare milk on their labels. The products were distributed nationwide through retail and grocery stores. As of the recall date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

en.description: Weaver Nut Company recalled select lots of semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas seeds due to undeclared milk. No illnesses reported.

en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Weaver Nut Company, Inc. has issued a recall for certain lots of its semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas colored seeds due to the presence of undeclared milk allergens. According to the official FDA notice, this poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recall was initiated after a wholesale customer complaint and laboratory testing confirmed the presence of milk in affected lots, which do not declare milk on their labels. The products were distributed nationwide through retail and grocery stores. As of the recall date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

title: Little Remedies Honey Cough Syrup Recalled Nationwide for Bacillus cereus Contamination Risk
headline: Medtech Products Inc. Recalls Little Remedies Honey Cough Syrup Due to Potential Bacillus cereus Contamination
description: Medtech Products Inc. recalled select lots of Little Remedies Honey Cough Syrup nationwide after potential Bacillus cereus contamination was identified. FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Medtech Products Inc. has announced a nationwide recall of specific lots of Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup due to potential contamination with Bacillus cereus, a bacterium that can cause foodborne illness. The affected syrup was distributed in the United States through retail and online channels from December 14, 2022, to June 4, 2025.

The recall applies to certain 4 FL OZ (118 mL) amber bottles. Lot codes are printed on both the bottle label and the bottom of the outer carton. No other Little Remedies® products are included in this recall. According to the FDA notice, no serious adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this issue.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Consumers may report adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA's MedWatch program.

ProductUPCLot NumberExpiration Date
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL)7-56184-10737-9003911/2025
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL)7-56184-10737-9054501/2026
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL)7-56184-10737-9064002/2026
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL)7-56184-10737-9045005/2026
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL)7-56184-10737-9119812/2026

For questions about the recall or reimbursement, consumers may contact Medtech Products Inc. by email at medicalaffairs@prestigebrands.com, through the website at https://www.prestigebrands.com/contact, or by phone at (800) 754-8853 (Monday – Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM ET).

Adverse event reports can be submitted to the FDA MedWatch program online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm, or by mail or fax using forms available at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using Little Remedies Honey Cough Syrup if their product matches the recalled lot numbers. Check the lot code on the bottle or carton and contact Medtech Products Inc. for questions or reimbursement if affected.

title: Frederik's by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds Recalled for Undeclared Cashews
headline: Recall: Frederik's by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds May Contain Undeclared Cashews
description: Meijer has recalled Frederik's by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds due to undeclared cashews, posing a risk for those with nut allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Meijer has issued a recall for certain packages of Frederik's by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds due to the presence of undeclared cashews. The affected products may contain dark chocolate-covered cashews, which are not listed on the label. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to cashews.

The recall applies to Frederik's Dark Chocolate Almonds sold in black stand-up pouches and 8-count, 1.5-ounce multi-pack boxes available at Meijer stores in Michigan, Indiana, Illinois, Ohio, Kentucky, and Wisconsin. The products have specific sell-by dates ranging from May 5, 2026, to May 28, 2026. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses have been reported to date.

UPCRecalled Product NameSell By Date(s)
7-08820-68730-1Frederik's by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds (12 oz.)05/07/2026, 05/28/2026
7-19283-11923-0Frederik's Dark Chocolate Almonds 8-count 1.5 oz.05/05/2026

For more information, visit the Meijer Newsroom or the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Frederik's by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds for the affected UPCs and sell-by dates. If included in the recall, do not consume the product. For questions or support, contact Meijer as directed in the official recall notice.

title: Fuentes Farms Inc Recalls Bushel Box Cucumbers for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Recall: Fuentes Farms Inc Cucumbers in Bushel Boxes Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Fuentes Farms Inc is recalling cucumbers in bushel boxes distributed in Texas due to potential Salmonella contamination, per FDA notice. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Fuentes Farms LLC of McAllen, Texas, has recalled 71 boxes of fresh cucumbers due to potential Salmonella contamination. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe.

The recalled cucumbers were distributed in Texas through local vendors at flea markets in the McAllen and Alamo areas. The affected product is labeled with Lot number 357 and packaged in 40-pound (1-1/9 bushel) boxes marked "vegetables" on a black background. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. The recall was initiated after a routine FDA sample detected Salmonella in the finished product. Production and distribution have been halted while the investigation continues.

ProductLot NumberPackage SizeDistribution AreaRecall Dates
Fresh Cucumbers with Fuentes Farms Label35740 lbs (1-1/9 bushel boxes)Texas (McAllen and Alamo flea markets)May 31 to June 3

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Fuentes Farms Inc cucumbers in bushel boxes with Lot #357 between May 31 and June 3 should not consume the product. Dispose of the cucumbers or return them to the place of purchase as directed in the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: 1-956-340-8653 | sales@fuentesfarmsllc.com

title: Vita Warehouse Corp. Recalls Welby, Berkely Jensen, and VitaGlobe Gummy Products for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: Recall: Undeclared Peanut Allergen in Welby, Berkely Jensen, and VitaGlobe Gummy Products by Vita Warehouse Corp.
description: Vita Warehouse Corp. recalled select Welby, Berkely Jensen, and VitaGlobe gummy products due to undeclared peanut allergen. No illnesses reported per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On June 13, 2025, Vita Warehouse Corp. announced a voluntary recall of certain Vitamin B12 gummy products under the Welby®, Berkely Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ brands. The recall was initiated after routine testing indicated the possible presence of undeclared peanut allergen, which can pose a serious risk to individuals with peanut allergies. The affected products were distributed nationwide through ALDI and BJ's retail stores and online channels.

The recall involves products with lot number 248046601 and expiration date October 2026. According to the FDA notice, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported to date. The issue was identified during routine testing by ALDI, and Vita Warehouse Corp. is recalling the products out of an abundance of caution. Internal testing by the company did not detect peanuts, but the recall is being conducted to protect consumers.

BrandPackaging DescriptionUPC CodeLot NumberExpiration DateNotes
Welby®Clear bottle with white cap, green, red, and white labeling409910029086824804660110/20261000 mcg, 140 gummies
Berkley Jensen®Clear bottle with white cap, red and pink labeling88867013248724804660110/20261000 mcg, 250 gummies
VitaGlobe™Clear bottle with white cap, white and red labeling85000521467024804660110/2026Extra Strength, 60 gummies

No other lots or products from these brands are affected. This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who are allergic to peanuts should check their Welby, Berkely Jensen, and VitaGlobe gummy products for lot number 248046601 and expiration date 10/2026. If affected, stop use and return the product to the place of purchase or discard it as directed in the FDA notice.

title: Floria Dried Apricots Recalled by Turkana Food Inc. for Undeclared Sulfites
headline: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Floria Dried Apricots Due to Undeclared Sulfites
description: Turkana Food Inc. recalled Floria Dried Apricots after FDA found undeclared sulfites, posing a risk to those with sulfite allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Turkana Food Inc., based in Kenilworth, NJ, is recalling 352 cases of Floria Dried Apricots due to undeclared sulfites on the product label. The presence of sulfites, a potential allergen, was not disclosed on the packaging, presenting a health risk to sulfite-sensitive consumers.

The affected Floria Dried Apricots were distributed across multiple states including Florida, Kentucky, Virginia, New York, New Jersey, Tennessee, Massachusetts, Texas, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Rhode Island, Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Maryland, Ohio, Alabama, Missouri, and California. The recall is limited to the 200-gram paper packaging with specific lot and expiration details as noted below.

This action follows a routine sampling by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets that detected sulfites in the finished product which were not listed on the label. As of June 12, 2025, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue. Turkana Food Inc. has halted production and distribution while working with the FDA to resolve the labeling problem.

ProductLot NumberUPCExpiration Date
Floria Dried Apricots, 200 Gram Paper Packaging440090478-15-333253956001011/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
