en.title: Floria Dried Apricots Recalled by Turkana Food Inc. for Undeclared Sulfites
en.headline: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Floria Dried Apricots Due to Undeclared Sulfites
en.description: Turkana Food Inc. recalled Floria Dried Apricots after FDA found undeclared sulfites, posing a risk to those with sulfite allergies. No illnesses reported.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Allergens, Food & Beverages, Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Turkana Food Inc., based in Kenilworth, NJ, is recalling 352 cases of Floria Dried Apricots due to undeclared sulfites on the product label. The presence of sulfites, a potential allergen, was not disclosed on the packaging, presenting a health risk to sulfite-sensitive consumers.

The affected Floria Dried Apricots were distributed across multiple states including Florida, Kentucky, Virginia, New York, New Jersey, Tennessee, Massachusetts, Texas, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Rhode Island, Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Maryland, Ohio, Alabama, Missouri, and California. The recall is limited to the 200-gram paper packaging with specific lot and expiration details as noted below.

This action follows a routine sampling by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets that detected sulfites in the finished product which were not listed on the label. As of June 12, 2025, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue. Turkana Food Inc. has halted production and distribution while working with the FDA to resolve the labeling problem.

ProductLot NumberUPCExpiration Date
Floria Dried Apricots, 200 Gram Paper Packaging440090478-15-333253956001011/2026

content.text: Consumers should check their Floria Dried Apricots for lot code 440090478-15-333 and, if affected, return the product to the place of purchase for a refund.

eeatMeta.expertSummary: Turkana Food Inc. has recalled dried apricots due to the presence of undeclared sulfites, a potential allergen. This undeclared allergen poses health risks to individuals with sulfite allergies or sensitivities.
eeatMeta.riskExplanation: Sulfite allergens can cause adverse reactions in sensitive individuals, including respiratory difficulties and allergic responses, making undeclared sulfites a serious health concern.
eeatMeta.realWorldContext: Consumers with sulfite allergies rely on accurate labeling to avoid exposure. Undeclared allergens in food products can lead to unexpected allergic reactions and possibly emergency medical situations.

title: Medtronic Recalls Select Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus Ventilators and Service Parts for Safety Risk
headline: Medtronic Recalls Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus Ventilators Due to Potential Device Failure
description: Medtronic has recalled certain Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and service parts after reports of device failures linked to serious injuries and one death.
productDescription: Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA notice, Medtronic has voluntarily recalled specific Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and related service parts due to a potential defect involving two capacitors on the controller Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA). This defect may cause the ventilator to shut down unexpectedly or result in the shutdown alert alarm failing, which can lead to inadequate ventilation.

The FDA classified this as a Class I recall, the most serious type, as use of the affected devices may result in reduced oxygen levels, increased carbon dioxide levels, brain injury, or death. Since May 2025, Medtronic has received 63 medical device reports, including two serious injuries and one death related to this issue.

The affected ventilators are used in various settings, including hospitals and home care, and are especially important for infant and pediatric patients. Medtronic has advised discontinuing clinical use of the affected devices and replacing them with alternative ventilation methods. The company will no longer service these ventilators or related parts.

Product NameManufacture DateUse By Date
Newport™ HT70 VentilatorSpecific dates not provided in sourceNot specified
Newport™ HT70 Plus VentilatorSpecific dates not provided in sourceNot specified
Certain Newport™ Service PartsPost-May 2025 manufacture dates assumedNot specified

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers using Newport™ HT70 or HT70 Plus ventilators or related service parts should review their devices for inclusion in this FDA recall and follow Medtronic's guidance for next steps.

title: Uneviscerated Salted Smoked Split Herring Recalled by P. East Trading Corp Distributors for Potential Clostridium Botulinum Risk
headline: P. East Trading Corp Distributors Recalls Uneviscerated Salted Smoked Split Herring Due to Botulism Hazard
description: P. East Trading Corp Distributors recalled No Brand Uneviscerated Salted Smoked Split Herring due to potential Clostridium botulinum contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
P. East Trading Corp. Distributors of Bronx, NY, has recalled its No Brand Uneviscerated Salted Smoked Split Herring due to the risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum, which can cause serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness. The product, over 5 inches in length and uneviscerated, increases the risk of botulinum spores remaining in the fish's viscera. Consumers are advised not to eat the product even if it appears normal.

Clostridium botulinum can cause botulism, a severe illness with symptoms such as weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product should seek immediate medical attention.

The recalled herring was distributed to retail stores in New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut. It was packaged in 18-pound wooden boxes with Lot 1 PRC5073, produced by Sea Star Seafood Ltd. in Canada. Retailers may have repackaged the product into different packaging with varying codes.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall, according to the FDA notice.

title: Bornstein Seafoods Recalls Coldwater Shrimp Meat for Listeria Risk
headline: Coldwater Shrimp Meat by Bornstein Seafoods Recalled Due to Listeria monocytogenes
description: Bornstein Seafoods Inc. is recalling Coldwater Shrimp Meat after Listeria monocytogenes was detected in routine sampling. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bornstein Seafoods Inc., based in Bellingham, Washington, is recalling approximately 44,550 pounds of Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The recalled shrimp products were distributed to distributors and retailers in California, Oregon, Washington, and British Columbia, Canada, with possible further distribution nationwide. The products are sold under the Bornstein Seafoods brand in 1-pound and 5-pound plastic bags.

No illnesses have been reported to date. Symptoms of Listeria monocytogenes infection may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at particular risk, as infection may cause miscarriages and stillbirths.

This recall was initiated after routine sampling detected Listeria monocytogenes in a shrimp sample during production. Distribution has been halted while the company investigates the cause. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Coldwater Shrimp Meat products for the affected lot numbers listed in the recall. Do not consume recalled products; follow any safety instructions from Bornstein Seafoods and contact the company if you have questions.

title: August Egg Company Recalls Brown Cage Free and Organic Eggs for Salmonella Risk
headline: Recall of Brown Cage Free and Organic Eggs by August Egg Company Due to Salmonella
description: August Egg Company has recalled brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs sold under multiple brands due to possible Salmonella contamination, per FDA.
productDescription: Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, August Egg Company of Hilmar, California, has issued a voluntary recall of approximately 1,700,000 dozen brown cage-free and brown certified organic eggs due to potential contamination with Salmonella enteritidis. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can lead to more severe conditions such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recalled eggs were distributed between February 3, 2025, and May 15, 2025, with sell-by dates from March 4, 2025, to June 4, 2025, in California and Nevada. Additional shipments were made to Walmart stores in California, Washington, Nevada, Arizona, Wyoming, New Mexico, Nebraska, Indiana, and Illinois between February 3, 2025, and May 6, 2025, with sell-by dates from March 4, 2025, through June 19, 2025.

Egg cartons included in the recall have plant codes P-6562 or CA5330 and Julian Dates between 32 and 126 printed on one side of the carton. The cartons may be fiber or plastic. August Egg Company has stopped selling fresh shell eggs and is diverting eggs to an egg-breaking plant for pasteurization.

Distribution DatesSell-By DatesLocationsPlant CodesJulian Dates
Feb 3, 2025 – May 15, 2025Mar 4, 2025 – Jun 4, 2025California, Nevada (Save Mart, FoodMaxx, Lucky, Smart & Final, Safeway, Raleys, Food 4 Less, Ralphs)P-6562, CA533032 to 126
Feb 3, 2025 – May 6, 2025Mar 4, 2025 – Jun 19, 2025CA, WA, NV, AZ, WY, NM, NE, IN, IL (Walmart locations)P-6562, CA533032 to 126

The FDA notice references injuries or adverse events related to this recall. Consumers are urged to review the official recall notice for further details.

facts.product: Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their egg cartons for plant codes P-6562 or CA5330 and Julian Dates between 32 and 126. If your eggs are affected, do not consume them and contact August Egg Company for further support.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Customer Service | 800-710-2554

title: Zicam® and Orajel™ Swabs Recalled Nationwide for Microbial Contamination Risk
headline: Church & Dwight Recalls Zicam® Cold Remedy, Nasal AllClear, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs Due to Fungal Contamination
description: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. recalled Zicam® and Orajel™ swab products nationwide due to potential fungal contamination. No serious adverse events reported.
productDescription: Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Church & Dwight Co., Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of select lots of Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs due to potential microbial contamination. The contamination involves fungi detected in the cotton swab components, which may pose health risks, especially for individuals with weakened immune systems, underlying conditions, or damaged nasal mucosa. According to the official FDA notice, no serious adverse events have been reported to date, but use of contaminated products could result in serious or life-threatening infections.

The recalled products were distributed throughout the United States and Puerto Rico. All lots within expiry are included in the recall. Other Zicam and Orajel products, such as Zicam RapidMelts, are not affected.

Product NameUPCLot NumbersAdditional Details
Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs732216301205All lots within expiryZinc-free, homeopathic cold remedy swab
Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs732216301656All lots within expiryNasal cleansing swab (discontinued December 2024)
Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs310310400002All lots within expiryPre-moistened swabs for teething discomfort

Consumers who experience adverse effects after using these products can report incidents to the FDA MedWatch program as outlined in the official recall notice.

facts.product: Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Zicam® or Orajel™ swab products listed in the recall and visit www.churchdwightrecall.com for details on returns or replacements.

title: TGD Cuts, LLC Recalls Fresh Cucumbers and Prepared Foods for Potential Salmonella Risk
headline: Fresh Cucumbers and Prepared Foods Recalled by TGD Cuts, LLC Due to Salmonella Concerns
description: TGD Cuts, LLC has recalled multiple brands of fresh cucumbers and prepared foods containing cucumbers due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Fresh cucumbers and salsa and salads containing fresh cucumbers
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On June 4, 2025, TGD Cuts, LLC, based in Jessup, Maryland, voluntarily recalled certain tubs and trays containing cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers Inc. due to the potential presence of Salmonella. Salmonella can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Rarely, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe complications.

The recall affects products with use-by dates from May 20, 2025, to May 28, 2025, distributed in Maryland, Virginia, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and North Carolina. According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported to date. Most affected products are past their shelf life and are not expected to be available in stores.

Product DescriptionUse By DateDistribution Area
Specific tubs and trays containing cucumber from Bedner Growers Inc.May 20, 2025 – May 28, 2025Maryland, Virginia, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, North Carolina

facts.product: Fresh cucumbers and salsa and salads containing fresh cucumbers

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for recalled fresh cucumbers and prepared foods with use-by dates from May 20 to May 28, 2025, and dispose of them if found. For questions, contact TGD Cuts, LLC as directed in the official recall notice.

title: Firehook Recalls Classic Sea Salt Crackers for Undeclared Sesame Allergen
headline: Firehook Classic Sea Salt Crackers Recalled Over Undeclared Sesame Risk
description: Firehook of Virginia recalled one lot of Classic Sea Salt Crackers due to undeclared sesame, posing an allergy risk. No illnesses reported per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Firehook of Virginia has recalled one lot of Firehook Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers due to undeclared sesame. Individuals with sesame allergies or sensitivities may face a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The affected crackers are packaged in 8-ounce clear packages, with a Best By Date of 09/29/25 and UPC code 8 99055 00063 5. Distribution included retail stores in Connecticut, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, North Carolina, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, and Virginia.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that sesame-containing crackers were mislabeled due to a temporary production and packaging issue. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

ProductPackage SizeBest By DateUPC CodeStates Sold
Firehook Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers8 oz09/29/258 99055 00063 5CT, MA, MD, ME, NC, NH, NJ, NY, PA, RI, VA

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the Best By Date and UPC on Firehook Classic Sea Salt Crackers and avoid eating recalled products. Return affected packages to the place of purchase or contact Firehook of Virginia for guidance.

content.text:
Consumer support: Firehook of Virginia | 888-580-0745
Media: Brent Fowler | | 888-580-0745

title: Amneal Recalls Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim 400 mg/80 mg Tablets Nationwide for Microbial Contamination
headline: Amneal Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim 400 mg/80 mg Tablets Recalled Nationwide Due to Microbial Contamination Risk
description: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC recalled select Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim 400 mg/80 mg tablets nationwide after microbial contamination was identified. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC has issued a nationwide recall of three lots of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg due to microbial contamination. Black spots were observed on the surface of some tablets, identified after a product quality complaint. The contamination involves Aspergillus, which may cause serious infections, particularly in immunocompromised individuals.

The recalled tablets were distributed in the United States between December 4, 2024, and May 15, 2025. Only the 400 mg/80 mg strength is affected. No adverse events or injuries related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA announcement.

This medication is used for treating urinary tract infections, acute otitis media in children, acute exacerbations of chronic bronchitis, enteritis caused by Shigella flexneri, and traveler's diarrhea in adults. The product is supplied in bottles of 100 and 500 tablets. Consumers are advised to check the lot number on their bottle or consult their pharmacy if unsure.

Lot NumberDistribution DatesProduct StrengthPackage Size
Specific lot numbers not listed in source12/4/2024 - 5/15/2025400 mg/80 mg100 and 500 tablets per bottle

Amneal is notifying customers and arranging for the return of affected products. Wholesalers, distributors, and retailers are instructed to inform their customers and facilitate returns. No illnesses or injuries have been reported to date, per the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot number on their Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim 400 mg/80 mg tablets packaging to determine if their product is included in the recall. If affected, follow the FDA notice instructions or contact Amneal for guidance.

en.description: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC recalled select Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim 400 mg/80 mg tablets nationwide after microbial contamination was identified. No injuries reported.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice, Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC has issued a nationwide recall of three lots of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg due to microbial contamination. Black spots were observed on the surface of some tablets, identified after a product quality complaint. The contamination involves Aspergillus, which may cause serious infections, particularly in immunocompromised individuals.

The recalled tablets were distributed in the United States between December 4, 2024, and May 15, 2025. Only the 400 mg/80 mg strength is affected. No adverse events or injuries related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA announcement.

This medication is used for treating urinary tract infections, acute otitis media in children, acute exacerbations of chronic bronchitis, enteritis caused by Shigella flexneri, and traveler's diarrhea in adults. The product is supplied in bottles of 100 and 500 tablets. Consumers are advised to check the lot number on their bottle or consult their pharmacy if unsure.

Lot NumberDistribution DatesProduct StrengthPackage Size
Specific lot numbers not listed in source12/4/2024 - 5/15/2025400 mg/80 mg100 and 500 tablets per bottle

Amneal is notifying customers and arranging for the return of affected products. Wholesalers, distributors, and retailers are instructed to inform their customers and facilitate returns. No illnesses or injuries have been reported to date, per the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot number on their Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim 400 mg/80 mg tablets packaging to determine if their product is included in the recall. If affected, follow the FDA notice instructions or contact Amneal for guidance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Amneal Drug Safety | 1-833-582-0812 | sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim-recall@amneal.com

See the company's consumer information on their website.

Consult your healthcare provider for an alternative treatment
Check the batch numbers and lot information to see if your medication is affected

eeatMeta.expertSummary: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC has issued a nationwide recall for Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets due to microbial contamination that may compromise product safety.
eeatMeta.riskExplanation: Microbial contamination in drug products can lead to serious infections or adverse health effects, especially in vulnerable patients, thus posing a significant health risk.
eeatMeta.realWorldContext: This recall highlights the importance of pharmaceutical quality control and the potential dangers when contamination occurs in commonly prescribed antibiotics.

title: Aldi Casa Mamita Churro Bites Recalled for Undeclared Milk, FDA Alert 2025
headline: Camerican International Recalls Aldi Casa Mamita Churro Bites for Undeclared Milk
description: Camerican International recalled select Aldi Casa Mamita Churro Bites due to undeclared milk. No illnesses reported. See FDA notice for details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Camerican International, Inc. of Paramus, NJ is recalling 7.05 oz boxes of Aldi brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream due to undeclared milk. People with milk allergies or severe sensitivities risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled product was distributed in select Aldi stores in Alabama, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Illinois, Kentucky, Louisiana, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee. The recall was initiated after a consumer reported that milk-containing Churro Bites were sold in packaging that did not declare milk as an ingredient. The company traced the issue to a temporary production and packaging process breakdown, which has been corrected.

No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date, per the FDA notice.

ProductPackage SizeBest If Used By DateLot NumberDistribution States
Aldi Brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream7.05 oz boxJUL/14/202501425AL, AR, FL, GA, IA, IL, KY, LA, MO, MS, NC, SC, TN

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with milk allergies should not eat the recalled Aldi Casa Mamita Churro Bites. Check your packaging for the affected lot and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

title: Shore Lunch Breading & Batter Mix Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Homegrown Family Foods Recalls Shore Lunch Breading & Batter Mix Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Homegrown Family Foods recalled Shore Lunch Breading & Batter Mix after undeclared milk was found. One allergic reaction reported; FDA notice issued May 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Homegrown Family Foods has recalled Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix 6-ounce boxes due to the presence of undeclared milk. This allergen can cause serious or life-threatening reactions in people with milk allergies or sensitivities.

The recalled product was distributed in retail stores across Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Minnesota, Nebraska, New York, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, and Wisconsin between April 29, 2024, and May 1, 2025. The affected products can be identified by specific Best By dates, lot numbers, and UPC codes.

The FDA notice states that one allergic reaction has been reported in connection with this recall, and the individual has recovered. The recall was initiated after a consumer reported an allergic reaction on April 23, 2025, leading to the discovery of milk in product not labeled for milk allergens.

ProductNet WeightBest By DatesLot NumbersUPC CodeDistribution States
Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix6 oz (170 g)April 23, 2025 – February 25, 2026RP117050, RP120012, RP120011, RP120013, RP123249, RP123389, RP129004, RP129005, RP1290062473912000IL, IN, IA, MN, NE, NY, ND, OH, SD, WI
  • Best By date and Lot Code are located on the top of the box.
  • UPC code is found on the bottom of the box.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the Best By date, lot code, and UPC on their Shore Lunch Breading & Batter Mix. If your product matches the recalled information and you have a milk allergy or sensitivity, do not consume it.

title: Isabelle's Kitchen, Inc. Recalls Deli Salads with Fresh Cucumbers for Salmonella Risk
headline: Deli Salads with Fresh Cucumbers Recalled by Isabelle's Kitchen, Inc. Due to Salmonella Concern
description: Isabelle's Kitchen, Inc. has recalled select deli salads containing fresh cucumbers due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
brandName: Kings, Isabelle's Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods
brandNames: Kings, Isabelle's Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Isabelle's Kitchen, Inc., based in Harleysville, PA, is recalling 946 cases of refrigerated deli salads containing fresh cucumbers supplied by Bedner Grower's, Inc. due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, including fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Vulnerable populations such as young children, elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems are at increased risk of severe or potentially fatal infections.

The recalled refrigerated deli salads were distributed to foodservice distributors and local supermarkets in New Hampshire, Florida, Maryland, South Carolina, New Jersey, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. These products are packaged in white plastic containers inside white or brown corrugated boxes under the brand names "Isabelle's Kitchen," "Maple Avenue Foods," or "Kings." It is possible that repackaged items sold at the supermarket level may have different sell-by dates, but none will exceed the original manufacture sell-by date.

The recall only affects specific deli salad items produced using cucumbers processed on May 14 and May 15, 2025, bearing designated Julian code use-by dates. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products to date, according to the FDA notice. Retailers have been notified to remove and destroy the recalled products and inform consumers accordingly. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

title: Supreme Produce Fresh Cucumbers and Salad Trays Recalled for Salmonella Risk
headline: Supreme Produce Recalls Fresh Cucumbers and Salad Trays Due to Salmonella Contamination Concern
description: Supreme Service Solutions, LLC. has recalled Supreme Produce fresh cucumbers and salad or vegetable trays containing cucumbers due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses linked to these products have been reported.
productDescription: Fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers.
reason: Due to possible contamination with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On May 28, 2025, Supreme Service Solutions, LLC., doing business as Supreme Produce, voluntarily recalled fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers due to possible contamination with Salmonella. According to the FDA notice, these products were distributed through Kroger and affiliated retail stores and sourced from Bedner Growers Inc.

Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Severe cases can result in more serious health complications.

The recall was initiated after notification from the FDA regarding a Salmonella outbreak associated with cucumbers. The affected products were distributed to Kroger stores and affiliates in Indiana, Illinois, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Mississippi, Missouri, Arkansas, and Michigan. As of the recall date, no illnesses or consumer complaints have been reported in connection with Supreme Produce products.

title: Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing Recalled for Undeclared Soy Allergen
headline: Santa Monica Seafood Recalls Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing for Undeclared Soy
description: Santa Monica Seafood recalled Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing due to undeclared soy, posing a risk to those with soy allergies. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On May 24, 2025, Santa Monica Seafood, based in Rancho Dominguez, California, issued a voluntary recall of Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing sold under the Aldi brand. The recall was initiated after undeclared soy was discovered in the product, which could cause serious or life-threatening reactions in individuals with soy allergies or sensitivities.

The affected product was distributed to Aldi stores in California, Nevada, and Arizona. According to the FDA recall notice, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. The recall followed the identification of a labeling error during a routine inspection, where soy was not declared as an ingredient.

Product NameBrandSizeUse/Freeze By
Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood StuffingAldi16 ozJun. 02, 2025

facts.product: Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing should check the packaging for the affected product and lot. If you have a soy allergy, do not consume the product and contact Santa Monica Seafood for further assistance.

title: Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD Reptile Food Recalled for Salmonella Risk, FDA Notice
headline: Spectrum Brands Pet LLC Recalls Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD Reptile Food Due to Salmonella Contamination
description: Spectrum Brands Pet LLC recalled one lot of Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food after a component tested positive for Salmonella. No illnesses reported.
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Spectrum Brands Pet LLC is voluntarily recalling a single lot of Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food due to possible Salmonella contamination. The affected product is intended for aquatic turtles and comes in a 1.56 oz plastic container with a green label. Only lot code 951790 is included in this recall; no other lots or Tetra products are affected.

Salmonella can cause illness in both animals and humans. In humans, symptoms may include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. Rarely, more severe complications can occur. In aquatic turtles, signs may include lethargy, diarrhea, vomiting, fever, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Even animals without symptoms can spread Salmonella to people or other animals.

The recalled lot was distributed nationwide from May 6, 2025, to May 16, 2025, through retailers such as Petco, Walmart, Chewy, Amazon, and Meijer. The recall was initiated after internal testing indicated a component ingredient may contain Salmonella, though the finished product tested negative. Spectrum Brands Pet LLC has not received any reports of illness or adverse events related to this recall as of the FDA notice.

Product NameLot CodePackage SizeUPCDistribution DatesRetailers
Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food9517901.56 oz plastic container0 46798 78626 9May 6, 2025 – May 16, 2025Petco, Walmart, Chewy, Amazon, Meijer

content.text: Consumers should stop using Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food with lot code 951790 and contact the retailer for a refund if affected, as directed by the FDA recall notice.

title: Albertsons Companies Recalls Ready Meals & Star Market Greek Salad for Salmonella Risk
headline: Ready Meals & Star Market Greek Salad Recalled by Albertsons Companies Due to Salmonella Concerns
description: Albertsons Companies recalled select Ready Meals & Star Market Greek Salad products due to possible Salmonella contamination, per FDA notice issued May 23, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on May 23, 2025, Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled three store-made deli Greek Salad items under the Ready Meals and Star Market brands. The recall is due to possible Salmonella contamination linked to cucumbers supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser's Fine Foods, Inc. The cucumbers were grown by Bedner Growers, Inc. in Boynton Beach, Florida and are associated with an ongoing Salmonella illness outbreak.

Salmonella infection may cause serious illness, particularly in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms can include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The recalled Greek Salad products were distributed across multiple states in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic regions through various store banners.

The FDA notice states that injuries or adverse events related to this recall have been reported. Consumers are urged to review the product details below and follow the recommended actions.

Product NameUPCSizeSell Thru DatesStore BannersStates
SALAD GREEK AUTHENTIC FS29307000000–00901LBSMay 23, 2025 to May 24, 2025ACME, Safeway, Kings Food Markets, Balducci's Food Lovers MarketCT, DE, DC, MD, NJ, NY, PA, VA
READYMEALS SALAD GREEK SS29248300000LBSMay 20, 2025 to May 24, 2025Shaw's, Star MarketMA, ME, NH, RI, VT
SALAD GREEK FS29232900000LBSMay 20, 2025 to May 24, 2025Shaw's, Star MarketMA, ME, NH, RI, VT

For additional information, see the official FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have the recalled Ready Meals or Star Market Greek Salad products and, if so, do not consume them. Dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase as directed in the FDA recall notice.

title: Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices Recalled by Walmart in Texas for Salmonella Risk
headline: Walmart Recalls Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices in Select Texas Stores Due to Salmonella
description: Walmart has recalled Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices in select Texas stores after FDA notification of possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Walmart Inc. has voluntarily recalled Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices sold in select Texas stores due to potential Salmonella contamination. This action follows an FDA notification regarding whole cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers, Inc., which have been linked to reported salmonellosis illnesses. No illnesses have been reported in connection with the recalled Marketside product as of the recall date.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, particularly in young children, older adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The recall applies to the following product:

According to the FDA recall notice, Walmart Inc. has voluntarily recalled Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices sold in select Texas stores due to potential Salmonella contamination. This action follows an FDA notification regarding whole cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers, Inc., which have been linked to reported salmonellosis illnesses. No illnesses have been reported in connection with the recalled Marketside product as of the recall date.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, particularly in young children, older adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The recall applies to the following product:

Product DescriptionUPC/PLUAverage Unit WeightDate Codes
Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices629691.5 lbsAll date codes up to 5/24/2025

Consumers are advised not to consume, serve, sell, or distribute the recalled cucumber slices. For more information, refer to the official FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers in Texas who purchased Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices with date codes up to 5/24/2025 should not eat the product. Check packaging for details and follow the FDA recall notice for instructions on disposal or return.

title: Snowfruit & Snowfox Fresh Cut Cucumber and Sushi Recall for Salmonella Risk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, JFE Franchising, Inc. of Houston, TX, is voluntarily recalling a limited number of Snowfruit and Snowfox branded fresh-cut cucumber products and sushi items containing cucumber due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Typical symptoms include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

No illnesses or consumer complaints have been reported to date in connection with these products. The recall follows a prior recall of cucumbers by Bedner Growers, Inc., which supplied cucumbers used in JFE Franchising's products. The affected items were distributed through Kroger, Roundy's (Pick n Save, Metro Market), and Weis Market stores in states including Indiana, Kentucky, Illinois, Michigan, Tennessee, Mississippi, Arkansas, Alabama, Wisconsin, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Delaware, Maryland, Virginia, and New York.

title: PennRose Farms Recalls Whole Cucumbers for Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: PennRose Farms Recalls Whole Cucumbers Due to Salmonella Risk
description: PennRose Farms, LLC has recalled whole cucumbers in 5-pound mesh bags due to possible Salmonella contamination. FDA notice published May 22, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA recall notice, PennRose Farms, LLC has recalled 5-pound mesh bags of whole cucumbers due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can lead to more severe complications.

The recalled cucumbers were repacked by PennRose Farms and originally recalled by Bedner Growers, Inc., as notified by their supplier Fresh Start Produce Sales, Inc. The affected products are 5-pound mesh bags labeled with Restaurant Depot and PennRose Farms logos, packaged between May 2 and May 5, 2025, and distributed to Restaurant Depot centers in New Jersey, Georgia, Florida, Illinois, and Ohio.

As of the FDA notice, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product DescriptionPackagingUPC CodePackaged DatesLot NumbersDistribution Locations
Whole Cucumbers5-pound mesh bags841214101714May 2 – May 5, 202548-122, 48-123, 48-124, 48-125Restaurant Depot distribution centers in NJ, GA, FL, IL, OH

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their whole cucumbers are part of the recalled lots and avoid consuming them. If affected, follow the FDA notice and dispose of the product.

title: UMARY USA Recalls Unavy and Umovy Ácido Hialurónico Caplets for Undeclared Dexamethasone, Diclofenac, and Omeprazole
headline: Unavy and Umovy Ácido Hialurónico Caplets Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Drug Ingredients
description: UMARY USA is recalling all lots of Unavy and Umovy Ácido Hialurónico caplets (30 caplets/850 mg) due to undeclared dexamethasone, diclofenac, and omeprazole, per FDA notice.
productDescription: Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg)
reason: The product contains undeclared Dexamethasone, Diclofenac and Omeprazole

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, UMARY USA has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Unavy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) due to the presence of undeclared drug ingredients: dexamethasone, diclofenac, and omeprazole. These products were marketed as dietary supplements but have been identified as unapproved new drugs by the FDA because they contain active pharmaceutical ingredients not listed on the labels.

Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that may impair immune function and cause serious side effects such as high blood sugar, muscle injuries, psychiatric conditions, cardiovascular events, and adrenal gland suppression, with potential withdrawal symptoms upon abrupt discontinuation. Diclofenac is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that can cause cardiovascular, gastrointestinal, renal, and allergic reactions, especially in individuals taking other NSAIDs or anticoagulants. Omeprazole, a proton pump inhibitor, may mask serious stomach conditions including ulcers and cancer.

UMARY USA is notifying distributors and customers and is organizing return and refund procedures for affected products. No injuries or adverse events have been reported to date, according to the FDA.

BrandProduct NameQuantityDosage per CapletContainer DescriptionLot NumbersExpiration Dates
UNAVYÁcido Hialurónico30 caplets850 mgWhite plastic bottle with black background label, white and yellow writingAll lotsAll expiration dates
UMOVYÁcido Hialurónico30 caplets850 mgBlack plastic bottle with black label, white and blue letteringAll lotsAll expiration dates

Distribution occurred nationwide via online sales through umary-usa.com. Adverse events or quality issues can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program as outlined in the official notice.

facts.product: Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using Unavy or Umovy Ácido Hialurónico caplets (30 caplets/850 mg) and contact UMARY USA for support. Review your product details and avoid use until further guidance is provided by the company.

title: Publix Recalls GreenWise Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food Pouches for Elevated Lead
headline: GreenWise Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food Pouches Recalled by Publix for Potential Lead Contamination
description: Publix has recalled GreenWise Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food pouches due to possible elevated lead levels. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA notice.
productDescription: Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food pouches
reason: Product may be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Publix Super Markets, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of GreenWise Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food pouches because routine sampling found potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. The product, produced by Bowman Andros LLC, was distributed in Publix stores across its eight-state operating area.

The recalled baby food is packaged in 4-ounce plastic pouches. This action is being taken in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to protect consumers.

Publix has removed the affected product from store shelves. According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse events related to this recall have been reported to date.

title: Ukrop's Recalls Marinated Cucumber Salad for Possible Salmonella Contamination

description: Ukrop's Homestyle Foods has recalled select Marinated Cucumber Salad products due to possible Salmonella contamination, per FDA notice issued May 22, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ukrop's Homestyle Foods, LLC, based in Richmond, VA, has issued a recall of three marinated cucumber salad products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This recall is linked to a related recall by Bedner Growers of cucumbers, which are used as an ingredient in these products. According to the FDA, Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection may include fever, diarrhea (which can be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe if it spreads to the bloodstream, potentially leading to complications such as infected aneurysms, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall affects specific sell-by dates of Ukrop's Marinated Cucumber Salad in various sizes, distributed to multiple retailers across several states.

ProductNet WeightSell-By Date Range
Marinated Cucumber Salad16 ounces5/11/25 through 5/26/25
Marinated Cucumber Salad36 ounces5/11/25 through 5/26/25
Marinated Cucumber Salad Bulk5 lbs.5/11/25 through 5/26/25

The affected products were available at the following retailers:

  • Food Lion Stores in Virginia and North Carolina
  • Harris Teeter, Williamsburg, VA
  • Kroger Stores in Virginia, West Virginia, Ohio, and Kentucky
  • Libbie Market, Richmond, VA
  • Ukrop's Market Hall, Richmond, VA

All retail locations have been notified and are removing the recalled products from their shelves. For more information, visit ukropshomestylefoods.com or the FDA website.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the sell-by dates on Ukrop's Marinated Cucumber Salad and avoid consuming any affected products. Refer to the FDA notice for full details.

title: Big Y Foods Recalls Made-to-Order Subs, Wraps, and Paninis for Salmonella Risk in MA and CT
headline: Big Y Foods Issues Recall of Made-to-Order Subs, Wraps, and Paninis Due to Salmonella Concerns
description: Big Y Foods has recalled made-to-order subs, wraps, and paninis sold in select Massachusetts and Connecticut stores due to potential salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on May 22, 2025, Big Y Foods of Springfield, MA, is voluntarily recalling certain made-to-order subs, wraps, and paninis sold between May 20 and May 21, 2025. The recall affects products from select Big Y retail locations in Massachusetts and Connecticut due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, older adults, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection may include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions.

The recall is linked to sliced cucumbers supplied by Smartcuts, used as an ingredient in the affected products. This action is part of a broader investigation into a cucumber-related outbreak. Big Y Foods has ceased operations at the affected service lines, discarded remaining product, and performed thorough cleaning and sanitization at the listed stores.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice. Consumers who purchased these items on the specified dates at the affected locations are eligible for a refund. For more information, refer to the official FDA recall notice.

title: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Recalls Breads, Bagels, Cookies, and More for Undeclared Allergens
headline: Recall: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Products Due to Undeclared Egg, Tree Nuts, Soy, and Milk
description: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery recalled select breads, bagels, cookies, caramel bars, and croutons for undeclared egg, tree nuts, soy, and milk. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Breads, bagels, caramel bars, cookies and croutons
reason: Undeclared Allergen – Egg, tree nuts Soy and Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, New Grains Gluten Free Bakery has recalled multiple bakery products due to undeclared allergens: egg, tree nuts, soy, and milk. These allergens are not listed on the product labels, which may pose a risk for people with allergies or sensitivities. The recall includes various breads, bagels, cookies, caramel bars, and croutons distributed in Utah retail stores.

The affected products were distributed between April 7 and April 21, 2025, under lot numbers 90–107. Breads and croutons were packaged in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags, while cookies were in clear regular plastic bags. All products are labeled as "gluten-free" under the New Grains brand, with label colors in red, purple, orange, blue, green, or pink.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that some products contained egg, tree nuts, soy, and milk without proper allergen labeling. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

  • Lot Numbers: 90–107

  • FDA Recall Information: FDA recall notice

title: Bedner Growers, Inc. Recalls No Brand Name Cucumbers for Salmonella Risk
headline: No Brand Name Cucumbers Recalled by Bedner Growers, Inc. Due to Salmonella Contamination
description: Bedner Growers, Inc. is recalling No Brand Name cucumbers sold in Florida markets due to potential Salmonella contamination, per FDA notice in May 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Bedner Growers, Inc. of Boynton Beach, Florida, is voluntarily recalling cucumbers sold at Bedner's Farm Fresh Market locations between April 29, 2025, and May 14, 2025, due to possible Salmonella contamination. Salmonella can cause serious infections, especially in children, elderly adults, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe illnesses. The recalled cucumbers were sold without any identifying stickers or labels at Bedner's Farm Fresh Market stores in Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, and West Palm Beach, Florida. The product was also distributed to a wholesale distributor for further distribution.

The FDA has linked this recall to a Salmonella outbreak with 26 reported illnesses in multiple states. Cucumbers sold after May 14, 2025, or other products from Bedner's Farm Fresh Markets are not affected. Bedner Growers, Inc. has ended cucumber production for the season.

ProductRetail LocationsRecall DatesReason for Recall
Cucumbers (No brand name, no labels)Bedner's Farm Fresh Markets in Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, and West Palm Beach, FLApril 29, 2025 – May 14, 2025Potential Salmonella contamination
  • No injuries or illnesses have been reported by Bedner Growers, Inc. at the time of the FDA notice.
  • This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
  • For more information, see the official FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased cucumbers at Bedner's Farm Fresh Market locations in Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, or West Palm Beach, FL, between April 29 and May 14, 2025, should not eat them and should discard the product immediately, as advised in the FDA recall notice.

title: Madeline's Pâtisserie Recalls Croissants and Croissant Buns for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Recall: Madeline's Pâtisserie Croissants and Croissant Buns Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Element 112, LLC dba Madeline's Pâtisserie is recalling select croissants and croissant buns because wheat was not properly declared on the label, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Element 112, LLC dba Madeline's Pâtisserie has voluntarily recalled certain croissants and croissant buns due to undeclared wheat. The ingredient statement listed "flour" instead of specifying "wheat," which may pose a risk to individuals with wheat allergies.

The recalled products were fully baked and frozen, distributed in food service cases of 24 units, and produced between March 10, 2025, and May 7, 2025. Distribution occurred through food distributors in Michigan and Ohio.

No illnesses or adverse reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA's announcement.

title: Recall of Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara Recalled Over Undeclared Peanut Allergen Risk
description: South Asian Food Inc. recalled Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara due to undeclared peanut allergen. No illnesses reported. FDA notice May 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
South Asian Food Inc., based in Maspeth, NY, has recalled its 1875g Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara due to undeclared peanuts. This poses a risk to those with peanut allergies or sensitivities.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail order. The issue was discovered when a peanut-containing ingredient was found in the product but not listed on the label, attributed to human error during packaging. Production has been suspended while the company collaborates with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to address the issue.

No allergic reactions or illnesses related to this recall have been reported, according to the official FDA notice.

ProductPackage SizeLot NumberUPC NumberExpiration Date
Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara1875g007182444837234006/10/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with peanut allergies should not consume the recalled Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara. Check your packaging for the affected lot and UPC. Contact South Asian Food Inc. for further instructions if you have this product.

title: Ariana Sweets Recalls Afghani Corn Bread for Undeclared Sesame and Wheat Allergens
headline: Ariana Sweets Inc. Recalls Afghani Corn Bread Due to Undeclared Sesame and Wheat
description: Ariana Sweets Inc. recalled Afghani Corn Bread after FDA found undeclared sesame and wheat allergens. No illnesses reported as of May 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA notice published on May 14, 2025, Ariana Sweets Inc. of Fremont, California, has recalled its AFGHANI CORN BREAD "Doda" produced before April 24, 2025. The recall was issued after a routine FDA inspection found the product contained undeclared sesame and wheat allergens, which were not listed on the packaging. This poses a risk to individuals with allergies or sensitivities to these ingredients.

The recalled product is a 24 oz round sweet bread, packaged in a plastic bag, and distributed to retail locations in California. The affected product has UPC 753182432670. As of the recall date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

ProductDescriptionManufacture DateUPCDistribution Area
AFGHANI CORN BREAD "Doda"24 oz round sweet bread, plastic bag packagingProduced before 4/24/2025753182432670California retail locations

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with sesame or wheat allergies should not eat Ariana Afghani Corn Bread produced before April 24, 2025. Check the UPC 753182432670 and return the product to the place of purchase or dispose of it as directed in the FDA notice.

content.text: Consumer support: (510) 795-9600 | info@arianasweets.com

title: Nature Mills Recalls Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads, and Vadam for Undeclared Wheat, Milk, Sesame
headline: Nature Mills US Recalls Multiple Food Products for Undeclared Wheat, Milk, and Sesame Allergens
description: Nature Mills US recalled select rice mixes, soups, spice mixes, porridge mix, papads, and vadam due to undeclared wheat, milk, and sesame. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Nature Mills US Inc. of Prosper, Texas, has recalled specific rice mixes, soups, spice mixes, porridge mix, papads, and vadam products due to undeclared wheat, milk, and sesame allergens. The absence of these allergens on product labels may pose a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for those with allergies or sensitivities to wheat, milk, or sesame.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide in the United States through the Nature Mills website from December 1, 2023, to May 10, 2025. The recall was initiated following an internal audit that identified missing allergen information on the labels. As of the FDA recall notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

title: Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits Recalled for Undeclared Almonds, FDA Alert 2025
headline: Knockro Recalls Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits for Undeclared Tree Nut Allergen
description: Knockro Inc. recalled Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits due to undeclared almonds, posing a risk to those with nut allergies. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Knockro Inc. of Pennsylvania has recalled its Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits because the granola component contains undeclared almonds, a tree nut allergen. Individuals with almond allergies or severe sensitivities risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The affected parfaits were distributed nationwide in 12-ounce clear plastic cups with an expiration date of May 5, 2025. As of the recall announcement, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the granola included almonds not listed on the product label. The issue was traced to a temporary lapse in production and packaging processes.

BrandProductPackage SizeExpiration DateCauseContact Phone
BonyaLow Fat Yogurt Parfaits12 oz clear plastic cupMay 5, 2025Undeclared Almonds (Tree Nuts) in Granola914-313-6905

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the packaging of Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits for the affected expiration date and discontinue use if allergic to almonds. For questions or assistance, contact Knockro Inc. as directed in the official FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Knockro Inc. of Pennsylvania has recalled its Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits because the granola component contains undeclared almonds, a tree nut allergen. Individuals with almond allergies or severe sensitivities risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The affected parfaits were distributed nationwide in 12-ounce clear plastic cups with an expiration date of May 5, 2025. As of the recall announcement, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the granola included almonds not listed on the product label. The issue was traced to a temporary lapse in production and packaging processes.

title: Fresh & Ready Foods Recalls Ready-to-Eat Sandwiches and Snacks for Potential Listeria Risk in Four States
headline: Ready-to-Eat Sandwiches and Snack Items Recalled by Fresh & Ready Foods Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
description: Fresh & Ready Foods LLC has recalled select ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items sold in AZ, CA, NV, and WA due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
productDescription: Ready to eat sandwiches and snack items
brandName: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go
brandNames: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fresh & Ready Foods LLC has issued a voluntary recall of certain ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items distributed in Arizona, California, Nevada, and Washington due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. The affected products were distributed between April 18, 2025, and April 28, 2025, mainly in vending and breakroom areas of corporate offices, medical buildings, and healthcare facilities. The recall applies to products with "Use By" dates from April 22, 2025, to May 19, 2025.

The recall was initiated after an FDA site inspection detected Listeria monocytogenes on production equipment. Fresh & Ready Foods has removed the affected equipment and taken corrective actions in accordance with FDA guidance.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections in young children, elderly people, individuals with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. No illnesses have been reported in connection with these products as of the recall date.

title: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Recalls Breads, Bagels, Cookies, and Croutons for Undeclared Eggs, Soy, and Milk
headline: Recall: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Breads, Bagels, Cookies, and Croutons Due to Undeclared Allergens
description: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery recalled select breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons sold in Utah due to undeclared eggs, soy, and milk. No illnesses reported.
reason: Products may contain undeclared eggs, soy, and milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Grains Gluten Free Bakery has recalled multiple bakery products, including breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons, because they may contain undeclared eggs, soy, and milk. These allergens were not listed on the product labels, which could cause serious or life-threatening reactions in those with allergies or sensitivities to these ingredients.

The recalled products were distributed in Utah retail stores from April 7, 2024, to April 21, 2024, under lot numbers 90–107. Breads and croutons were packaged in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags, while cookies were in clear plastic bags. All items are labeled as "gluten-free" under the New Grains Gluten Free Bakery brand, with various label colors.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall as of the FDA notice. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Organic Traditions Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds Recalled for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Advantage Health Matters Inc. Recalls Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Advantage Health Matters Inc. is recalling Organic Traditions Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Advantage Health Matters Inc., based in North York, Ontario, Canada, has recalled its 8-ounce packages of Organic Traditions Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds due to potential Salmonella contamination, according to the FDA notice. Salmonella can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The affected product was distributed in New York, New Jersey, and Virginia through retail stores and mail orders. As of the recall date, no illnesses have been reported. The recall follows a supplier recall from another country, and production has been suspended while the source is investigated.

ProductPackage SizeLot NumberExpiration DateDistribution Area
Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds8 ouncesL25032020005/02/2027New York, New Jersey, Virginia

content.text: Consumers should check if they have Organic Traditions Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds with the specified lot number and expiration date. Do not consume the product; dispose of it safely. For questions, contact the company at info@organictraditions.com.

title: Endurance Boost Dietary Supplement Recalled for Undeclared Sildenafil and Propoxyphenylsildenafil
headline: Endurance Boost Capsules Recalled Nationwide Due to Undeclared Drug Ingredients
description: EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi is recalling Endurance Boost dietary supplements after FDA found undeclared sildenafil and propoxyphenylsildenafil. No adverse events reported.
productDescription: Dietary supplement for male performance and energy

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of its dietary supplement capsules, ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED (20 Count). This product, marketed for male performance and energy, was found to contain undeclared Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil. Sildenafil is a pharmaceutical ingredient used to treat erectile dysfunction and cannot be legally included in dietary supplements.

The presence of these unlisted drug substances poses health risks, especially for individuals who take nitrates for conditions such as heart disease, diabetes, high blood pressure, or high cholesterol. These interactions can cause dangerously low blood pressure, which may be life threatening. The recalled product lot is identified as LOT: 250214PRO with an expiration date of 02/14/2027.

To date, no adverse events or illnesses related to this recall have been reported, according to the official FDA notice. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product NameLot NumberExpiration DateProduct DescriptionDistribution
ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED (20 Count)250214PRO02/14/2027Blue and white cardboard carton with white and black lettering, containing a 20-count blister packOnline via US Amazon.com only

facts.product: Dietary supplement for male performance and energy

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Endurance Boost with lot number 250214PRO and return the product as directed in the official recall notice.

title: Recall of New England Village Snacks 19th Hole Snack Mix for Undeclared Almonds and Sesame
headline: Undeclared Almonds and Sesame Prompt Recall of 19th Hole Snack Mix by New England Village Foods
description: New England Village Foods recalled all lots of 19th Hole Snack Mix due to undeclared almonds and sesame, posing allergy risks. No illnesses reported per FDA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, New England Village Foods of Milford, New Hampshire, has recalled all lots of its "19th Hole Snack Mix" sold under the New England Village Snacks brand. The recall involves 5-ounce and 10-ounce containers due to undeclared almonds and sesame, which could cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in those with allergies to these ingredients.

The affected snack mix was distributed to small independent grocery markets and convenience stores in New England, New York, and Pennsylvania. The products are packaged in clear, reclosable cups and tubs with lids bearing UPCs 609465693477 (5-ounce) and 642147152459 (10-ounce). The labeling did not disclose almonds, and the "Contains" statement omitted sesame, despite its presence in the ingredient list.

The recall was initiated after a customer found almonds in the product without proper labeling. The FDA confirmed the labeling issue, which resulted from a temporary breakdown in the company's packaging process. Production of the snack mix has been suspended as a precaution. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA notice.

Product NamePackage SizeUPC
19th Hole Snack Mix5-ounce609465693477
19th Hole Snack Mix10-ounce642147152459

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their 19th Hole Snack Mix matches the affected UPCs and sizes listed in the FDA notice. If so, do not consume the product and follow the FDA guidance for returns or questions.

The labeling did not disclose almonds, and the "Contains" statement omitted sesame, despite its presence in the ingredient list.

The recall was initiated after a customer found almonds in the product without proper labeling. The FDA confirmed the labeling issue, which resulted from a temporary breakdown in the company's packaging process. Production of the snack mix has been suspended as a precaution. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA notice.

19th Hole Snack Mix10-ounce642147152459

title: President Brand Licorice Plum Recalled for Undeclared Sulfites and Unapproved Color
headline: Eats CL Trading, Inc. Recalls President Brand Licorice Plum for Undeclared Sulfites and Amaranth (E123)
description: Eats CL Trading, Inc. recalled President Brand Licorice Plum due to undeclared sulfites and unapproved color Amaranth (E123). No illnesses have been reported.
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – sulfites Unapproved color – Amaranth (E123)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Eats CL Trading, Inc. of Long Island City, NY, has recalled its 8-ounce President Brand Licorice Plum due to undeclared sulfites and the use of the unapproved color additive Amaranth (E123), according to the FDA notice.

People with sulfite sensitivity may experience serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product. Testing found 29.1 mg of sulfites per serving, exceeding the 10 mg threshold that can cause severe reactions in some individuals, including anaphylactic shock in those sensitive to sulfites.

The recalled Licorice Plum was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail order. As of the FDA notice date, no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported. The recall was initiated after routine inspection and laboratory analysis by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets identified undeclared sulfites.

ProductPackage SizeUPC CodeReason for Recall
Licorice Plum8 ounces (clear plastic bottle)0077-20729Undeclared sulfites; Unapproved color Amaranth (E123)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have the recalled President Brand Licorice Plum (8 oz, UPC 0077-20729) and follow FDA guidance. For questions, contact Eats CL Trading, Inc. as directed in the official notice.

title: Recall of Yellowstone Baked Beans for Undeclared Soy Allergen, Vietti Food Group, 2025
headline: Yellowstone 15-oz Baked Beans Recalled for Undeclared Soy by Vietti Food Group
description: Vietti Food Group recalled Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz) due to undeclared soy. No illnesses reported. FDA notice May 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published May 5, 2025, Vietti Food Group of Nashville, TN has recalled 4,515 cases of Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz.) due to undeclared soy. Individuals with a soy allergy or severe sensitivity risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled beans were distributed to retailers in multiple states, including Arizona, Colorado, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Mississippi, Missouri, New Hampshire, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Virginia. The affected cans are marked with the lot code "Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028" on the bottom.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the FDA notice. The recall details, including affected states and product identification, are provided in the official FDA announcement.

ProductSizeLot CodeReason for RecallDistribution States
Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans15 oz.Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028Undeclared soy allergenAZ, CO, DE, FL, GA, IL, IA, KS, KY, LA, MI, MS, MO, NH, NY, NC, OH, OR, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz) with the lot code 'Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028' and avoid consuming if allergic to soy. For questions, contact Vietti Food Group at (513) 682-2474 or recall@zwanfood.com.

title: Ray & Mascari Inc. Recalls 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes for Potential Salmonella Risk
headline: 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes by Ray & Mascari Inc. Recalled Due to Salmonella Contamination Risk
description: Ray & Mascari Inc. has recalled 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported as of the FDA's May 2025 notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ray & Mascari Inc. of Indianapolis, Indiana, is voluntarily recalling their 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes packaged in 20 oz. (1 lb. 4 oz) clam shell containers due to a potential risk of Salmonella contamination. The affected products carry UPC# 7 96553 20062 1 and are identified by master case Lot# RM250424 15250B or Lot# RM250427 15250B. This recall follows notification from Hanshaw & Capling Farms, the original supplier, who initiated a recall after detecting possible Salmonella presence in their facility.

Salmonella infections can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms commonly include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, Salmonella can lead to severe complications such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The recalled 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes were sold in plastic clamshell containers labeled "Packed by Ray & Mascari Inc., Indianapolis, IN 46204" and distributed primarily through Gordon Food Service Stores across Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Missouri, Mississippi, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, and Wisconsin.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date, according to the FDA notice.

ProductPackage SizeUPCLot NumberDistribution Areas
4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes20 oz. (1 lb. 4 oz) Clam shell (567g)7 96553 20062 1RM250424 15250B, RM250427 15250BIL, IN, KY, MI, MO, MS, NY, OH, PA, TN, WI

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes with the listed UPC and lot numbers and avoid consuming them due to potential Salmonella risk. Dispose of affected products as directed by the recall notice.

title: H&C Farms Label Tomatoes Recalled by Williams Farms Repack LLC for Possible Salmonella
headline: Williams Farms Repack LLC Issues Recall of H&C Farms Label Tomatoes for Salmonella Risk
description: Williams Farms Repack LLC recalled H&C Farms Label tomatoes due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses have been reported as of the FDA's May 2025 notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published on May 2, 2025, Williams Farms Repack LLC has recalled multiple tomato products under the H&C Farms Label due to potential Salmonella contamination. Salmonella can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly people, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may become severe.

The recall was initiated after notification from Southeast Tomato Distributors regarding possible contamination in tomatoes supplied from H&C Farms. As of the recall date, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported in connection with these products.

The affected tomatoes were distributed to wholesalers and distributors in Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina between April 23, 2025, and April 28, 2025.

title: JJWV Marketing Recalls Ankimo Monkfish Liver for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Ankimo Monkfish Liver Recalled Due to Undeclared Milk, FDA Reports
description: JJWV Marketing Corporation recalled Ankimo Monkfish Liver after discovering undeclared milk, posing a risk to those with milk allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
JJWV Marketing Corporation of Santa Fe Springs, California, has recalled Ankimo Monkfish Liver because it contains undeclared milk. Individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities may face a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled product was distributed in California through retailers such as Little Tokyo Market Place and H Marts. According to the official FDA notice, no illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the recall date.

The recall was initiated after it was found that the product packaging did not disclose the presence of milk protein. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is aware of and cooperating with this recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Ankimo Monkfish Liver should check the packaging for lot information and avoid consuming the product if it may be affected by the recall due to undeclared milk.
