title: Trader Joe's Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Undeclared Milk Prompts Recall of Trader Joe's Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon
description: Taylor Fresh Foods has recalled Trader Joe's Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon due to undeclared milk. No illnesses have been reported, per the FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Taylor Fresh Foods has voluntarily recalled Trader Joe's Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon because some packages may contain a sealed packet of parmesan cheese crumbles, resulting in undeclared milk. This recall affects products with Use By dates of April 28, 2025, and April 29, 2025.

Approximately 500 units were distributed to Trader Joe's stores in multiple states. Individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities are at risk if they consume the affected product. No adverse reactions or illnesses have been reported as of the FDA's announcement. The recall is limited to the specified product and dates; no other Trader Joe's products are involved.

title: Health Fixer Recalls Male Enhancement Supplements for Undeclared PDE-5 Inhibitors
headline: Health Fixer Recalls Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor, and Male Ultra Pro Due to Undeclared Drug Ingredients
description: Health Fixer recalled certain male enhancement supplements after FDA testing found undeclared chloropretadalafil, propoxyphenylsildenafil, and sildenafil. No adverse events reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 29, 2025, Health Fixer announced a voluntary nationwide recall of several male enhancement dietary supplements after FDA laboratory analysis detected undeclared chloropretadalafil, propoxyphenylsildenafil, and sildenafil. These substances are phosphodiesterase (PDE-5) inhibitors or analogues, which are not permitted in dietary supplements and may pose health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates for heart conditions.

The recalled products include Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor, and Male Ultra Pro, distributed nationwide via Amazon in blister packs of 10 capsules. According to the official FDA notice, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported to date.

Product NameNDC/UPCLot NumberExpiration Date
Male UltraB0CMQ4FTHGKT-1ST-43-0110-202501/10/2027
MalextraB0CWKZ6ZP3KT-1ST-43-0104/202604/25/2026
Electro BuzzB0DK68LF6JKT-1ST-43-0107/202410/15/2026
Ultra ArmorB0CYJ7Y5H9KT-1ST-43-0110/202501/10/2027
Male Ultra ProB0CZN7C6YHKT-1ST-43-0110/202503/15/2026

Consumers with questions about the recall can contact Health Fixer by phone or email as provided in the FDA notice. Anyone experiencing health issues after using these products should consult a healthcare provider and may report adverse reactions to the FDA's MedWatch program.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Health Fixer male enhancement supplements and check the lot numbers listed in the recall. For further instructions or questions, contact Health Fixer at 414-888-8818 or ultrahelpbyfixer@gmail.com as directed in the FDA notice.

title: Mauna Loa Recalls Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias for Undeclared Almonds and Cashews
headline: Recall: Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias May Contain Undeclared Almonds and Cashews
description: Mauna Loa is recalling select Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias due to undeclared almond and cashew allergens. No illnesses have been reported, per FDA.
companyName: Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC of Kea'au, HI, has voluntarily recalled certain Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias due to undeclared almonds and cashews. This recall is important for individuals with nut allergies, as consuming the product could cause a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction.

The recall covers 0.6oz and 4oz bags distributed in multiple states and Guam. The affected products were identified through the company's internal quality control process. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported as of the recall date.

title: Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ Veterinary Dexmedetomidine Injection Recalled for Particulate Matter
headline: Cronus Pharma Recalls Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL Due to Particulates
description: Cronus Pharma LLC recalled Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL for veterinary use due to visible particulate matter, per FDA notice.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 24, 2025, Cronus Pharma LLC initiated a voluntary recall of Dexased™ (Aspen) and Dexmedvet™ (Cronus Pharma LLC) Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL for veterinary use due to the presence of visible particulate matter. These injectable products are used as sedatives and analgesics in dogs and cats for clinical procedures and as preanesthetics.

The recall affects one batch with two lot numbers distributed between December 2024 and April 2025. Particulate matter observed included crystal-like particles, white floating material, or precipitates. According to the FDA notice, particulates in injectable products may cause inflammation, granuloma, fibrosis, or blood vessel blockage, potentially leading to serious adverse events such as stroke, life-threatening clots, or death.

Veterinary professionals and pet owners are advised to monitor animals for adverse reactions, including pain, weakness, swelling, paralysis, fever, respiratory difficulty, vomiting, decreased activity, vocalization, or loss of consciousness. The company is coordinating with distributors to remove affected products from the market. Adverse events may be reported to the FDA as directed in the official notice.

BrandNDC NumberLot NumberManufacturing DateExpiration DateDistribution Dates
Dexased™ (Aspen)46066-088-13C24041AV1Nov 2024Oct 2026December 2024 to April 4, 2025
Dexmedvet™ (Cronus Pharma LLC)69043-038-10C24041AV2Nov 2024Oct 2026March 2025 to April 4, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and veterinary professionals should check Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL for affected lot numbers and discontinue use if identified in the recall.

title: Recall of 19th Hole Snack Mix by New England Village Foods for Undeclared Almonds
headline: New England Village Snacks 19th Hole Snack Mix Recalled for Undeclared Almonds
description: New England Village Foods recalled all lots of 19th Hole Snack Mix due to undeclared almonds. No illnesses reported. See FDA notice for details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice published April 24, 2025, New England Village Foods of Milford, NH, is recalling all lots of its "19th Hole Snack Mix" under the New England Village Snacks brand. The recall affects both 5-ounce and 10-ounce containers due to the presence of undeclared almonds, which may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in those with almond allergies.

The recalled products were distributed to independent grocery markets and convenience stores in New England, New York, and Pennsylvania. The snack mix was packaged in clear cups and tubs with re-closable lids. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that almonds were not listed as an ingredient due to a temporary packaging issue. Production has been suspended while corrective actions are taken.

No illnesses or allergic reactions related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice date.

title: Amneal Pharmaceuticals Recalls Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP 500mg/100mL IV Bags for Potential Polypropylene Fiber Contamination
headline: Recall of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP 500mg/100mL IV Bags Due to Polypropylene Fiber Contamination Risk
description: Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC is recalling two lots of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bags due to possible polypropylene fiber contamination. No adverse events have been reported.
productDescription: Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag
reason: Product may contain an inert fiber identified as polypropylene fibers from the IV bag

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC has announced a nationwide recall of two lots of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL infusion bags. The recall is due to the potential presence of inert polypropylene fiber particulates originating from the IV bag. These fibers could pose serious health risks if introduced into the epidural or intrathecal spaces during administration.

The product is commonly used for local or regional anesthesia in surgery and acute pain management. Exposure to polypropylene particulate matter in the epidural space may trigger inflammatory processes such as meningitis or cause spinal cord damage. If administered intrathecally, these particulates could lead to inflammation, hydrocephalus, embolization, and organ damage. Currently, there have been no reported adverse events or injuries linked to this recall, according to the official FDA notice.

The affected lots, AL240003 and AL240004, both expiring in January 2026, were distributed nationwide to wholesalers and distributors between April 23, 2024, and November 8, 2024. Amneal Pharmaceuticals is contacting customers directly to facilitate the return of recalled products and requests that wholesalers and distributors inform hospital and user facilities about the recall.

ProductLot NumberExpiration DateNDCPackage Size
Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bagAL24000301/202670121-1734312 x 100mL Single Dose IV bags
Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bagAL24000401/202670121-1734312 x 100mL Single Dose IV bags

  • Fax: 631-983-2595
  • Email: RopivacaineHCl-Recall@amneal.com

For medical inquiries or to report adverse events or quality issues related to this product, contact Amneal Drug Safety:

  • Phone: 1-877-835-5472 (Monday-Friday, 8:00 am – 6:00 pm EST)
  • Email: DrugSafety@amneal.com

en.title: Amneal Pharmaceuticals Recalls Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP 500mg/100mL IV Bags for Potential Polypropylene Fiber Contamination
en.headline: Recall of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP 500mg/100mL IV Bags Due to Polypropylene Fiber Contamination Risk
en.description: Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC is recalling two lots of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bags due to possible polypropylene fiber contamination. No adverse events have been reported.
en.productDescription: Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag
en.reason: Product may contain an inert fiber identified as polypropylene fibers from the IV bag
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC has announced a nationwide recall of two lots of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL infusion bags. The recall is due to the potential presence of inert polypropylene fiber particulates originating from the IV bag. These fibers could pose serious health risks if introduced into the epidural or intrathecal spaces during administration.

The product is commonly used for local or regional anesthesia in surgery and acute pain management. Exposure to polypropylene particulate matter in the epidural space may trigger inflammatory processes such as meningitis or cause spinal cord damage. If administered intrathecally, these particulates could lead to inflammation, hydrocephalus, embolization, and organ damage. Currently, there have been no reported adverse events or injuries linked to this recall, according to the official FDA notice.

The affected lots, AL240003 and AL240004, both expiring in January 2026, were distributed nationwide to wholesalers and distributors between April 23, 2024, and November 8, 2024. Amneal Pharmaceuticals is contacting customers directly to facilitate the return of recalled products and requests that wholesalers and distributors inform hospital and user facilities about the recall.

title: Hofood99 Inc Enoki Mushrooms Recalled by Harvest NYC Inc for Listeria Risk
headline: Harvest NYC Inc Recalls Hofood99 Inc Enoki Mushrooms Over Listeria Contamination Concern
description: Harvest NYC Inc has recalled Hofood99 Inc Enoki Mushrooms due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses have been reported.
reason: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Harvest NYC Inc, based in Brooklyn, NY, has recalled its 200g packages of Enoki Mushrooms distributed under the Hofood99 Inc brand due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Listeria infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illness, especially in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The recall was initiated after the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Laboratory detected Listeria monocytogenes in a sample collected from a retail store in Buffalo, NY. The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail stores. According to the official notice, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

ProductPackage SizeUPC BarcodeDistributorDistribution Period
Enoki Mushrooms200g (green plastic package)6975730520101Hofood99 Inc., 21903 56th Ave, Oakland Gardens, NY 11364January 11–31, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Hofood99 Inc Enoki Mushrooms in 200g packages between January 11 and January 31, 2025, should not consume the product and may dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase as directed by the recall notice.

title: Q'Apel Medical 072 Aspiration System Recalled for Distal Tip Design Outside 510(k) Clearance
headline: FDA Class I Recall: Q'Apel Medical 072 Aspiration System Due to Distal Tip Design Issue
description: Q'Apel Medical recalled its 072 Aspiration System after FDA found the distal tip design was outside the scope of 510(k) clearance. Three adverse events reported.
reason: Distal Tip Design outside scope of 510k clearance

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has classified Q'Apel Medical Inc.'s voluntary recall and discontinuation of its 072 Aspiration System (also known as Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool) as a Class I recall. The recall affects 1,617 units and was initiated on February 26, 2025, after an FDA Warning Letter regarding the distal tip design being outside the scope of its 510(k) clearance.

Q'Apel Medical has chosen to remove all affected lots from distribution and discontinue the 072 Aspiration System. The product was distributed in the United States, Qatar, United Arab Emirates, and the Republic of Kazakhstan.

Three adverse events related to the Hippo product were reported: tip detachment (retrieved without patient injury), vessel rupture, and vasospasm. The company's investigation indicated that factors other than the device's distal tip may have contributed to these events.

Product DescriptionCatalog NumberUDILot Number(s)
072 Aspiration System (Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool) with Aspiration TubingAPT6072-13200857545008127FG241008C-03, FG240916C-04, FG240905C-04
072 Aspiration System (Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool)AP6072-13200857545008097FG241206A-03, FG240917A-01
072 Aspiration TubingAPT-9500857545008103FG241206A-04

Customers and distributors have been contacted to facilitate product returns. No patient injuries were reported in the tip detachment event. Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Q'Apel Medical 072 Aspiration System is affected by the recall and follow the company's instructions for product return or replacement as provided in the official FDA notice.

title: May Flower International Recalls Soybean Paste for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: May Flower International Soybean Paste Recalled Nationwide for Undeclared Wheat
description: May Flower International recalled its Beijing Soybean Paste due to undeclared wheat, posing an allergy risk. No illnesses reported per FDA notice in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, May Flower International Inc. of Maspeth, NY, has recalled its 8.82-ounce (250g) packages of "Beijing Soybean Paste" because they contain undeclared wheat. People with wheat allergies may risk serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled soybean paste was distributed nationwide in retail stores and is packaged in plastic containers with UPC 6917799000385. The product was packed for Fu Xiang Yuan Trading Inc., New York City, NY 11378, and distributed between March 25, 2023, and March 5, 2024.

The recall was initiated after a routine FDA inspection found that wheat, a major allergen, was not declared on the label, although flour was listed as an ingredient. As of the FDA notice, no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this product.

ProductPackage SizeUPCDistribution DatesPacked For
Beijing Soybean Paste8.82 ounces / 250g691779900038503/25/2023 to 03/05/2024Fu Xiang Yuan Trading Inc., New York City, NY 11378

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with wheat allergies should not consume the recalled Beijing Soybean Paste. Check the product label for UPC 6917799000385 and distribution dates. For questions or further instructions, contact May Flower International Inc. at 1-718-366-8668.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-718-366-8668

title: Dorado Nutrition and Space Garden Spermidine Supplements Recalled for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Recall: Dorado Nutrition and Space Garden Spermidine/Spermidin Supplements Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. recalled Dorado Nutrition and Space Garden Spermidine supplements for undeclared wheat. No illnesses reported as of April 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. has voluntarily recalled Dorado Nutrition brand Spermidine Maximum Strength 10 MG per serving and Space Garden (Deep Green GmbH) Spermidin 60 Kapseln 38g due to undeclared wheat. Individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities risk serious allergic reactions if they consume these products.

The recalled Dorado Nutrition product was sold online via Amazon from May 22, 2024, to April 4, 2025. Space Garden's Spermidin was distributed in Germany. The Dorado Nutrition supplement is packaged in white bottles with a blue outlined label, containing 120 capsules. The recall followed product testing that detected wheat not listed on the label. As of the FDA notice, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

en.headline: Recall: Dorado Nutrition and Space Garden Spermidine/Spermidin Supplements Due to Undeclared Wheat
en.description: Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. recalled Dorado Nutrition and Space Garden Spermidine supplements for undeclared wheat. No illnesses reported as of April 2025.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Allergens, Food & Beverages, Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA, Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. has voluntarily recalled Dorado Nutrition brand Spermidine Maximum Strength 10 MG per serving and Space Garden (Deep Green GmbH) Spermidin 60 Kapseln 38g due to undeclared wheat. Individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities risk serious allergic reactions if they consume these products.

The recalled Dorado Nutrition product was sold online via Amazon from May 22, 2024, to April 4, 2025. Space Garden's Spermidin was distributed in Germany. The Dorado Nutrition supplement is packaged in white bottles with a blue outlined label, containing 120 capsules. The recall followed product testing that detected wheat not listed on the label. As of the FDA notice, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

BrandProduct NameFormLot NumberBest By DatePackage SizeDistribution DatesRetail Platforms

No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date, per the FDA recall notice.

content.text: Consumers should check Dorado Nutrition and Space Garden Spermidine/Spermidin supplements for the affected lot numbers and stop using products with undeclared wheat. Contact Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. at 833-810-9896 or SMPQuality@smpnutra.com for further instructions.

title: Blue Ridge Beef Recalls Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix for Salmonella and Listeria Risk
headline: Blue Ridge Beef Recalls Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix Due to Salmonella and Listeria Contamination
description: Blue Ridge Beef recalled specific lots of Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix after Salmonella and Listeria were detected. The FDA notice details affected products and risks.
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 11, 2025, Blue Ridge Beef announced a voluntary recall of certain lots of its Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix due to potential contamination with Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes, as reported in the official FDA notice. The recall affects 2 lb logs of Puppy Mix (Lot #N25 1230) and Kitten Mix (Lot #N26 0114), distributed between January 3 and January 24, 2025, to retail stores in Virginia, Maryland, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, Massachusetts, New York State, Tennessee, Rhode Island, and Wisconsin.

According to the FDA, Salmonella and Listeria can cause illness in both pets and humans. Pets may show symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea (possibly bloody), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, or abdominal pain, and can sometimes carry the bacteria without symptoms. Humans handling contaminated products may experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. More severe complications are possible. The recall was initiated after a customer complaint and testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets confirmed contamination. No legal or medical advice is provided here; refer to the official FDA notice for full details.

ProductSizeLot Number
Blue Ridge Beef Puppy Mix2 lbN25 1230
Blue Ridge Beef Kitten Mix2 lbN26 0114

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using the recalled Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix, check the lot numbers, and follow the FDA notice instructions for safe disposal or return. Contact Blue Ridge Beef if further guidance is needed.

content.text: Consumer support: 704-873-2072 | blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com

title: Marketside Celery Sticks Recalled by Duda Farm Fresh Foods for Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Duda Farm Fresh Foods Recalls Marketside Celery Sticks Over Listeria Risk
description: Duda Farm Fresh Foods has recalled Marketside Celery Sticks sold at Walmart due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the official FDA notice, Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. has voluntarily recalled a specific lot of Marketside Celery Sticks (4 in / 1.6 oz bundle pack) due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. The recall was initiated after the Georgia Department of Agriculture detected Listeria in a sample during routine testing. The affected product was distributed to Walmart stores in multiple states and has an expiration date of March 23, 2025. The product is identified by UPC code 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650, both printed on the packaging.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious or sometimes fatal infections in young children, elderly people, those with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. Healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported as of the FDA notice date.

ProductStoreStates DistributedUPC CodeLot CodeExpiration DatePackage Size
Marketside Celery Sticks 4 in / 1.6 oz Bundle PackWalmartAL, CA, CO, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, IL, IN, KS, KY, MD, MI, MO, MT, NC, NJ, NY, OH, PA, SC, TN, TX, VA, WI, WV, WY6 81131 16151 0P04765003/23/20254 / 1.6-ounce, bag

This recall applies only to the specified celery sticks and not to other Marketside or Duda Farm Fresh Foods products. All affected products are expired and have been removed from store shelves. For more information, see the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Marketside Celery Sticks for UPC 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650. Do not consume affected products; dispose of them if found.

content.text:
Consumer support: foodsafety@duda.com

title: Marketside Celery Sticks Recalled by Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. for Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. Recalls 1,587 Cases of Marketside Celery Sticks Due to Listeria Risk
description: Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. recalled Marketside Celery Sticks sold at Walmart due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
reason: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of 1,587 cases of Marketside Celery Sticks due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. The affected product is a 4-count bundle of 4 in/1.6 oz washed and ready-to-eat celery sticks with a best if used by date of March 23, 2025. These products are past their expiration date and are no longer available for sale in stores, but may still be present in consumers' freezers.

This recall follows random sampling by the Georgia Department of Agriculture, which detected Listeria monocytogenes in a sample from a store in Georgia. Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth if exposed.

No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this product. The recall applies only to the specific Marketside Celery Sticks product listed below and does not affect other Marketside or Duda Farm Fresh Foods products.

title: Heinen's Honey Roasted Peanuts Recalled for Undeclared Cashew Allergen, FDA 2025
headline: Trophy Nut Company Recalls Heinen's Honey Roasted Peanuts Due to Undeclared Cashews
description: Trophy Nut Company recalled Heinen's Honey Roasted Peanuts after a cashew allergen was found undeclared. FDA notice cites one allergic reaction reported.
productType: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
According to the FDA recall notice, Trophy Nut Company of Tipp City, Ohio, is recalling 143 cases of Heinen's 8.25 oz cans of Honey Roasted Peanuts due to undeclared cashews. Individuals with cashew allergies or sensitivities are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The recalled Honey Roasted Peanuts were distributed in Ohio and Illinois through Heinen's Grocery stores from October 24, 2024, to April 4, 2025. The product is sold in 8.25 oz composite cans with a lid, marked with "best if used by" and "best by" dates on the bottom.

The recall was initiated after a consumer complaint identified the presence of cashews. The company determined the issue was caused by a temporary production and packaging error. The FDA notice states that one allergic reaction has been reported in connection with this product.

ProductSizeBest If Used By DateBest By DateUPCDistribution AreaDate Range
Heinen's Honey Roasted Peanuts8.25 oz composite can with lid10-01-202510-03-202520601401877Ohio, Illinois (Heinen's Grocery stores)October 24, 2024 – April 4, 2025

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you purchased Heinen's Honey Roasted Peanuts and have a cashew allergy, do not consume the product. Check the recall details and contact Trophy Nut Company for further information.
